11: ---
12: 
13: # TypeScript Code Quality Checklist
14: 
15: Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.
16: 
17: ---
18: 
19: ## 1. OVERVIEW
20: 
21: ### Purpose
22: 
23: Specific quality checks for TypeScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.
24: 
25: ### Priority Levels
26: 
27: | Level | Meaning       | Enforcement                        |
28: |-------|---------------|------------------------------------|
29: | P0    | HARD BLOCKER  | Must fix before commit             |
30: | P1    | Required      | Must fix or get explicit approval  |
31: | P2    | Recommended   | Can defer with justification       |
32: 
33: ---
34: 
35: ## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS
36: 
37: These items MUST be fixed before any commit.
38: 
39: ### File Header
40: 
41: ```markdown
42: [ ] File has TypeScript module header identifying the module
43: ```
44: 
45: **Required format**:
46: ```typescript
47: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
48: // MODULE: [Module Name]
49: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
50: ```
51: 
52: ### No `any` in Public API
53: 
54: ```markdown
55: [ ] No `any` type used in exported functions, interfaces, or type aliases
56: ```
57: 
58: **Correct**:
59: ```typescript
60: export function parse(input: unknown): SearchConfig { }
61: export interface SearchOptions { metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }
62: ```
63: 
64: **Wrong**:
65: ```typescript
66: export function parse(input: any): any { }
67: export interface SearchOptions { metadata: any; }
68: ```
69: 
70: **Exception**: Interop with untyped third-party modules requires justification comment:
71: ```typescript
72: // Interop: sqlite-vec has no type definitions
73: // eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
74: const sqliteVec = require('sqlite-vec') as any;
75: ```
76: 
77: ### PascalCase Types and Interfaces
78: 
79: ```markdown
80: [ ] All interfaces, type aliases, and enums use PascalCase naming
81: ```
82: 
83: **Correct**:
84: ```typescript
85: interface SearchResult { }
86: type MemoryState = 'active' | 'archived';
87: enum ErrorCode { NotFound, Timeout }
88: ```
89: 
90: **Wrong**:
91: ```typescript
92: interface searchResult { }       // camelCase
93: type memoryState = string;       // camelCase
94: enum errorCode { }               // camelCase
95: ```
96: 
97: ### No Commented-Out Code
98: 
99: ```markdown
100: [ ] No commented-out code blocks present
101: ```
102: 
103: Same rule as JavaScript. Delete unused code; git preserves history.
104: 
105: ### WHY Comments
106: 
107: ```markdown
108: [ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
109:     - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
110:     - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
111: ```
112: 
113: ### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections
114: 
115: ```markdown
116: [ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
117: ```
118: 
119: Same rule as JavaScript. Explain reasoning, not mechanics.
120: 
121: ---
122: 
123: ## 3. P1 - REQUIRED
124: 
125: These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.
126: 
127: ### Explicit Return Types on Public Functions
128: 
129: ```markdown
130: [ ] All exported functions have explicit return type annotations
131: ```
132: 
133: **Correct**:
134: ```typescript
135: export function calculate(input: number): number { }
136: export async function search(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
137: ```
138: 
139: **Wrong**:
140: ```typescript
141: export function calculate(input: number) { }      // inferred return
142: export async function search(query: string) { }    // inferred return
143: ```
144: 
145: Private/internal helper functions may use inferred return types.
146: 
147: ### Interfaces for Data Shapes
148: 
149: ```markdown
150: [ ] Object shapes passed across module boundaries use named interfaces
151: ```
152: 
153: **Correct**:
154: ```typescript
155: interface SearchOptions {
156:   limit: number;
157:   offset: number;
158: }
159: function search(query: string, options: SearchOptions): void { }
160: ```
161: 
162: **Wrong**:
163: ```typescript
164: function search(query: string, options: { limit: number; offset: number }): void { }
165: ```
166: 
167: ### Strict Mode Enabled
168: 
169: ```markdown
170: [ ] tsconfig.json has "strict": true
171: ```
172: 
173: No partial strict mode. All strict flags must be enabled.
174: 
175: ### Module System Matches Package Boundary
176: 
177: ```markdown
178: [ ] TypeScript module settings match the owning workspace/package
179: ```
180: 
181: **Current system-spec-kit baseline**:
182: - `shared/` and `mcp_server/`: package `"type": "module"` plus
183:   `module: "nodenext"` and `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`.
184: - `scripts/`: package `"type": "module"` plus `module: "es2022"` and
185:   `moduleResolution: "node"`.
186: - Root `tsconfig.json`: CommonJS fallback for workspaces that do not override it.
187: 
188: Do not describe CommonJS output as the universal TypeScript baseline.
189: 
190: ### No Non-Null Assertions Without Justification
191: 
192: ```markdown
193: [ ] Non-null assertions (!) have a justification comment on the preceding line
194: ```
195: 
196: **Correct**:
197: ```typescript
198: // Map guaranteed to contain key after initialization phase (line 42)
199: const value = map.get(key)!;
200: ```
201: 
202: **Wrong**:
203: ```typescript
204: const value = map.get(key)!;  // No explanation
205: ```
206: 
207: ### TSDoc on Public API
208: 
209: ```markdown
210: [ ] Exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments
211: ```
212: 
213: **Minimum**:
214: ```typescript
215: /** Search memories matching the given query. */
216: export async function searchMemories(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
217: ```
218: 
219: **Full (for complex functions)**:
220: ```typescript
221: /**
222:  * Search memories matching the given query.
223:  *
224:  * @param query - Search query text
225:  * @param options - Search configuration
226:  * @returns Matching memories sorted by relevance
227:  * @throws {@link MemoryError} If database unavailable
228:  */
229: ```
230: 
231: ### Typed Error Handling
232: 
233: ```markdown
234: [ ] catch blocks use `unknown` type and narrow with instanceof
235: ```
236: 
237: **Correct**:
238: ```typescript
239: try { } catch (error: unknown) {
240:   if (error instanceof MemoryError) { }
241: }
242: ```
243: 
244: **Wrong**:
245: ```typescript
246: try { } catch (error) {        // implicitly any
247:   console.log(error.message);  // unsafe access
248: }
249: ```
250: 
251: ### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks
252: 
253: ```markdown
254: [ ] KISS: remove unnecessary abstraction layers
255: [ ] DRY: duplicate constants/rules are centralized
256: [ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP risks reviewed with evidence
257: ```
258: 
259: ---
260: 
261: ## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED
262: 
263: These improve quality but can be deferred.
264: 
265: ### Utility Types Used Where Appropriate
266: 
267: ```markdown
268: [ ] Standard utility types preferred over manual type construction
269: ```
270: 
271: **Correct**:
272: ```typescript
273: function update(overrides: Partial<Config>): Config { }
274: type Summary = Pick<MemoryRecord, 'id' | 'text'>;
275: ```
276: 
277: **Less optimal**:
278: ```typescript
279: function update(overrides: { timeout?: number; limit?: number }): Config { }
280: // Manual partial instead of Partial<Config>
281: ```
282: 
283: ### Discriminated Unions for Complex State
284: 
285: ```markdown
286: [ ] Multi-state objects use discriminated unions instead of optional properties
287: ```
288: 
289: **Correct**:
290: ```typescript
291: type Result<T> =
292:   | { success: true; data: T }
293:   | { success: false; error: string };
294: ```
295: 
296: **Less optimal**:
297: ```typescript
298: interface Result<T> {
299:   success: boolean;
300:   data?: T;         // unclear when present
301:   error?: string;   // unclear when present
302: }
303: ```
304: 
305: ### Type-Only Imports Separated
306: 
307: ```markdown
308: [ ] Imports used only in type positions use `import type`
309: ```
310: 
311: **Correct**:
312: ```typescript
313: import { MemoryError } from './errors';          // runtime value
314: import type { SearchResult } from '../types';     // type only
315: ```
316: 
317: **Less optimal**:
318: ```typescript
319: import { MemoryError, SearchResult } from './module';
320: // SearchResult only used as type but imported as value
321: ```
322: 
323: ### Generic Constraints Where Applicable
324: 
325: ```markdown
326: [ ] Generic type parameters have constraints when meaningful
327: ```
328: 
329: **Correct**:
330: ```typescript
331: function findById<T extends { id: string }>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
332: ```
333: 
334: **Less optimal**:
335: ```typescript
336: function findById<T>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
337: // No guarantee T has an 'id' property
338: ```
339: 
340: ### Readonly Where Appropriate
341: 
342: ```markdown
343: [ ] Configuration objects and immutable data use readonly modifiers
344: ```
345: 
346: **Correct**:
347: ```typescript
348: interface SearchConfig {
349:   readonly maxResults: number;
350:   readonly timeout: number;
351: }
352: ```
353: 
354: ---
355: 
356: ## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE
357: 
358: For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as TypeScript surface evidence.
359: 
360: ```markdown
361: ## TypeScript Standards Surface Evidence
362: 
363: - [ ] TypeScript standards validated in `typescript_checklist.md`
364: - [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
365: - [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
366: - [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
367: - [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
368: ```
369: 
370: ---
371: 
372: ## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS
373: 
374: ```bash
375: # Type check without emitting files
376: tsc --noEmit
377: 
378: # Build with project references
379: tsc --build
380: 
381: # Check for `any` in public API
382: grep -rn ': any' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v 'node_modules'
383: 
384: # Check for missing return types on exports
385: grep -rn 'export function' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v '): '
386: 
387: # Check for untyped catch blocks
388: grep -rn 'catch (error)' src/ --include='*.ts'
389: ```
390: 
391: ---
392: 
393: ## 7. RELATED RESOURCES
394: 
395: ### Checklists
396: - [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks
397: 
398: ### Style Guide
399: - [TypeScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/typescript/style_guide.md)
400: - [TypeScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/typescript/quality_standards.md)

(End of file - total 400 lines)
</content>

# TypeScript Code Quality Checklist

Quality validation checklist for TypeScript code in the OpenCode development environment.

---

## 1. OVERVIEW

### Purpose

Specific quality checks for TypeScript files. Use alongside the [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) for complete validation.

### Priority Levels

| Level | Meaning       | Enforcement                        |
|-------|---------------|------------------------------------|
| P0    | HARD BLOCKER  | Must fix before commit             |
| P1    | Required      | Must fix or get explicit approval  |
| P2    | Recommended   | Can defer with justification       |

---

## 2. P0 - HARD BLOCKERS

These items MUST be fixed before any commit.

### File Header

```markdown
[ ] File has TypeScript module header identifying the module
```

**Required format**:
```typescript
// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
// MODULE: [Module Name]
// ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
```

### No `any` in Public API

```markdown
[ ] No `any` type used in exported functions, interfaces, or type aliases
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
export function parse(input: unknown): SearchConfig { }
export interface SearchOptions { metadata: Record<string, unknown>; }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
export function parse(input: any): any { }
export interface SearchOptions { metadata: any; }
```

**Exception**: Interop with untyped third-party modules requires justification comment:
```typescript
// Interop: sqlite-vec has no type definitions
// eslint-disable-next-line @typescript-eslint/no-explicit-any
const sqliteVec = require('sqlite-vec') as any;
```

### PascalCase Types and Interfaces

```markdown
[ ] All interfaces, type aliases, and enums use PascalCase naming
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchResult { }
type MemoryState = 'active' | 'archived';
enum ErrorCode { NotFound, Timeout }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
interface searchResult { }       // camelCase
type memoryState = string;       // camelCase
enum errorCode { }               // camelCase
```

### No Commented-Out Code

```markdown
[ ] No commented-out code blocks present
```

Same rule as JavaScript. Delete unused code; git preserves history.

### WHY Comments

```markdown
[ ] Purposeful WHY comments only (max 3 comments per 10 LOC)
    - Comments explain WHY, not WHAT
    - No narrative comments describing obvious mechanics
```

### Numbered ALL-CAPS Sections

```markdown
[ ] Numbered ALL-CAPS section headers are preserved (`## N. SECTION NAME`)
```

Same rule as JavaScript. Explain reasoning, not mechanics.

---

## 3. P1 - REQUIRED

These must be addressed or receive approval to defer.

### Explicit Return Types on Public Functions

```markdown
[ ] All exported functions have explicit return type annotations
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
export function calculate(input: number): number { }
export async function search(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
export function calculate(input: number) { }      // inferred return
export async function search(query: string) { }    // inferred return
```

Private/internal helper functions may use inferred return types.

### Interfaces for Data Shapes

```markdown
[ ] Object shapes passed across module boundaries use named interfaces
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchOptions {
  limit: number;
  offset: number;
}
function search(query: string, options: SearchOptions): void { }
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
function search(query: string, options: { limit: number; offset: number }): void { }
```

### Strict Mode Enabled

```markdown
[ ] tsconfig.json has "strict": true
```

No partial strict mode. All strict flags must be enabled.

### Module System Matches Package Boundary

```markdown
[ ] TypeScript module settings match the owning workspace/package
```

**Current system-spec-kit baseline**:
- `shared/` and `mcp_server/`: package `"type": "module"` plus
  `module: "nodenext"` and `moduleResolution: "nodenext"`.
- `scripts/`: package `"type": "module"` plus `module: "es2022"` and
  `moduleResolution: "node"`.
- Root `tsconfig.json`: CommonJS fallback for workspaces that do not override it.

Do not describe CommonJS output as the universal TypeScript baseline.

### No Non-Null Assertions Without Justification

```markdown
[ ] Non-null assertions (!) have a justification comment on the preceding line
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
// Map guaranteed to contain key after initialization phase (line 42)
const value = map.get(key)!;
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
const value = map.get(key)!;  // No explanation
```

### TSDoc on Public API

```markdown
[ ] Exported functions, classes, and interfaces have TSDoc comments
```

**Minimum**:
```typescript
/** Search memories matching the given query. */
export async function searchMemories(query: string): Promise<SearchResult[]> { }
```

**Full (for complex functions)**:
```typescript
/**
 * Search memories matching the given query.
 *
 * @param query - Search query text
 * @param options - Search configuration
 * @returns Matching memories sorted by relevance
 * @throws {@link MemoryError} If database unavailable
 */
```

### Typed Error Handling

```markdown
[ ] catch blocks use `unknown` type and narrow with instanceof
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
try { } catch (error: unknown) {
  if (error instanceof MemoryError) { }
}
```

**Wrong**:
```typescript
try { } catch (error) {        // implicitly any
  console.log(error.message);  // unsafe access
}
```

### KISS / DRY / SOLID Checks

```markdown
[ ] KISS: remove unnecessary abstraction layers
[ ] DRY: duplicate constants/rules are centralized
[ ] SOLID: SRP/OCP/LSP/ISP/DIP risks reviewed with evidence
```

---

## 4. P2 - RECOMMENDED

These improve quality but can be deferred.

### Utility Types Used Where Appropriate

```markdown
[ ] Standard utility types preferred over manual type construction
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
function update(overrides: Partial<Config>): Config { }
type Summary = Pick<MemoryRecord, 'id' | 'text'>;
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
function update(overrides: { timeout?: number; limit?: number }): Config { }
// Manual partial instead of Partial<Config>
```

### Discriminated Unions for Complex State

```markdown
[ ] Multi-state objects use discriminated unions instead of optional properties
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
type Result<T> =
  | { success: true; data: T }
  | { success: false; error: string };
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
interface Result<T> {
  success: boolean;
  data?: T;         // unclear when present
  error?: string;   // unclear when present
}
```

### Type-Only Imports Separated

```markdown
[ ] Imports used only in type positions use `import type`
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
import { MemoryError } from './errors';          // runtime value
import type { SearchResult } from '../types';     // type only
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
import { MemoryError, SearchResult } from './module';
// SearchResult only used as type but imported as value
```

### Generic Constraints Where Applicable

```markdown
[ ] Generic type parameters have constraints when meaningful
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
function findById<T extends { id: string }>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
```

**Less optimal**:
```typescript
function findById<T>(items: T[], id: string): T | undefined { }
// No guarantee T has an 'id' property
```

### Readonly Where Appropriate

```markdown
[ ] Configuration objects and immutable data use readonly modifiers
```

**Correct**:
```typescript
interface SearchConfig {
  readonly maxResults: number;
  readonly timeout: number;
}
```

---

## 5. CHECKLIST TEMPLATE

For formal findings-first review output, use `sk-code-review` as the baseline and treat this file as TypeScript surface evidence.

```markdown
## TypeScript Standards Surface Evidence

- [ ] TypeScript standards validated in `typescript_checklist.md`
- [ ] Universal standards validated in `universal_checklist.md`
- [ ] Findings severity/order produced with `sk-code-review/references/quick_reference.md`
- [ ] Baseline security, quality, and test checks sourced from `sk-code-review`
- [ ] Surface-specific deviations documented with file:line evidence
```

---

## 6. VALIDATION COMMANDS

```bash
# Type check without emitting files
tsc --noEmit

# Build with project references
tsc --build

# Check for `any` in public API
grep -rn ': any' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v 'node_modules'

# Check for missing return types on exports
grep -rn 'export function' src/ --include='*.ts' | grep -v '): '

# Check for untyped catch blocks
grep -rn 'catch (error)' src/ --include='*.ts'
```

---

## 7. RELATED RESOURCES

### Checklists
- [universal_checklist.md](./universal_checklist.md) - Language-agnostic checks

### Style Guide
- [TypeScript Style Guide](../../../references/opencode/typescript/style_guide.md)
- [TypeScript Quality Standards](../../../references/opencode/typescript/quality_standards.md)

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Hybrid Search
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Combines vector, FTS, and BM25 search with fallback
5: 
6: // 1. IMPORTS
7: 
8: // Local
9: import {
10:   getIndex,
11:   isBm25Enabled,
12:   shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine,
13: } from './bm25-index.js';
14: import { fuseResultsMulti } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/rrf-fusion';
15: import { adaptiveFuse, getAdaptiveWeights, isAdaptiveFusionEnabled } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/adaptive-fusion';
16: import { CO_ACTIVATION_CONFIG, spreadActivation } from '../cognitive/co-activation.js';
17: import { applyMMR } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/mmr-reranker';
18: import { INTENT_LAMBDA_MAP, classifyIntent } from './intent-classifier.js';
19: import { fts5Bm25Search } from './sqlite-fts.js';
20: import { DEGREE_CHANNEL_WEIGHT } from './graph-search-fn.js';
21: import {
22:   isMMREnabled,
23:   isSearchFallbackEnabled,
24:   isDocscoreAggregationEnabled,
25:   isDegreeBoostEnabled,
26:   isContextHeadersEnabled,
27:   isArchivedRetrievalIncludedByDefault,
28:   isCosineTopnReorderEnabled,
29: } from './search-flags.js';
30: import { resolveAbsoluteRelevance, type PipelineRow } from './pipeline/types.js';
31: import { computeDegreeScores } from './graph-search-fn.js';
32: import type { GraphSearchFn } from './search-types.js';
33: 
34: // Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
35: 
36: export type { GraphSearchFn } from './search-types.js';
37: 
38: import { routeQuery } from './query-router.js';
39: import { isComplexityRouterEnabled } from './query-classifier.js';
40: import { enforceChannelRepresentation } from './channel-enforcement.js';
41: import {
42:   truncateByConfidence,
43:   isConfidenceTruncationEnabled,
44:   DEFAULT_MIN_RESULTS,
45:   GAP_THRESHOLD_MULTIPLIER,
46: } from './confidence-truncation.js';
47: import {
48:   getDynamicTokenBudget,
49:   isDynamicTokenBudgetEnabled,
50:   DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CONFIG,
51: } from './dynamic-token-budget.js';
52: import {
53:   buildProgressiveResponse,
54:   DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE,
55: } from './progressive-disclosure.js';
56: import type {
57:   ProgressiveResponse,
58:   DisclosureResult,
59: } from './progressive-disclosure.js';
60: import { resolveFusionIntentContract } from './search-utils.js';
61: import { ensureDescriptionCache, getSpecsBasePaths } from './folder-discovery.js';
62: import {
63:   isFolderScoringEnabled,
64:   lookupFolders,
65:   computeFolderRelevanceScores,
66:   enrichResultsWithFolderScores,
67:   twoPhaseRetrieval,
68: } from './folder-relevance.js';
69: import { parse_trigger_phrases, specFolderLikePattern } from './vector-index-types.js';
70: 
71: import { collapseAndReassembleChunkResults } from '../scoring/mpab-aggregation.js';
72: 
73: // Type-only
74: import type Database from 'better-sqlite3';
75: import type { SpreadResult } from '../cognitive/co-activation.js';
76: import type { MMRCandidate } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/mmr-reranker';
77: import type { FusionResult } from '@spec-kit/shared/algorithms/rrf-fusion';
78: import type { ChannelName } from './query-router.js';
79: import type { EnforcementResult } from './channel-enforcement.js';
80: import type { TruncationResult } from './confidence-truncation.js';
81: 
82: // 2. INTERFACES
83: 
84: type VectorSearchFn = (
85:   embedding: Float32Array | number[],
86:   options: Record<string, unknown>
87: ) => Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
88: 
89: interface HybridSearchOptions {
90:   limit?: number;
91:   specFolder?: string;
92:   minSimilarity?: number;
93:   useBm25?: boolean;
94:   useFts?: boolean;
95:   useVector?: boolean;
96:   useGraph?: boolean;
97:   includeArchived?: boolean;
98:   includeContent?: boolean;
99:   /**
100:    * Evaluation-only mode.
101:    * When true, preserve the requested top-K window by bypassing confidence
102:    * truncation and token-budget truncation without changing live defaults.
103:    */
104:   evaluationMode?: boolean;
105:   /** Classified query intent for adaptive fusion weight selection (e.g. 'understand', 'fix_bug'). */
106:   intent?: string;
107:   /** Optional trigger phrases for query-classifier trigger-match routing path. */
108:   triggerPhrases?: string[];
109:   /**
110:    * Internal fallback override: when true, bypass complexity routing and
111:    * enable all retrieval channels for this search call.
112:    */
113:   forceAllChannels?: boolean;
114:   /**
115:    * Internal raw-candidate mode used by the Stage 1 pipeline.
116:    * When true, stop after channel collection and return pre-fusion candidates.
117:    */
118:   skipFusion?: boolean;
119:   /**
120:    * Internal pipeline handoff mode.
121:    * When true, return immediately after adaptive/RRF fusion so Stage 2/3 can
122:    * apply the remaining pipeline scoring and aggregation steps.
123:    */
124:   stopAfterFusion?: boolean;
125: }
126: 
127: interface HybridSearchResult {
128:   id: number | string;
129:   /**
130:    * Normalized relevance score (0-1). Semantics depend on `source`:
131:    * - `'vector'` — cosine similarity from sqlite-vec (normalized from 0-100 to 0-1)
132:    * - `'bm25'` — BM25 term-frequency relevance (min-max normalized per source group)
133:    * - `'fts'` — FTS5 rank score (absolute value, min-max normalized per source group)
134:    * - `'graph'` — graph traversal relevance
135:    *
136:    * After hybrid merge, all source scores are min-max normalized to 0-1 within
137:    * their source group to ensure fair cross-method comparison.
138:    */
139:   score: number;
140:   source: string;
141:   title?: string;
142:   [key: string]: unknown;
143: }
144: 
145: type HardExclusionSource = 'archived-tier' | 'deprecated-tier';
146: type HardExclusionClassification = 'intended' | 'silent-risk' | 'unclassified';
147: 
148: interface HardExclusionPredicate {
149:   id: string;
150:   source: HardExclusionSource;
151:   channel: string;
152:   predicate: string;
153:   defaultApplied: boolean;
154:   classification: HardExclusionClassification;
155: }
156: 
157: const HARD_EXCLUSION_PREDICATES: readonly HardExclusionPredicate[] = Object.freeze([
158:   {
159:     id: 'includeArchived-default',
160:     source: 'archived-tier',
161:     channel: 'vector/fts',
162:     predicate: 'includeArchived defaults to false',
163:     defaultApplied: true,
164:     classification: 'intended',
165:   },
166:   {
167:     id: 'fts-deprecated-tier',
168:     source: 'deprecated-tier',
169:     channel: 'fts',
170:     predicate: "m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated'",
171:     defaultApplied: true,
172:     classification: 'silent-risk',
173:   },
174:   {
175:     id: 'bm25-deprecated-tier',
176:     source: 'deprecated-tier',
177:     channel: 'bm25',
178:     predicate: "importance_tier != 'deprecated'",
179:     defaultApplied: true,
180:     classification: 'silent-risk',
181:   },
182:   {
183:     id: 'trigger-deprecated-tier',
184:     source: 'deprecated-tier',
185:     channel: 'trigger',
186:     predicate: "m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated'",
187:     defaultApplied: true,
188:     classification: 'silent-risk',
189:   },
190:   {
191:     id: 'structural-deprecated-archived-tier',
192:     source: 'deprecated-tier',
193:     channel: 'structural',
194:     predicate: "importance_tier IS NULL OR importance_tier NOT IN ('deprecated', 'archived')",
195:     defaultApplied: true,
196:     classification: 'silent-risk',
197:   },
198: ]);
199: 
200: /** Return recall-path exclusion metadata without executing or changing search. */
201: function getHardExclusionPredicates(): HardExclusionPredicate[] {
202:   return HARD_EXCLUSION_PREDICATES.map((predicate) => ({ ...predicate }));
203: }
204: 
205: type Bm25MemoryMetadata = {
206:   specFolder: string | null;
207:   importanceTier: string | null;
208: };
209: 
210: /** Non-enumerable shadow metadata attached to result arrays via Object.defineProperty. */
211: interface ShadowMetaArray {
212:   _s4shadow?: unknown;
213:   _s4attribution?: unknown;
214:   _degradation?: unknown;
215: }
216: 
217: /** Normalize a fused RRF result to the HybridSearchResult contract. */
218: function toHybridResult(result: FusionResult): HybridSearchResult {
219:   const sourceCandidate = (result as { source?: unknown }).source;
220:   const primarySource = result.sources[0] ?? 'hybrid';
221:   const scoreCandidate = (result as { score?: unknown }).score;
222: 
223:   return {
224:     ...result,
225:     id: result.id,
226:     score: typeof scoreCandidate === 'number' ? scoreCandidate : result.rrfScore,
227:     source: typeof sourceCandidate === 'string' ? sourceCandidate : primarySource,
228:   };
229: }
230: 
231: // PIPELINE METADATA
232: 
233: /**
234:  * Optional metadata about pipeline stages attached to enhanced search results.
235:  * Only populated when the corresponding feature flags are enabled.
236:  */
237: interface Sprint3PipelineMeta {
238:   /** Query complexity routing result (SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER). */
239:   routing?: {
240:     tier: string;
241:     channels: string[];
242:     skippedChannels: string[];
243:     featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
244:     confidence: string;
245:     features: Record<string, unknown>;
246:   };
247:   /** Channel enforcement result (SPECKIT_CHANNEL_MIN_REP). */
248:   enforcement?: { applied: boolean; promotedCount: number; underRepresentedChannels: string[] };
249:   /** Confidence truncation result (SPECKIT_CONFIDENCE_TRUNCATION). */
250:   truncation?: {
251:     truncated: boolean;
252:     originalCount: number;
253:     truncatedCount: number;
254:     medianGap: number;
255:     cutoffGap: number;
256:     cutoffIndex: number;
257:     thresholdMultiplier: number;
258:     minResultsGuaranteed: number;
259:     featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
260:   };
261:   /** Dynamic token budget result (SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_TOKEN_BUDGET). */
262:   tokenBudget?: {
263:     tier: string;
264:     budget: number;
265:     applied: boolean;
266:     featureFlagEnabled: boolean;
267:     configValues: Record<string, number>;
268:     headerOverhead: number;
269:     adjustedBudget: number;
270:   };
271: }
272: 
273: // 4. PI-A2: DEGRADATION TYPES
274: 
275: /** Fallback tier in the 3-tier degradation chain. */
276: type FallbackTier = 1 | 2 | 3;
277: 
278: /** Why degradation was triggered at a given tier. */
279: interface DegradationTrigger {
280:   reason: 'low_quality' | 'insufficient_results' | 'both';
281:   topScore: number;
282:   resultCount: number;
283:   relativeGap?: number;
284: }
285: 
286: /** Record of a single degradation event during tiered fallback. */
287: interface DegradationEvent {
288:   tier: FallbackTier;
289:   trigger: DegradationTrigger;
290:   resultCountBefore: number;
291:   resultCountAfter: number;
292: }
293: 
294: /**
295:  * Absolute quality floor for degradation checks.
296:  *
297:  * Raw RRF scores are typically small decimals (often <0.05), so a
298:  * high fixed threshold causes false degradations. Use a conservative floor and
299:  * pair it with a relative-gap check to avoid score-scale coupling.
300:  */
301: const DEGRADATION_QUALITY_THRESHOLD = 0.02;
302: 
303: /** Minimum relative separation between top-1 and top-2 scores. */
304: const DEGRADATION_MIN_RELATIVE_GAP = 0.2;
305: 
306: /** Minimum result count: must have >= this many results to stay at current tier. */
307: const DEGRADATION_MIN_RESULTS = 3;
308: 
309: // 5. MODULE STATE
310: 
311: /** Default result limit when none is specified by the caller. */
312: const DEFAULT_LIMIT = 20;
313: /** Primary vector similarity floor for hybrid fallback passes (percentage units). */
314: const PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 30;
315: /** Secondary vector similarity floor for adaptive retry passes (percentage units). */
316: const SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 17;
317: /** Tier-2 vector similarity floor for quality-aware fallback (percentage units). */
318: const TIERED_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY = 10;
319: 
320: /** Minimum MMR candidates required for diversity reranking to be worthwhile. */
321: const MMR_MIN_CANDIDATES = 2;
322: 
323: /** Fallback lambda (diversity vs relevance) when intent is not in INTENT_LAMBDA_MAP. */
324: const MMR_DEFAULT_LAMBDA = 0.7;
325: 
326: /** Number of top results used as seeds for co-activation spreading. */
327: const SPREAD_ACTIVATION_TOP_N = 5;
328: 
329: /** Maximum contextual tree header length prepended to content (including brackets). */
330: const CONTEXT_HEADER_MAX_CHARS = 100;
331: /** Header/content separator characters added during contextual tree injection. */
332: const CONTEXT_HEADER_SEPARATOR_CHARS = 1;
333: /** Reserved token overhead per contextual header, calibrated to max header length. */
334: const CONTEXT_HEADER_TOKEN_OVERHEAD = Math.ceil(
335:   (CONTEXT_HEADER_MAX_CHARS + CONTEXT_HEADER_SEPARATOR_CHARS) / 4
336: );
337: 
338: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
339: let vectorSearchFn: VectorSearchFn | null = null;
340: let graphSearchFn: GraphSearchFn | null = null;
341: let enrichFusedResultsObserver: (() => void) | null = null;
342: 
343: // 6. GRAPH CHANNEL METRICS
344: 
345: interface GraphChannelMetrics {
346:   totalQueries: number;
347:   graphHits: number;
348:   graphResultCount: number;
349:   graphOnlyResults: number;
350:   graphMultiSourceResults: number;
351:   multiSourceResults: number;
352:   degreeQueries: number;
353:   degreeHits: number;
354:   degreeResultCount: number;
355: }
356: 
357: const graphMetrics: GraphChannelMetrics = {
358:   totalQueries: 0,
359:   graphHits: 0,
360:   graphResultCount: 0,
361:   graphOnlyResults: 0,
362:   graphMultiSourceResults: 0,
363:   multiSourceResults: 0,
364:   degreeQueries: 0,
365:   degreeHits: 0,
366:   degreeResultCount: 0,
367: };
368: 
369: /**
370:  * Return current graph channel metrics for health check reporting.
371:  * graphHitRate is computed as graphHits / totalQueries.
372:  */
373: function getGraphMetrics(): GraphChannelMetrics & { graphHitRate: number; degreeHitRate: number } {
374:   return {
375:     ...graphMetrics,
376:     graphHitRate: graphMetrics.totalQueries > 0
377:       ? graphMetrics.graphHits / graphMetrics.totalQueries
378:       : 0,
379:     degreeHitRate: graphMetrics.degreeQueries > 0
380:       ? graphMetrics.degreeHits / graphMetrics.degreeQueries
381:       : 0,
382:   };
383: }
384: 
385: /** Reset all graph channel metrics counters to zero. */
386: function resetGraphMetrics(): void {
387:   graphMetrics.totalQueries = 0;
388:   graphMetrics.graphHits = 0;
389:   graphMetrics.graphResultCount = 0;
390:   graphMetrics.graphOnlyResults = 0;
391:   graphMetrics.graphMultiSourceResults = 0;
392:   graphMetrics.multiSourceResults = 0;
393:   graphMetrics.degreeQueries = 0;
394:   graphMetrics.degreeHits = 0;
395:   graphMetrics.degreeResultCount = 0;
396: }
397: 
398: // 7. INITIALIZATION
399: 
400: /**
401:  * Initialize hybrid search with database, vector search, and optional graph search dependencies.
402:  * @param database - The better-sqlite3 database instance for FTS and graph queries.
403:  * @param vectorFn - Optional vector search function for semantic similarity.
404:  * @param graphFn - Optional graph search function for causal/structural retrieval.
405:  */
406: function init(
407:   database: Database.Database,
408:   vectorFn: VectorSearchFn | null = null,
409:   graphFn: GraphSearchFn | null = null
410: ): void {
411:   db = database;
412:   vectorSearchFn = vectorFn;
413:   graphSearchFn = graphFn;
414: }
415: 
416: // 8. BM25 SEARCH
417: 
418: /**
419:  * Search the BM25 index with optional spec folder filtering.
420:  * @param query - The search query string.
421:  * @param options - Optional limit and specFolder filter.
422:  * @returns Array of BM25-scored results tagged with source 'bm25'.
423:  */
424: function bm25Search(
425:   query: string,
426:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string } = {}
427: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
428:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
429:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] BM25 not enabled — returning empty bm25Search results');
430:     return [];
431:   }
432: 
433:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder } = options;
434: 
435:   try {
436:     if (shouldUseSqliteLexicalEngine(db)) {
437:       return ftsSearch(query, {
438:         limit,
439:         specFolder,
440:       }).map((result) => ({
441:         ...result,
442:         bm25_score: result.score,
443:         source: 'bm25',
444:         sources: Array.from(new Set([
445:           ...(
446:             Array.isArray((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources)
447:               ? ((result as Record<string, unknown>).sources as unknown[]).filter((source): source is string => typeof source === 'string')
448:               : []
449:           ),
450:           'fts',
451:           'bm25',
452:         ])),
453:       }));
454:     }
455: 
456:     // Spec-folder scope can only be resolved from DB metadata; fail closed before
457:     // searching when the DB is unavailable rather than over-fetching the whole
458:     // corpus and then discarding it.
459:     if (specFolder && !db) {
460:       console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope requested without a database; returning empty scoped results');
461:       return [];
462:     }
463: 
464:     const index = getIndex();
465:     const candidateLimit = (specFolder || db)
466:       ? index.getStats().documentCount
467:       : limit;
468:     // Metadata filters run after in-memory scoring, so fetch the corpus-bounded
469:     // candidate set whenever those filters can remove otherwise top-ranked hits.
470:     const results = index.search(query, candidateLimit);
471: 
472:     // BM25 document IDs are stringified
473:     // Numeric memory IDs (e.g., "42"), not spec folder paths. The old filter compared
474:     // R.id against specFolder which never matched. Use DB lookup to resolve spec_folder.
475: 
476:     // Batch-resolve metadata for all result IDs (was N+1 individual queries)
477:     // SECURITY: Spec-folder scope MUST fail closed — any error in scope
478:     // resolution returns [] rather than leaking unscoped BM25 candidates.
479:     let memoryMetadataMap: Map<number, Bm25MemoryMetadata> | null = null;
480:     if (results.length > 0) {
481:       if (!db) {
482:         if (specFolder) {
483:           const error = new Error('Database unavailable for spec-folder scope lookup');
484:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
485:           return [];
486:         }
487:       } else {
488:         try {
489:           const ids = results.map((r: { id: string }) => Number(r.id));
490:           memoryMetadataMap = new Map();
491:           // Resolve metadata in bounded batches. The candidate set can now be
492:           // corpus-sized (over-fetch for scope/tier filtering), and a single
493:           // IN (...) with one bind per id would exceed SQLite's parameter limit
494:           // and throw — which the catch below turns into an empty result.
495:           const METADATA_BATCH = 500;
496:           for (let offset = 0; offset < ids.length; offset += METADATA_BATCH) {
497:             const batch = ids.slice(offset, offset + METADATA_BATCH);
498:             const placeholders = batch.map(() => '?').join(',');
499:             const rows = db.prepare(
500:               `SELECT id, spec_folder, importance_tier FROM memory_index WHERE id IN (${placeholders})`
501:             ).all(...batch) as Array<{ id: number; spec_folder: string | null; importance_tier: string | null }>;
502:             for (const row of rows) {
503:               memoryMetadataMap.set(row.id, {
504:                 specFolder: row.spec_folder,
505:                 importanceTier: row.importance_tier,
506:               });
507:             }
508:           }
509:         } catch (error: unknown) {
510:           console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
511:           return [];
512:         }
513:       }
514: 
515:       // DEFENSE-IN-DEPTH: If specFolder was requested but metadata resolution
516:       // is still null after the resolution block, something unexpected happened.
517:       // Fail closed rather than falling through to unscoped results.
518:       if (specFolder && !memoryMetadataMap) {
519:         const error = new Error('memoryMetadataMap unexpectedly null after scope resolution');
520:         console.warn('[BM25] Spec-folder scope lookup failed, returning empty scoped results:', error);
521:         return [];
522:       }
523:     }
524: 
525:     const excludeCold = !isArchivedRetrievalIncludedByDefault();
526:     return results
527:       .filter((r: { id: string }) => {
528:         const metadata = memoryMetadataMap?.get(Number(r.id));
529:         // Cold/deprecated rows are included by default (FSRS ranks them lower);
530:         // only drop them when archived retrieval is disabled.
531:         if (excludeCold && metadata?.importanceTier === 'deprecated') return false;
532:         if (!specFolder) return true;
533:         if (!metadata) return false;
534:         const folder = metadata.specFolder;
535:         if (!folder) return false;
536:         return folder === specFolder || folder.startsWith(specFolder + '/');
537:       })
538:       .slice(0, limit)
539:       .map((r: { id: string; score: number }) => ({
540:         ...r,
541:         source: 'bm25',
542:       }));
543:   } catch (error: unknown) {
544:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
545:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] BM25 search failed: ${msg}`);
546:     return [];
547:   }
548: }
549: 
550: /**
551:  * Check whether the BM25 index is populated and available for search.
552:  * @returns True if the BM25 index exists and contains at least one document.
553:  */
554: function isBm25Available(): boolean {
555:   if (!isBm25Enabled()) {
556:     return false;
557:   }
558: 
559:   try {
560:     const index = getIndex();
561:     return index.getStats().documentCount > 0;
562:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
563:     // Swallow index-not-initialized errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
564:     return false;
565:   }
566: }
567: 
568: // 9. FTS SEARCH
569: 
570: /**
571:  * Check whether the FTS5 full-text search table exists in the database.
572:  * @returns True if the memory_fts table exists in the connected database.
573:  */
574: function isFtsAvailable(): boolean {
575:   if (!db) {
576:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized — isFtsAvailable returning false');
577:     return false;
578:   }
579: 
580:   try {
581:     const result = (db.prepare(`
582:       SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_fts'
583:     `) as Database.Statement).get() as { name: string } | undefined;
584:     return !!result;
585:   } catch (_err: unknown) {
586:     // Swallow DB errors; caller treats absence as unavailable
587:     return false;
588:   }
589: }
590: 
591: /**
592:  * Run FTS5 full-text search with weighted BM25 scoring and optional spec folder filtering.
593:  * @param query - The search query string.
594:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
595:  * @returns Array of FTS-scored results tagged with source 'fts'.
596:  */
597: function ftsSearch(
598:   query: string,
599:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
600: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
601:   if (!db || !isFtsAvailable()) {
602:     console.warn('[hybrid-search] db not initialized or FTS unavailable — returning empty ftsSearch results');
603:     return [];
604:   }
605: 
606:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder, includeArchived = false } = options;
607: 
608:   try {
609:     // Delegate to weighted BM25 FTS5 search from sqlite-fts.ts
610:     // Uses bm25(memory_fts, 10.0, 5.0, 2.0, 1.0) for per-column weighting
611:     // (title 10x, trigger_phrases 5x, file_path 2x, content 1x)
612:     // Filters: deprecated-tier exclusion and spec-folder matching handled by fts5Bm25Search
613:     const bm25Results = fts5Bm25Search(db, query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
614: 
615:     return bm25Results.map(row => ({
616:       ...row,
617:       id: row.id as number,
618:       score: row.fts_score || 0,
619:       source: 'fts',
620:     }));
621:   } catch (error: unknown) {
622:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
623:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] FTS search failed: ${msg}`);
624:     return [];
625:   }
626: }
627: 
628: // 10. COMBINED LEXICAL SEARCH
629: 
630: /**
631:  * Merge FTS and BM25 search results, deduplicating by ID and preferring FTS scores.
632:  * @param query - The search query string.
633:  * @param options - Optional limit, specFolder filter, and includeArchived flag.
634:  * @returns Deduplicated array of merged results sorted by score descending.
635:  */
636: function combinedLexicalSearch(
637:   query: string,
638:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
639: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
640:   const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, options);
641:   const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, options);
642: 
643:   // Merge by canonical ID, prefer FTS scores.
644:   // CanonicalResultId() prevents duplicate rows when one channel emits
645:   // Numeric IDs (42) and another emits string IDs ("42" or "mem:42").
646:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
647: 
648:   for (const r of ftsResults) {
649:     merged.set(canonicalResultId(r.id), r);
650:   }
651: 
652:   for (const r of bm25Results) {
653:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
654:     if (!merged.has(canonicalId)) {
655:       merged.set(canonicalId, r);
656:     }
657:   }
658: 
659:   return Array.from(merged.values())
660:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
661:     .slice(0, options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT);
662: }
663: 
664: function normalizeTriggerText(value: string): string {
665:   return value
666:     .toLowerCase()
667:     .replace(/[^a-z0-9]+/g, ' ')
668:     .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
669:     .trim();
670: }
671: 
672: function triggerQueryTokens(query: string): string[] {
673:   return Array.from(new Set(
674:     normalizeTriggerText(query)
675:       .split(/\s+/)
676:       .filter((token) => token.length >= 2)
677:   ));
678: }
679: 
680: function computeTriggerMatchScore(query: string, phrase: string): number {
681:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
682:   const normalizedPhrase = normalizeTriggerText(phrase);
683:   if (!normalizedQuery || !normalizedPhrase) return 0;
684:   if (normalizedPhrase === normalizedQuery) return 1.0;
685: 
686:   const queryTokens = triggerQueryTokens(normalizedQuery);
687:   const phraseTokens = new Set(triggerQueryTokens(normalizedPhrase));
688:   if (phraseTokens.size < 2) return 0;
689:   if (normalizedPhrase.includes(normalizedQuery)) return 0.94;
690:   if (normalizedQuery.includes(normalizedPhrase)) return 0.88;
691:   if (queryTokens.length === 0) return 0;
692:   const overlap = queryTokens.filter((token) => phraseTokens.has(token)).length;
693:   const coverage = overlap / queryTokens.length;
694:   return coverage >= 0.8 ? coverage * 0.75 : 0;
695: }
696: 
697: function timestampBoost(value: unknown): number {
698:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.length === 0) return 0;
699:   const parsed = Date.parse(value);
700:   if (!Number.isFinite(parsed)) return 0;
701:   const ageDays = Math.max(0, (Date.now() - parsed) / 86_400_000);
702:   return 1 / (1 + ageDays / 30);
703: }
704: 
705: function exactTriggerSearch(
706:   query: string,
707:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string; includeArchived?: boolean } = {}
708: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
709:   if (!db) return [];
710: 
711:   const tokens = triggerQueryTokens(query).filter((token) => token.length >= 3).slice(0, 8);
712:   if (tokens.length === 0) return [];
713: 
714:   const { limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT, specFolder } = options;
715:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeTriggerText(query);
716:   const exactPhraseLike = `%${normalizedQuery}%`;
717:   const candidateLimit = Math.max(200, Math.min(1000, limit * 100));
718:   const conditions = [
719:     `m.trigger_phrases IS NOT NULL`,
720:     `m.trigger_phrases != ''`,
721:     `m.trigger_phrases != '[]'`,
722:     // Cold/deprecated-tier rows are included by default (FSRS retrievability ranks
723:     // them below hot memories); the hard exclusion only applies when archived
724:     // retrieval is disabled.
725:     ...(isArchivedRetrievalIncludedByDefault()
726:       ? []
727:       : [`(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != 'deprecated')`]),
728:     `(m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime('now'))`,
729:     `(${tokens.map(() => 'LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ?').join(' OR ')})`,
730:   ];
731:   const params: unknown[] = tokens.map((token) => `%${token}%`);
732: 
733:   if (specFolder) {
734:     conditions.push(`(m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')`);
735:     params.push(specFolder, specFolderLikePattern(specFolder));
736:   }
737: 
738:   try {
739:     const rows = db.prepare(`
740:       SELECT m.*
741:       FROM memory_index m
742:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
743:       WHERE ${conditions.join(' AND ')}
744:       ORDER BY
745:         CASE WHEN LOWER(m.trigger_phrases) LIKE ? THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
746:         COALESCE(m.updated_at, m.created_at, '') DESC
747:       LIMIT ${candidateLimit}
748:     `).all(...params, exactPhraseLike) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
749: 
750:     return rows
751:       .map((row): HybridSearchResult | null => {
752:         const phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases as string | string[] | null | undefined);
753:         const matchScore = Math.max(0, ...phrases.map((phrase) => computeTriggerMatchScore(query, phrase)));
754:         if (matchScore <= 0) return null;
755: 
756:         const importance = typeof row.importance_weight === 'number' && Number.isFinite(row.importance_weight)
757:           ? Math.max(0, Math.min(1, row.importance_weight))
758:           : 0.5;
759:         const recency = timestampBoost(row.updated_at ?? row.created_at);
760:         const score = Math.min(1, matchScore + importance * 0.03 + recency * 0.04);
761: 
762:         return {
763:           ...row,
764:           id: row.id as number,
765:           score,
766:           triggerScore: matchScore,
767:           source: 'trigger',
768:         };
769:       })
770:       .filter((row): row is HybridSearchResult => row !== null)
771:       .sort((a, b) => {
772:         const scoreDelta = b.score - a.score;
773:         if (scoreDelta !== 0) return scoreDelta;
774:         const bTime = Date.parse(String(b.updated_at ?? b.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
775:         const aTime = Date.parse(String(a.updated_at ?? a.created_at ?? '')) || 0;
776:         return bTime - aTime;
777:       })
778:       .slice(0, limit);
779:   } catch (error: unknown) {
780:     const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
781:     console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Trigger phrase search failed: ${msg}`);
782:     return [];
783:   }
784: }
785: 
786: type RawChannelList = {
787:   source: string;
788:   results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
789: };
790: 
791: function resolveRawCandidateScore(result: { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown }): number {
792:   if (typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)) {
793:     return result.score;
794:   }
795:   if (typeof result.similarity === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.similarity)) {
796:     return result.similarity / 100;
797:   }
798:   return 0;
799: }
800: 
801: function getCandidateSources(result: HybridSearchResult): string[] {
802:   const sourceList = (result as { sources?: unknown }).sources;
803:   if (Array.isArray(sourceList)) {
804:     return sourceList.filter((value): value is string => typeof value === 'string');
805:   }
806:   return typeof result.source === 'string' && result.source.length > 0
807:     ? [result.source]
808:     : [];
809: }
810: 
811: function getCandidateSourceScores(result: HybridSearchResult): Record<string, number> {
812:   const sourceScores = (result as { sourceScores?: unknown }).sourceScores;
813:   if (sourceScores && typeof sourceScores === 'object' && !Array.isArray(sourceScores)) {
814:     const normalizedScores: Record<string, number> = {};
815:     for (const [source, score] of Object.entries(sourceScores as Record<string, unknown>)) {
816:       if (typeof score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(score)) {
817:         normalizedScores[source] = score;
818:       }
819:     }
820:     return normalizedScores;
821:   }
822: 
823:   const fallbackScores: Record<string, number> = {};
824:   const resultScore = typeof result.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(result.score)
825:     ? result.score
826:     : 0;
827:   for (const source of getCandidateSources(result)) {
828:     fallbackScores[source] = resultScore;
829:   }
830:   return fallbackScores;
831: }
832: 
833: function mergeRawCandidate(
834:   existing: HybridSearchResult | undefined,
835:   incoming: HybridSearchResult
836: ): HybridSearchResult {
837:   if (!existing) {
838:     const sources = getCandidateSources(incoming);
839:     const channelCount = sources.length;
840:     return {
841:       ...incoming,
842:       sources,
843:       channelCount,
844:       sourceScores: getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
845:       channelAttribution: sources,
846:     };
847:   }
848: 
849:   const existingScore = typeof existing.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(existing.score)
850:     ? existing.score
851:     : 0;
852:   const incomingScore = typeof incoming.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(incoming.score)
853:     ? incoming.score
854:     : 0;
855:   const primary = incomingScore > existingScore ? incoming : existing;
856:   const secondary = primary === incoming ? existing : incoming;
857:   const sources = Array.from(new Set([
858:     ...getCandidateSources(existing),
859:     ...getCandidateSources(incoming),
860:   ]));
861:   const channelCount = sources.length;
862:   const crossChannelBonus = Math.min(0.06, 0.02 * Math.max(0, channelCount - 1));
863:   const mergedScore = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, Math.max(existingScore, incomingScore) + crossChannelBonus));
864: 
865:   return {
866:     ...(secondary ?? {}),
867:     ...primary,
868:     score: mergedScore,
869:     source: typeof primary.source === 'string' ? primary.source : (sources[0] ?? 'hybrid'),
870:     sources,
871:     channelCount,
872:     sourceScores: {
873:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(existing),
874:       ...getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
875:     },
876:     channelAttribution: sources,
877:   };
878: }
879: 
880: function collectCandidatesFromLists(
881:   lists: RawChannelList[],
882:   limit?: number
883: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
884:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
885: 
886:   for (const list of lists) {
887:     if (!Array.isArray(list.results) || list.results.length === 0) continue;
888: 
889:     const scored = list.results.map((result) => {
890:       const rawScore = resolveRawCandidateScore(result as { score?: unknown; similarity?: unknown });
891:       return { result, rawScore };
892:     });
893: 
894:     let min = Infinity;
895:     let max = -Infinity;
896:     for (const { rawScore } of scored) {
897:       if (rawScore < min) min = rawScore;
898:       if (rawScore > max) max = rawScore;
899:     }
900:     const range = max - min;
901: 
902:     for (const { result, rawScore } of scored) {
903:       const normalizedScore = range > 0 ? (rawScore - min) / range : (rawScore > 0 ? 1.0 : 0);
904:       const candidate: HybridSearchResult = {
905:         ...result,
906:         id: result.id,
907:         source: list.source,
908:         score: normalizedScore,
909:         sources: [list.source],
910:         sourceScores: { [list.source]: normalizedScore },
911:         channelAttribution: [list.source],
912:       };
913:       const key = canonicalResultId(candidate.id);
914:       deduped.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(deduped.get(key), candidate));
915:     }
916:   }
917: 
918:   return applyResultLimit(
919:     Array.from(deduped.values()).sort((a, b) => {
920:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
921:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
922:       return bScore - aScore;
923:     }),
924:     limit
925:   );
926: }
927: 
928: function mergeRawCandidateSets(
929:   existing: HybridSearchResult[],
930:   incoming: HybridSearchResult[],
931:   limit?: number
932: ): HybridSearchResult[] {
933:   const merged = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
934: 
935:   for (const result of [...existing, ...incoming]) {
936:     const key = canonicalResultId(result.id);
937:     merged.set(key, mergeRawCandidate(merged.get(key), result));
938:   }
939: 
940:   return applyResultLimit(
941:     Array.from(merged.values()).sort((a, b) => {
942:       const aScore = typeof a.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(a.score) ? a.score : 0;
943:       const bScore = typeof b.score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(b.score) ? b.score : 0;
944:       return bScore - aScore;
945:     }),
946:     limit
947:   );
948: }
949: 
950: function getAllowedChannels(options: HybridSearchOptions): Set<ChannelName> {
951:   const allowed = new Set<ChannelName>(['vector', 'fts', 'graph', 'degree']);
952: 
953:   if (isBm25Enabled()) {
954:     allowed.add('bm25');
955:   }
956: 
957:   if (options.useVector === false) allowed.delete('vector');
958:   if (options.useBm25 === false) allowed.delete('bm25');
959:   if (options.useFts === false) allowed.delete('fts');
960:   if (options.useGraph === false) {
961:     allowed.delete('graph');
962:     allowed.delete('degree');
963:   }
964: 
965:   return allowed;
966: }
967: 
968: function applyAllowedChannelOverrides(
969:   options: HybridSearchOptions,
970:   allowedChannels: Set<ChannelName>,
971:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
972: ): HybridSearchOptions {
973:   return {
974:     ...options,
975:     ...overrides,
976:     useVector: allowedChannels.has('vector'),
977:     useBm25: allowedChannels.has('bm25'),
978:     useFts: allowedChannels.has('fts'),
979:     useGraph: allowedChannels.has('graph'),
980:   };
981: }
982: 
983: type FallbackPlanKind = 'adaptive' | 'tiered';
984: type FallbackStageName = 'primary' | 'retry';
985: 
986: interface FallbackPlanStage {
987:   stage: FallbackStageName;
988:   options: HybridSearchOptions;
989:   results: HybridSearchResult[];
990:   execution: HybridFusionExecution | null;
991:   trigger?: 'empty' | DegradationTrigger;
992: }
993: 
994: interface FallbackPlanExecution {
995:   allowedChannels: Set<ChannelName>;
996:   stages: FallbackPlanStage[];
997: }
998: 
999: interface HybridFusionExecution {
1000:   evaluationMode: boolean;
1001:   intent: string;
1002:   lists: Array<{
1003:     source: string;
1004:     results: Array<{ id: number | string; [key: string]: unknown }>;
1005:     weight?: number;
1006:   }>;
1007:   routeResult: ReturnType<typeof routeQuery>;
1008:   budgetResult: ReturnType<typeof getDynamicTokenBudget>;
1009:   s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta;
1010:   fusedResults: HybridSearchResult[];
1011:   vectorEmbeddingCache: Map<number, Float32Array>;
1012: }
1013: 
1014: function resolveAdaptiveDocumentType(
1015:   lists: Array<{ results: Array<Record<string, unknown>> }>,
1016: ): string | undefined {
1017:   const counts = new Map<string, number>();
1018:   for (const list of lists) {
1019:     for (const result of list.results.slice(0, 3)) {
1020:       const candidate = result.documentType ?? result.document_type;
1021:       if (typeof candidate !== 'string' || candidate.length === 0) {
1022:         continue;
1023:       }
1024:       counts.set(candidate, (counts.get(candidate) ?? 0) + 1);
1025:     }
1026:   }
1027: 
1028:   let topType: string | undefined;
1029:   let topCount = 0;
1030:   for (const [documentType, count] of counts) {
1031:     if (count > topCount) {
1032:       topType = documentType;
1033:       topCount = count;
1034:     }
1035:   }
1036: 
1037:   return topType;
1038: }
1039: 
1040: function markFallbackRetry(results: HybridSearchResult[]): HybridSearchResult[] {
1041:   for (const result of results) {
1042:     (result as Record<string, unknown>).fallbackRetry = true;
1043:   }
1044: 
1045:   return results;
1046: }
1047: 
1048: function toEmbeddingBufferView(value: unknown): Float32Array | null {
1049:   if (value instanceof Float32Array) {
1050:     return value;
1051:   }
1052: 
1053:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
1054:     const asNumbers = value.every((entry) => typeof entry === 'number' && Number.isFinite(entry));
1055:     return asNumbers ? new Float32Array(value) : null;
1056:   }
1057: 
1058:   if (Buffer.isBuffer(value)) {
1059:     return new Float32Array(value.buffer, value.byteOffset, value.byteLength / 4);
1060:   }
1061: 
1062:   return null;
1063: }
1064: 
1065: async function executeFallbackPlan(
1066:   query: string,
1067:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1068:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {},
1069:   planKind: FallbackPlanKind,
1070:   overrides: Partial<HybridSearchOptions> = {}
1071: ): Promise<FallbackPlanExecution> {
1072:   const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
1073:   const stages: FallbackPlanStage[] = [];
1074: 
1075:   const primaryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1076:     minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1077:     ...overrides,
1078:   });
1079:   const primaryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1080:   const primaryResults = primaryExecution
1081:     ? applyResultLimit(primaryExecution.fusedResults, primaryOptions.limit)
1082:     : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, primaryOptions);
1083:   stages.push({
1084:     stage: 'primary',
1085:     options: primaryOptions,
1086:     results: primaryResults,
1087:     execution: primaryExecution,
1088:   });
1089: 
1090:   if (planKind === 'tiered') {
1091:     const trigger = checkDegradation(primaryResults);
1092:     if (!trigger) {
1093:       return { allowedChannels, stages };
1094:     }
1095: 
1096:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1097:       ...overrides,
1098:       minSimilarity: TIERED_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1099:       forceAllChannels: true,
1100:     });
1101:     const retryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1102:     const retryResults = retryExecution
1103:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1104:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1105:     stages.push({
1106:       stage: 'retry',
1107:       options: retryOptions,
1108:       results: retryResults,
1109:       execution: retryExecution,
1110:       trigger,
1111:     });
1112:     return { allowedChannels, stages };
1113:   }
1114: 
1115:   const primaryMinSimilarity = primaryOptions.minSimilarity ?? PRIMARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY;
1116:   if (primaryResults.length === 0 && primaryMinSimilarity >= SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY) {
1117:     const retryOptions = applyAllowedChannelOverrides(options, allowedChannels, {
1118:       ...overrides,
1119:       minSimilarity: SECONDARY_FALLBACK_MIN_SIMILARITY,
1120:     });
1121:     const retryExecution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1122:     const retryResultsBase = retryExecution
1123:       ? applyResultLimit(retryExecution.fusedResults, retryOptions.limit)
1124:       : await hybridSearch(query, embedding, retryOptions);
1125:     const retryResults = retryResultsBase.length > 0
1126:       ? markFallbackRetry(retryResultsBase)
1127:       : retryResultsBase;
1128:     stages.push({
1129:       stage: 'retry',
1130:       options: retryOptions,
1131:       results: retryResults,
1132:       execution: retryExecution,
1133:       trigger: 'empty',
1134:     });
1135:   }
1136: 
1137:   return { allowedChannels, stages };
1138: }
1139: 
1140: // 11. HYBRID SEARCH
1141: 
1142: /**
1143:  * Run multi-channel hybrid search combining vector, FTS, BM25, and graph results with per-source normalization.
1144:  * Prefer hybridSearchEnhanced() or searchWithFallback() instead. This function uses naive per-source
1145:  * min-max normalization which produces different orderings than the RRF pipeline in hybridSearchEnhanced().
1146:  * Retained as internal fallback only.
1147:  */
1148: async function hybridSearch(
1149:   query: string,
1150:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1151:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1152: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1153:   const {
1154:     limit = DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1155:     specFolder,
1156:     minSimilarity = 0,
1157:     useBm25 = isBm25Enabled(),
1158:     useFts = true,
1159:     useVector = true,
1160:     useGraph = true,
1161:     includeArchived = false,
1162:   } = options;
1163: 
1164:   const results: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1165: 
1166:   // Vector search
1167:   if (useVector && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
1168:     try {
1169:       const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
1170:         limit,
1171:         specFolder,
1172:         minSimilarity,
1173:         includeConstitutional: false, // Handler manages constitutional separately
1174:         includeArchived,
1175:       });
1176:       for (const r of vectorResults) {
1177:         results.push({
1178:           ...r,
1179:           id: r.id as number,
1180:           score: (r.similarity as number) || 0,
1181:           source: 'vector',
1182:         });
1183:       }
1184:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1185:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1186:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Vector search failed: ${msg}`);
1187:     }
1188:   }
1189: 
1190:   // FTS search
1191:   if (useFts) {
1192:     const ftsResults = ftsSearch(query, { limit, specFolder, includeArchived });
1193:     results.push(...ftsResults);
1194:   }
1195: 
1196:   // BM25 search
1197:   if (useBm25) {
1198:     const bm25Results = bm25Search(query, { limit, specFolder });
1199:     results.push(...bm25Results);
1200:   }
1201: 
1202:   // Graph search
1203:   if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
1204:     try {
1205:       graphMetrics.totalQueries++;
1206:       const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, { limit, specFolder });
1207:       for (const r of graphResults) {
1208:         results.push({
1209:           ...r,
1210:           id: r.id as number | string,
1211:           score: (r.score as number) || 0,
1212:           source: 'graph',
1213:         });
1214:       }
1215:       if (graphResults.length > 0) {
1216:         graphMetrics.graphHits++;
1217:         graphMetrics.graphResultCount += graphResults.length;
1218:       }
1219:     } catch (error: unknown) {
1220:       const msg = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
1221:       console.warn(`[hybrid-search] Graph search failed: ${msg}`);
1222:     }
1223:   }
1224: 
1225:   // Normalize scores per source before merging so one method's raw scale
1226:   // Does not dominate others during final ranking.
1227:   const bySource = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult[]>();
1228:   for (const r of results) {
1229:     const src = r.source || 'unknown';
1230:     if (!bySource.has(src)) bySource.set(src, []);
1231:     bySource.get(src)!.push(r); // non-null safe: has() guard above guarantees entry exists
1232:   }
1233: 
1234:   const normalized: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1235:   for (const [, group] of bySource) {
1236:     if (group.length === 0) continue;
1237:     const scores = group.map(r => r.score);
1238:     let min = Infinity;
1239:     let max = -Infinity;
1240:     for (const s of scores) {
1241:       if (s < min) min = s;
1242:       if (s > max) max = s;
1243:     }
1244:     const range = max - min;
1245:     for (const r of group) {
1246:       normalized.push({
1247:         ...r,
1248:         score: range > 0 ? (r.score - min) / range : (r.score > 0 ? 1.0 : 0),
1249:       });
1250:     }
1251:   }
1252: 
1253:   // Deduplicate by ID (keep highest normalized score)
1254:   // LIMITATION: When a result appears in multiple sources (e.g., vector + fts),
1255:   // Only the highest-scoring entry's `source` is preserved. Multi-source provenance
1256:   // Is lost here. To fix properly, HybridSearchResult would need a `sources: string[]`
1257:   // Field and downstream consumers would need to be updated accordingly.
1258:   const deduped = new Map<string, HybridSearchResult>();
1259:   for (const r of normalized) {
1260:     const canonicalId = canonicalResultId(r.id);
1261:     const existing = deduped.get(canonicalId);
1262:     if (!existing || r.score > existing.score) {
1263:       deduped.set(canonicalId, r);
1264:     }
1265:   }
1266: 
1267:   return Array.from(deduped.values())
1268:     .sort((a, b) => b.score - a.score)
1269:     .slice(0, limit);
1270: }
1271: 
1272: /**
1273:  * Enhanced hybrid search with RRF fusion.
1274:  * All search channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
1275:  * is correct — Promise.all would add overhead without achieving parallelism.
1276:  */
1277: async function hybridSearchEnhanced(
1278:   query: string,
1279:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1280:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1281: ): Promise<HybridSearchResult[]> {
1282:   const execution = await collectAndFuseHybridResults(query, embedding, options);
1283:   if (execution) {
1284:     if (options.stopAfterFusion) {
1285:       return applyResultLimit(execution.fusedResults, options.limit);
1286:     }
1287: 
1288:     return enrichFusedResults(query, execution, options);
1289:   }
1290: 
1291:   return hybridSearch(query, embedding, options);
1292: }
1293: 
1294: async function collectAndFuseHybridResults(
1295:   query: string,
1296:   embedding: Float32Array | number[] | null,
1297:   options: HybridSearchOptions = {}
1298: ): Promise<HybridFusionExecution | null> {
1299:   try {
1300:     const evaluationMode = options.evaluationMode === true;
1301:     const lists: HybridFusionExecution['lists'] = [];
1302: 
1303:     // Pipeline metadata collector (populated by flag-gated stages)
1304:     const s3meta: Sprint3PipelineMeta = {};
1305: 
1306:     // -- Stage A: Query Classification + Routing (SPECKIT_COMPLEXITY_ROUTER) --
1307:     // When enabled, classifies query complexity and restricts channels to a
1308:     // Subset (e.g., simple queries skip graph+degree). When disabled, all channels run.
1309:     const routeResult = routeQuery(query, options.triggerPhrases);
1310:     const allPossibleChannels: ChannelName[] = ['vector', 'fts', 'bm25', 'graph', 'degree'];
1311:     const activeChannels = options.forceAllChannels
1312:       ? new Set<ChannelName>(allPossibleChannels)
1313:       : new Set<ChannelName>(routeResult.channels);
1314: 
1315:     // Respect explicit caller channel disables across both the primary route and
1316:     // every fallback tier. useGraph=false also disables the dependent degree lane.
1317:     const allowedChannels = getAllowedChannels(options);
1318:     for (const channel of allPossibleChannels) {
1319:       if (!allowedChannels.has(channel)) activeChannels.delete(channel);
1320:     }
1321: 
1322:     const skippedChannels = allPossibleChannels.filter(ch => !activeChannels.has(ch));
1323: 
1324:     if (skippedChannels.length > 0) {
1325:       s3meta.routing = {
1326:         tier: routeResult.tier,
1327:         channels: routeResult.channels,
1328:         skippedChannels,
1329:         featureFlagEnabled: isComplexityRouterEnabled(),
1330:         confidence: routeResult.classification.confidence,
1331:         features: routeResult.classification.features as Record<string, unknown>,
1332:       };
1333:     }
1334: 
1335:     // -- Stage E: Dynamic Token Budget (SPECKIT_DYNAMIC_TOKEN_BUDGET) --
1336:     // Compute tier-aware budget early so it's available for downstream truncation.
1337:     // When disabled, getDynamicTokenBudget returns the default 4000 budget with applied=false.
1338:     // Weak / low-confidence queries keep the full budget so recall is not trimmed away.
1339:     const lowSignalQuery =
1340:       routeResult.classification.confidence === 'low' ||
1341:       routeResult.classification.confidence === 'fallback';
1342:     const budgetResult = getDynamicTokenBudget(routeResult.tier, undefined, {
1343:       lowSignal: lowSignalQuery,
1344:     });
1345:     if (budgetResult.applied && !evaluationMode) {
1346:       s3meta.tokenBudget = {
1347:         tier: budgetResult.tier,
1348:         budget: budgetResult.budget,
1349:         applied: budgetResult.applied,
1350:         featureFlagEnabled: isDynamicTokenBudgetEnabled(),
1351:         configValues: DEFAULT_TOKEN_BUDGET_CONFIG as unknown as Record<string, number>,
1352:         // headerOverhead and adjustedBudget are patched in below after they are computed
1353:         headerOverhead: 0,
1354:         adjustedBudget: budgetResult.budget,
1355:       };
1356:     }
1357: 
1358:     // Channel results collected independently, merged after all complete
1359:     let semanticResults: Array<{ id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown }> = [];
1360:     let ftsChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1361:     let bm25ChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1362:     let triggerChannelResults: HybridSearchResult[] = [];
1363:     const vectorEmbeddingCache = new Map<number, Float32Array>();
1364: 
1365:     // All channels use synchronous better-sqlite3; sequential execution
1366:     // Is correct — Promise.all adds overhead without parallelism.
1367: 
1368:     // Vector channel — gated by query-complexity routing
1369:     if (activeChannels.has('vector') && embedding && vectorSearchFn) {
1370:       try {
1371:         const vectorResults = vectorSearchFn(embedding, {
1372:           limit: options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1373:           specFolder: options.specFolder,
1374:           minSimilarity: options.minSimilarity || 0,
1375:           includeConstitutional: false,
1376:           includeArchived: options.includeArchived || false,
1377:           includeEmbeddings: true,
1378:         });
1379:         semanticResults = vectorResults.map((r: Record<string, unknown>): { id: number | string; source: string; [key: string]: unknown } => ({
1380:           ...r,
1381:           id: r.id as number | string,
1382:           source: 'vector',
1383:         }));
1384:         for (const result of semanticResults) {
1385:           if (typeof result.id !== 'number') continue;
1386:           const embeddingCandidate = toEmbeddingBufferView(
1387:             (result as Record<string, unknown>).embedding
1388:             ?? (result as Record<string, unknown>).embeddingBuffer
1389:           );
1390:           if (embeddingCandidate) {
1391:             vectorEmbeddingCache.set(result.id, embeddingCandidate);
1392:           }
1393:         }
1394:         lists.push({ source: 'vector', results: semanticResults, weight: 1.0 });
1395:       } catch (_err: unknown) {
1396:         // Non-critical — vector channel failure does not block pipeline
1397:         console.warn('[hybrid-search] Channel error:', _err instanceof Error ? _err.message : String(_err));
1398:       }
1399:     }
1400: 
1401:     // FTS channel (internal error handling in ftsSearch) — gated by query-complexity routing
1402:     if (activeChannels.has('fts')) {
1403:       ftsChannelResults = ftsSearch(query, options);
1404:       if (ftsChannelResults.length > 0) {
1405:         // FTS weight reduced to 0.3 after ablation showed 0.8 was harmful,
1406:         // flooding top-K with noisy lexical matches despite its exact-match value.
1407:         lists.push({ source: 'fts', results: ftsChannelResults, weight: 0.3 });
1408:       }
1409:     }
1410: 
1411:     // BM25 channel (internal error handling in bm25Search) — gated by query-complexity routing
1412:     if (activeChannels.has('bm25')) {
1413:       bm25ChannelResults = bm25Search(query, options);
1414:       if (bm25ChannelResults.length > 0) {
1415:         // BM25 weight 0.6 is lowest lexical channel — in-memory BM25 index
1416:         // Has less precise scoring than SQLite FTS5 BM25; kept for coverage breadth.
1417:         lists.push({ source: 'bm25', results: bm25ChannelResults, weight: 0.6 });
1418:       }
1419:     }
1420: 
1421:     triggerChannelResults = exactTriggerSearch(query, options);
1422:     if (triggerChannelResults.length > 0) {
1423:       lists.push({ source: 'trigger', results: triggerChannelResults, weight: 1.4 });
1424:     }
1425: 
1426:     // Graph channel — gated by query-complexity routing
1427:     const useGraph = (options.useGraph !== false) && activeChannels.has('graph');
1428:     if (useGraph && graphSearchFn) {
1429:       try {
1430:         graphMetrics.totalQueries++; // counted only if channel executes
1431:         const graphResults = graphSearchFn(query, {
1432:           limit: options.limit || DEFAULT_LIMIT,
1433:           specFolder: options.specFolder,
1434:           intent: options.intent,
1435:         });
1436:         if (graphResults.length > 0) {
1437:           graphMetrics.graphHits++;
1438:           graphMetrics.graphResultCount += graphResults.length;
1439:           lists.push({ source: 'graph', results: graphResults.map((r: Record<string, unknown>) => ({
1440:             ...r,
1441:             id: r.id as number | string,
1442:           })), weight: 0.5 });
1443:         }

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Vector Index Store
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
5: // SEARCH: VECTOR INDEX
6: // TypeScript port of the vector index implementation.
7: // DECAY STRATEGY: Search-time temporal decay uses an
8: // FSRS-preferred strategy. Memories with FSRS review data (last_review
9: // IS NOT NULL, review_count > 0) use the FSRS v4 power-law formula:
10: // R(t) = (1 + 0.2346 * t / S)^(-0.5)
11: // Memories without review data fall back to half-life exponential:
12: // Weight * 0.5^(days / half_life_days)
13: // This ensures backward compatibility while aligning reviewed
14: // Memories with the canonical FSRS algorithm.

172: export async function get_confirmed_embedding_dimension(timeout_ms = 5000): Promise<number> {
173:   const start = Date.now();
