195:   existing_db: boolean;
196:   stored_dim: number | null;
197:   source: 'vec_metadata' | 'active_embedder_profile' | 'startup_profile' | 'vec_memories' | null;
198:   reason?: string;
199: };
200: 
201: function get_existing_embedding_dimension(
202:   database: Database.Database,
203:   options: { allowProfileFallback?: boolean } = {},
204: ): StoredEmbeddingDimension {
205:   const allowProfileFallback = options.allowProfileFallback !== false;
206:   const schema_row = database.prepare(`
207:     SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='memory_index'
208:   `).get();
209: 
210:   if (!schema_row) {
211:     return {
212:       existing_db: false,
213:       stored_dim: null,
214:       source: null,
215:       reason: 'No existing schema',
216:     };
217:   }
218: 
219:   const attached = database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string }>;
220:   if (attached.some((entry) => entry.name === ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA)) {
221:     const shard_metadata_table = database.prepare(`
222:       SELECT name FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
223:     `).get();
224: 
225:     if (shard_metadata_table) {
226:       const stored_row = database.prepare(`
227:         SELECT value FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata WHERE key IN ('embedding_dim', 'dim')
228:         ORDER BY CASE key WHEN 'embedding_dim' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END
229:         LIMIT 1
230:       `).get() as { value: string } | undefined;
231: 
232:       if (stored_row) {
233:         const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
234:         if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
235:           return {
236:             existing_db: true,
237:             stored_dim,
238:             source: 'vec_metadata',
239:           };
240:         }
241:       }
242:     }
243:   }
244: 
245:   const metadata_table = database.prepare(`
246:     SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='vec_metadata'
247:   `).get();
248: 
249:   if (metadata_table) {
250:     const stored_row = database.prepare(`
251:       SELECT key, value FROM vec_metadata WHERE key IN ('embedding_dim', 'dim', 'active_embedder_dim')
252:       ORDER BY CASE key WHEN 'embedding_dim' THEN 0 WHEN 'dim' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END
253:       LIMIT 1
254:     `).get() as { key: string; value: string } | undefined;
255: 
256:     if (stored_row) {
257:       const stored_dim = parseInt(stored_row.value, 10);
258:       if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
259:         return {
260:           existing_db: true,
261:           stored_dim,
262:           source: stored_row.key === 'active_embedder_dim' ? 'active_embedder_profile' : 'vec_metadata',
263:         };
264:       }
265:     }
266:   }
267: 
268:   if (allowProfileFallback) {
269:     const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
270:     if (active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && active.dim > 0) {
271:       return {
272:         existing_db: true,
273:         stored_dim: active.dim,
274:         source: 'active_embedder_profile',
275:       };
276:     }
277: 
278:     const startupProfile = getStartupEmbeddingProfile();
279:     if (Number.isInteger(startupProfile.dim) && startupProfile.dim > 0) {
280:       return {
281:         existing_db: true,
282:         stored_dim: startupProfile.dim,
283:         source: 'startup_profile',
284:       };
285:     }
286:   }
287: 
288:   const vec_table = database.prepare(`
289:     SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE name='vec_memories'
290:   `).get() as { sql: string | null } | undefined;
291:   const vec_sql = typeof vec_table?.sql === 'string' ? vec_table.sql : null;
292:   const match = vec_sql?.match(/FLOAT\[(\d+)\]/i);
293: 
294:   if (match) {
295:     const stored_dim = parseInt(match[1], 10);
296:     if (Number.isFinite(stored_dim) && stored_dim > 0) {
297:       console.warn('[vector-index] Falling back to vec_memories schema text for embedding dimension; configure an active embedder profile to make dimension discovery authoritative.');
298:       return {
299:         existing_db: true,
300:         stored_dim,
301:         source: 'vec_memories',
302:       };
303:     }
304:   }
305: 
306:   return {
307:     existing_db: true,
308:     stored_dim: null,
309:     source: null,
310:     reason: 'No stored vector dimension found for existing schema',
311:   };
312: }
313: 
314: function validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(
315:   database: Database.Database | null,
316:   _sqlite_vec_available: boolean,
317: ): EmbeddingDimensionValidation {
318:   if (!database) {
319:     return { valid: true, stored: null, current: null, reason: 'No database' };
320:   }
321: 
322:   try {
323:     const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
324:     const current_dim = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && active.dim > 0
325:       ? active.dim
326:       : get_embedding_dim();
327:     const existing = get_existing_embedding_dimension(database);
328: 
329:     if (!existing.existing_db) {
330:       return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason };
331:     }
332: 
333:     if (existing.stored_dim == null) {
334:       return { valid: true, stored: null, current: current_dim, reason: existing.reason || 'No stored dimension' };
335:     }
336: 
337:     if (existing.stored_dim !== current_dim) {
338:       const SOURCE_LABELS = {
339:         vec_metadata: 'vec_metadata',
340:         active_embedder_profile: 'active embedder profile',
341:         startup_profile: 'startup embedding profile',
342:         vec_memories: 'vec_memories schema',
343:       };
344:       const source_label = existing.source ? SOURCE_LABELS[existing.source] : 'vec_memories schema';
345:       const warning = `EMBEDDING DIMENSION MISMATCH: Existing database stores ${existing.stored_dim}-dim vectors (${source_label}), ` +
346:         `but the active embedding configuration resolves to ${current_dim}. Refusing to bootstrap because vector search would fail. ` +
347:         `Solutions: 1) Delete database and re-index, 2) Set EMBEDDINGS_PROVIDER to match original, ` +
348:         `3) Let SPEC_KIT_DB_DIR auto-derive a profile-specific database, or set MEMORY_DB_PATH only for an intentional file override.`;
349:       console.error(`[vector-index] WARNING: ${warning}`);
350:       return { valid: false, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim, warning };
351:     }
352: 
353:     return { valid: true, stored: existing.stored_dim, current: current_dim };
354:   } catch (e: unknown) {
355:     console.warn('[vector-index] Dimension validation error:', get_error_message(e));
356:     return { valid: true, stored: null, current: get_embedding_dim(), reason: get_error_message(e) };
357:   }
358: }
359: 
360: /**
361:  * Validates that stored vector dimensions match the provider.
362:  * @returns The validation result.
363:  */
364: export function validate_embedding_dimension(): { valid: boolean; stored: number | null; current: number | null; reason?: string; warning?: string } {
365:   return validate_embedding_dimension_for_connection(db, sqlite_vec_available_flag);
366: }

369:    2. DATABASE PATH AND SECURITY

372: function get_default_db_path(): string {
373:   return process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH || resolveDatabasePaths().databasePath;
374: }
375: 
376: /** Default path for the vector-index database file. */
377: export const DEFAULT_DB_PATH = process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH
378:   || get_default_db_path();
379: const DB_PERMISSIONS = 0o600;

381: function resolve_database_path() {
382:   return process.env.MEMORY_DB_PATH || get_default_db_path();
383: }

385: export const ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA = 'active_vec';

387: type ActiveVectorSourceTelemetry = {
388:   canonical_path: string;
389:   shard_path: string;
390:   attached: boolean;
391:   profile: {
392:     provider: string;
393:     model: string;
394:     dim: number;
395:     dtype: string | null;
396:   };

399: function quote_sql_string(value: string): string {
400:   return `'${value.replace(/'/g, "''")}'`;
401: }
402: 
403: function quote_identifier(value: string): string {
404:   return `"${value.replace(/"/g, '""')}"`;
405: }

407: function get_database_file_path(database: Database.Database): string {
408:   const row = database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all()
409:     .find((entry: unknown) => (entry as { name?: string }).name === 'main') as { file?: string } | undefined;
410:   return row?.file && row.file.length > 0 ? row.file : db_path;
411: }
412: 
413: function resolve_database_base_dir(database: Database.Database): string {
414:   const filePath = get_database_file_path(database);
415:   if (!filePath || filePath === ':memory:') {
416:     return resolveDatabasePaths().databaseDir;
417:   }
418:   return path.dirname(filePath);
419: }

421: function legacy_profile_database_path(profile: EmbeddingProfile, baseDir: string): string {
422:   return path.join(baseDir, `context-index__${profile.slug}.sqlite`);
423: }

426:   const vectorDir = path.join(baseDir, 'vectors');
427:   if (!fs.existsSync(vectorDir)) {
428:     fs.mkdirSync(vectorDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
429:   }

433: function resolve_profile_for_active_embedder(database: Database.Database): EmbeddingProfile {
434:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
435:   if (active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && active.dim > 0) {
436:     const startup = getStartupEmbeddingProfile();
437:     return new EmbeddingProfile({
438:       provider: active.provider ?? startup.provider,
439:       model: active.name,
440:       dim: active.dim,
441:       dtype: null,
442:       baseUrl: startup.baseUrl,
443:     });
444:   }
445:   return getStartupEmbeddingProfile();
446: }
447: 
448: function vector_table_name_for_profile(profile: EmbeddingProfile): string {
449:   return `vec_${profile.dim}`;
450: }
451: 
452: function active_schema_table(tableName: string): string {
453:   return `${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${tableName}`;
454: }
455: 
456: export function activeVectorSource(tableName?: string): string {
457:   return tableName ? active_schema_table(tableName) : ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA;
458: }
459: 
460: function get_attached_vector_path(database: Database.Database): string | null {
461:   const attached = database.prepare('PRAGMA database_list').all() as Array<{ name?: string; file?: string }>;
462:   const row = attached.find((entry) => entry.name === ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA);
463:   return row?.file ? path.resolve(row.file) : null;
464: }
465: 
466: function table_exists_in_schema(database: Database.Database, schema: string, tableName: string): boolean {
467:   const row = database.prepare(`
468:     SELECT 1 AS found
469:     FROM ${quote_identifier(schema)}.sqlite_master
470:     WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = ?
471:     LIMIT 1
472:   `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
473:   return row?.found === 1;
474: }
475: 
476: type VectorShardIntegrityProbeResult =
477:   | { ok: true }
478:   | { ok: false; reason: string };
479: 
480: function quick_check_verdict(database: Database.Database, schema?: string): string {
481:   const rows = schema
482:     ? database.pragma(`${schema}.quick_check(1)`) as Array<Record<string, unknown>>
483:     : database.pragma('quick_check(1)') as Array<Record<string, unknown>>;
484:   const first = rows?.[0] ? Object.values(rows[0])[0] : undefined;
485:   return typeof first === 'string' ? first : 'quick_check returned no verdict';
486: }
487: 
488: function required_vector_shard_tables(profile: EmbeddingProfile, vecAvailable: boolean): string[] {
489:   return [
490:     'vec_metadata',
491:     'embedding_cache',
492:     vector_table_name_for_profile(profile),
493:     ...(vecAvailable ? ['vec_memories'] : []),
494:   ];
495: }
496: 
497: function run_attached_vector_shard_integrity_probe(
498:   database: Database.Database,
499:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
500:   vecAvailable: boolean,
501: ): VectorShardIntegrityProbeResult {
502:   try {
503:     const verdict = quick_check_verdict(database, ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA);
504:     if (verdict !== 'ok') {
505:       return { ok: false, reason: `quick_check failed: ${verdict}` };
506:     }
507:     for (const tableName of required_vector_shard_tables(profile, vecAvailable)) {
508:       if (!table_exists_in_schema(database, ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA, tableName)) {
509:         return { ok: false, reason: `required table missing: ${tableName}` };
510:       }
511:     }
512:     return { ok: true };
513:   } catch (error: unknown) {
514:     return { ok: false, reason: get_error_message(error) };
515:   }
516: }
517: 
518: function run_vector_shard_integrity_probe_at_path(
519:   shardPath: string,
520:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
521:   vecAvailable: boolean,
522: ): VectorShardIntegrityProbeResult {
523:   if (!fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
524:     return { ok: true };
525:   }
526: 
527:   let shard: Database.Database | null = null;
528:   try {
529:     shard = new Database(shardPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
530:     if (vecAvailable) {
531:       sqliteVec.load(shard);
532:     }
533:     const verdict = quick_check_verdict(shard);
534:     if (verdict !== 'ok') {
535:       return { ok: false, reason: `quick_check failed: ${verdict}` };
536:     }
537:     for (const tableName of required_vector_shard_tables(profile, vecAvailable)) {
538:       const row = shard.prepare(`
539:         SELECT 1 AS found
540:         FROM sqlite_master
541:         WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = ?
542:         LIMIT 1
543:       `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
544:       if (row?.found !== 1) {
545:         return { ok: false, reason: `required table missing: ${tableName}` };
546:       }
547:     }
548:     return { ok: true };
549:   } catch (error: unknown) {
550:     return { ok: false, reason: get_error_message(error) };
551:   } finally {
552:     if (shard) {
553:       try { shard.close(); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
554:     }
555:   }
556: }
557: 
558: function count_successful_embedding_rows(database: Database.Database): number {
559:   try {
560:     const row = database.prepare(`
561:       SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
562:       FROM memory_index
563:       WHERE embedding_status = 'success'
564:     `).get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
565:     return Math.max(0, Number(row?.count ?? 0));
566:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
567:     return 0;
568:   }
569: }
570: 
571: function count_attached_vector_rows(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): number {
572:   const tableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
573:   if (!table_exists_in_schema(database, ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA, tableName)) {
574:     return 0;
575:   }
576:   try {
577:     const row = database.prepare(`
578:       SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
579:       FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(tableName)}
580:     `).get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
581:     return Math.max(0, Number(row?.count ?? 0));
582:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
583:     return 0;
584:   }
585: }
586: 
587: function count_vector_rows_at_path(shardPath: string, profile: EmbeddingProfile): number {
588:   if (!fs.existsSync(shardPath)) {
589:     return 0;
590:   }
591: 
592:   const tableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
593:   let shard: Database.Database | null = null;
594:   try {
595:     shard = new Database(shardPath, { readonly: true, fileMustExist: true });
596:     const table = shard.prepare(`
597:       SELECT 1 AS found
598:       FROM sqlite_master
599:       WHERE type IN ('table', 'view') AND name = ?
600:       LIMIT 1
601:     `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
602:     if (table?.found !== 1) {
603:       return 0;
604:     }
605:     const row = shard.prepare(`
606:       SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
607:       FROM ${quote_identifier(tableName)}
608:     `).get() as { count?: number } | undefined;
609:     return Math.max(0, Number(row?.count ?? 0));
610:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
611:     return 0;
612:   } finally {
613:     if (shard) {
614:       try { shard.close(); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
615:     }
616:   }
617: }
618: 
619: function has_orphan_vector_shard_quarantine(shardPath: string): boolean {
620:   try {
621:     const shardDir = path.dirname(shardPath);
622:     const quarantinePrefix = `${path.basename(shardPath)}.quarantined-`;
623:     return fs.readdirSync(shardDir).some((name) => name.startsWith(quarantinePrefix));
624:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
625:     return false;
626:   }
627: }
628: 
629: function assess_vector_shard_repair_state(
630:   database: Database.Database,
631:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
632:   shardPath: string,
633: ): VectorShardRepairStateAssessment {
634:   const sentinel = read_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(shardPath);
635:   const attachedPath = get_attached_vector_path(database);
636:   const attached = attachedPath === path.resolve(shardPath);
637:   const probe = attached
638:     ? run_attached_vector_shard_integrity_probe(database, profile, sqlite_vec_available_flag)
639:     : run_vector_shard_integrity_probe_at_path(shardPath, profile, sqlite_vec_available_flag);
640:   const expected = count_successful_embedding_rows(database);
641:   const vecRows = probe.ok
642:     ? attached
643:       ? count_attached_vector_rows(database, profile)
644:       : count_vector_rows_at_path(shardPath, profile)
645:     : 0;
646:   const shardComplete = probe.ok && (expected === 0 || vecRows >= expected);
647:   const orphanQuarantine = !shardComplete && !sentinel && has_orphan_vector_shard_quarantine(shardPath);
648: 
649:   return { sentinel, shardComplete, orphanQuarantine };
650: }
651: 
652: function build_vector_shard_quarantine_path(shardPath: string): string {
653:   const stamp = new Date().toISOString().replace(/[^0-9A-Za-z]/g, '');
654:   return `${shardPath}.quarantined-${stamp}-${process.pid}`;
655: }
656: 
657: function quarantine_vector_shard(
658:   shardPath: string,
659:   reason: string,
660:   quarantinePath = build_vector_shard_quarantine_path(shardPath),
661:   sentinelPersisted = true,
662: ): string {
663:   for (const suffix of ['', '-wal', '-shm']) {
664:     const source = `${shardPath}${suffix}`;
665:     if (!fs.existsSync(source)) {
666:       continue;
667:     }
668:     fs.renameSync(source, `${quarantinePath}${suffix}`);
669:   }
670:   fsync_file_if_possible(quarantinePath);
671:   fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(shardPath));
672:   recordVectorShardQuarantined({ shardPath, quarantinePath, reason, sentinelPersisted });
673:   return quarantinePath;
674: }
675: 
676: function handle_vector_shard_repair_assessment(
677:   database: Database.Database,
678:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
679:   shardPath: string,
680:   assessment: VectorShardRepairStateAssessment,
681: ): void {
682:   if (assessment.sentinel) {
683:     if (assessment.shardComplete) {
684:       clear_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(shardPath);
685:       console.warn('[vector-index] Cleared stale vector shard repair sentinel after shard completeness check');
686:       return;
687:     }
688:     resume_vector_shard_repair_from_sentinel(database, profile, shardPath, assessment.sentinel);
689:     return;
690:   }
691: 
692:   if (assessment.orphanQuarantine) {
693:     const reason = 'orphan quarantine artifacts without sentinel';
694:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Scheduling vector shard repair: ${reason}`);
695:     schedule_vector_shard_rebuild(database, profile, shardPath, reason);
696:   }
697: }
698: 
699: function schedule_vector_shard_rebuild(
700:   database: Database.Database,
701:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
702:   shardPath: string,
703:   reason: string,
704: ): void {
705:   setImmediate(() => {
706:     void import('../embedders/reindex.js')
707:       .then(({ startVectorShardRepairReindex }) => {
708:         startVectorShardRepairReindex(profile, { db: database, reason, shardPath });
709:       })
710:       .catch((error: unknown) => {
711:         recordVectorShardRebuildFailed({
712:           jobId: 'not-started',
713:           shardPath,
714:           reason: get_error_message(error),
715:         });
716:       });
717:   });
718: }
719: 
720: function quarantine_and_rebuild_vector_shard(
721:   database: Database.Database,
722:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
723:   shardPath: string,
724:   reason: string,
725: ): void {
726:   let effectiveReason = reason;
727:   recordVectorShardProbeFailure({ shardPath, reason: effectiveReason });
728:   const quarantinePath = build_vector_shard_quarantine_path(shardPath);
729:   const sentinelPersisted = write_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(shardPath, profile, reason, quarantinePath);
730:   if (!sentinelPersisted) {
731:     effectiveReason = `${reason}; repair sentinel write FAILED - restart durability at risk`;
732:     console.error(`[vector-index] Vector shard repair sentinel write failed for ${path.basename(shardPath)}; restart durability at risk`);
733:   }
734:   quarantine_vector_shard(shardPath, effectiveReason, quarantinePath, sentinelPersisted);
735:   schedule_vector_shard_rebuild(database, profile, shardPath, `${effectiveReason}; quarantined as ${path.basename(quarantinePath)}`);
736: }
737: 
738: function write_shard_metadata(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
739:   database.exec(`
740:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata (
741:       key TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
742:       value TEXT NOT NULL,
743:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
744:     )
745:   `);
746: 
747:   const rows = database.prepare(`
748:     SELECT key, value
749:     FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata
750:     WHERE key IN ('provider', 'model', 'dim', 'embedding_dim')
751:   `).all() as Array<{ key: string; value: string }>;
752:   const metadata = new Map(rows.map((row) => [row.key, row.value]));
753:   const mismatches = [
754:     ['provider', profile.provider],
755:     ['model', profile.model],
756:     ['dim', String(profile.dim)],
757:     ['embedding_dim', String(profile.dim)],
758:   ].filter(([key, expected]) => {
759:     const actual = metadata.get(key);
760:     return actual != null && actual.length > 0 && actual !== expected;
761:   });
762: 
763:   if (mismatches.length > 0) {
764:     const detail = mismatches.map(([key, expected]) => `${key}: expected ${expected}, got ${metadata.get(key)}`).join('; ');
765:     throw new VectorIndexError(
766:       `Attached vector shard metadata mismatch (${detail})`,
767:       VectorIndexErrorCode.INTEGRITY_ERROR,
768:     );
769:   }
770: 
771:   const upsert = database.prepare(`
772:     INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_metadata (key, value)
773:     VALUES (?, ?)
774:   `);
775:   upsert.run('provider', profile.provider);
776:   upsert.run('model', profile.model);
777:   upsert.run('dim', String(profile.dim));
778:   upsert.run('embedding_dim', String(profile.dim));
779: }

781: function ensure_embedding_cache_table(database: Database.Database): void {
782:   database.exec(`
783:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.embedding_cache (
784:       content_hash TEXT NOT NULL,
785:       profile_key TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT '',
786:       input_kind TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'document' CHECK (input_kind IN ('document', 'query')),
787:       model_id TEXT NOT NULL,
788:       embedding BLOB NOT NULL,
789:       dimensions INTEGER NOT NULL,
790:       created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
791:       last_used_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
792:       PRIMARY KEY (content_hash, profile_key, input_kind, model_id, dimensions)
793:     )
794:   `);
795: }
796: 
797: function ensure_vector_shard_schema(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
798:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.journal_mode = WAL`);
799:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.cache_size = -8192`);
800:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.mmap_size = 33554432`);
801:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.temp_store = DEFAULT`);
802:   database.pragma(`${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.wal_autocheckpoint = 256`);
803: 
804:   write_shard_metadata(database, profile);
805:   ensure_embedding_cache_table(database);
806: 
807:   const dimTableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
808:   database.exec(`
809:     CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} (
810:       id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY,
811:       vec BLOB NOT NULL
812:     )
813:   `);
814: 
815:   if (sqlite_vec_available_flag && !table_exists_in_schema(database, ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA, 'vec_memories')) {
816:     database.exec(`
817:       CREATE VIRTUAL TABLE ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories USING vec0(
818:         embedding FLOAT[${profile.dim}]
819:       )
820:     `);
821:   }
822: }
823: 
824: function drop_canonical_vector_payload_tables(database: Database.Database): void {
825:   for (const tableName of ['embedding_cache', 'vec_memories']) {
826:     try {
827:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quote_identifier(tableName)}`);
828:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
829:       // Existing legacy virtual/shadow tables are migrated before this cleanup; failures are non-fatal.
830:     }
831:   }
832:   const rows = database.prepare(`
833:     SELECT name
834:     FROM main.sqlite_master
835:     WHERE type='table'
836:       AND (name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\' OR name LIKE 'vec_memories\\_%' ESCAPE '\\')
837:   `).all() as Array<{ name: string }>;
838:   for (const row of rows) {
839:     if (row.name === 'vec_metadata') {
840:       continue;
841:     }
842:     try {
843:       database.exec(`DROP TABLE IF EXISTS main.${quote_identifier(row.name)}`);
844:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
845:       // Best-effort canonical slimming. Query paths use active_vec after attach.
846:     }
847:   }
848: }

850: function create_legacy_temp_aliases(database: Database.Database, profile: EmbeddingProfile): void {
851:   const dimTableName = vector_table_name_for_profile(profile);
852:   try {
853:     database.exec(`
854:       DROP VIEW IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories;
855:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories_insert;
856:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.vec_memories_delete;
857:       CREATE TEMP VIEW vec_memories AS
858:         SELECT rowid, embedding FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories;
859:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER vec_memories_insert
860:       INSTEAD OF INSERT ON vec_memories
861:       BEGIN
862:         INSERT INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories(rowid, embedding)
863:         VALUES (CAST(new.rowid AS INTEGER), new.embedding);
864:       END;
865:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER vec_memories_delete
866:       INSTEAD OF DELETE ON vec_memories
867:       BEGIN
868:         DELETE FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.vec_memories WHERE rowid = old.rowid;
869:       END;
870:       DROP VIEW IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)};
871:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_insert`)};
872:       DROP TRIGGER IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_delete`)};
873:       CREATE TEMP VIEW ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} AS
874:         SELECT id, vec FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)};
875:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER ${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_insert`)}
876:       INSTEAD OF INSERT ON ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}
877:       BEGIN
878:         INSERT OR REPLACE INTO ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}(id, vec)
879:         VALUES (new.id, new.vec);
880:       END;
881:       CREATE TEMP TRIGGER ${quote_identifier(`${dimTableName}_delete`)}
882:       INSTEAD OF DELETE ON ${quote_identifier(dimTableName)}
883:       BEGIN
884:         DELETE FROM ${ACTIVE_VECTOR_SCHEMA}.${quote_identifier(dimTableName)} WHERE id = old.id;
885:       END;
886:     `);
887:   } catch (error: unknown) {
888:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not create legacy vector temp aliases: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
889:   }
890: }
891: 
892: function drop_legacy_temp_aliases(database: Database.Database): void {
893:   try {
894:     const rows = database.prepare(`
895:       SELECT type, name
896:       FROM temp.sqlite_master
897:       WHERE name = 'vec_memories'
898:          OR name LIKE 'vec_memories\\_%' ESCAPE '\\'
899:          OR name LIKE 'vec\\_%' ESCAPE '\\'
900:     `).all() as Array<{ type: string; name: string }>;
901: 
902:     for (const row of rows) {
903:       if (row.type !== 'view' && row.type !== 'trigger') {
904:         continue;
905:       }
906:       database.exec(`DROP ${row.type.toUpperCase()} IF EXISTS temp.${quote_identifier(row.name)}`);
907:     }
908:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
909:     // Best-effort cleanup only.
910:   }
911: }

913: // Unified allowed paths
914: const ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS = [
915:   path.join(process.cwd(), 'specs'),
916:   path.join(process.cwd(), '.opencode'),
917:   path.join(os.homedir(), '.claude'),
918:   process.cwd(),
919:   ...(process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS ? process.env.MEMORY_ALLOWED_PATHS.split(path.delimiter) : [])
920: ].filter(Boolean).map(p => path.resolve(p));
921: 
922: /**
923:  * Validates a file path against allowed local base paths.
924:  * @param file_path - The file path to validate.
925:  * @returns The validated file path, if allowed.
926:  */
927: export function validate_file_path_local(file_path: unknown): string | null {
928:   if (typeof file_path !== 'string') {
929:     return null;
930:   }
931: 
932:   return validateFilePath(file_path, ALLOWED_BASE_PATHS);
933: }
934: 
935: // Async version for non-blocking concurrent file reads
936: /**
937:  * Reads a file asynchronously after validating the path.
938:  * @param file_path - The file path to read.
939:  * @returns A promise that resolves to the file contents or an empty string.
940:  */
941: export async function safe_read_file_async(file_path: unknown): Promise<string> {
942:   const valid_path = validate_file_path_local(file_path);
943:   if (!valid_path) {
944:     return '';
945:   }
946: 
947:   try {
948:     return await fs.promises.readFile(valid_path, 'utf-8');
949:   } catch (err: unknown) {
950:     if (!(err instanceof Error && 'code' in err && get_error_code(err) === 'ENOENT')) {
951:       console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not read file ${valid_path}: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
952:     }
953:     return '';
954:   }
955: }
956: 
957: // Safely parse JSON with validation (CWE-502: Deserialization mitigation)
958: /**
959:  * Parses JSON with prototype-pollution safeguards.
960:  * @param json_string - The JSON string to parse.
961:  * @param default_value - The fallback value to return on failure.
962:  * @returns The parsed JSON value or the fallback.
963:  */
964: export function safe_parse_json(json_string: unknown, default_value = []): unknown {
965:   if (!json_string || typeof json_string !== 'string') {
966:     return default_value;
967:   }
968: 
969:   try {
970:     const parsed = JSON.parse(json_string);
971: 
972:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
973:       return parsed.filter(item =>
974:         item && typeof item === 'object' &&
975:         !Array.isArray(item) &&
976:         !('__proto__' in item) &&
977:         !('constructor' in item) &&
978:         !('prototype' in item)
979:       );
980:     }
981: 
982:     if (typeof parsed === 'object' && parsed !== null) {
983:       if ('__proto__' in parsed || 'constructor' in parsed || 'prototype' in parsed) {
984:         console.warn('[vector-index] Blocked potential prototype pollution in JSON');
985:         return default_value;
986:       }
987:     }
988: 
989:     return parsed;
990:   } catch (err: unknown) {
991:     console.warn(`[vector-index] JSON parse error: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
992:     return default_value;
993:   }
994: }

996: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
997:    3. DATABASE SINGLETON
998: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
999: 
1000: let db: Database.Database | null = null;
1001: let db_path = resolve_database_path();
1002: let sqlite_vec_available_flag = true;
1003: let active_vector_source: ActiveVectorSourceTelemetry | null = null;
1004: // C1 FIX: Key connections by resolved DB path to prevent cross-store data corruption
1005: const db_connections = new Map<string, Database.Database>();
1006: const UNCLEAN_SHUTDOWN_MARKER = '.unclean-shutdown';
1007: const CRASH_PROBE_RECEIPT_FILE = '.crash-probe-receipt';
1008: type DatabaseConnectionListener = (
1009:   database: Database.Database,
1010:   change: {
1011:     previousDb: Database.Database | null;
1012:     previousPath: string;
1013:     nextPath: string;
1014:   },
1015: ) => boolean | void;
1016: const database_connection_listeners = new Set<DatabaseConnectionListener>();
1017: 
1018: function set_active_database_connection(
1019:   connection: Database.Database,
1020:   target_path: string,
1021:   vec_available: boolean,
1022: ): void {
1023:   const previousDb = db;
1024:   const previousPath = db_path;
1025:   db = connection;
1026:   db_path = target_path;
1027:   sqlite_vec_available_flag = vec_available;
1028: 
1029:   if (previousDb !== connection || previousPath !== target_path) {
1030:     clear_constitutional_cache();
1031: 
1032:     const listenerErrors: string[] = [];
1033:     for (const listener of database_connection_listeners) {
1034:       try {
1035:         const listenerResult = listener(connection, {
1036:           previousDb,
1037:           previousPath,
1038:           nextPath: target_path,
1039:         });
1040:         if (listenerResult === false) {
1041:           listenerErrors.push('listener returned false');
1042:         }
1043:       } catch (error: unknown) {
1044:         const message = get_error_message(error);
1045:         listenerErrors.push(message);
1046:         console.warn(`[vector-index] Database connection listener failed: ${message}`);
1047:       }
1048:     }
1049:     if (listenerErrors.length > 0) {
1050:       throw new Error(`Database connection listener rebind failed: ${listenerErrors.join('; ')}`);
1051:     }
1052:   }
1053: 
1054:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
1055:     return;
1056:   }
1057: 
1058:   try {
1059:     fs.chmodSync(target_path, DB_PERMISSIONS);
1060:   } catch (err: unknown) {
1061:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Could not set permissions on ${target_path}: ${get_error_message(err)}`);
1062:   }
1063: }
1064: 
1065: function get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path: string): string | null {
1066:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
1067:     return null;
1068:   }
1069:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), UNCLEAN_SHUTDOWN_MARKER);
1070: }
1071: 
1072: function write_unclean_shutdown_marker(target_path: string): void {
1073:   const marker_path = get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path);
1074:   if (!marker_path) {
1075:     return;
1076:   }
1077: 
1078:   try {
1079:     fs.writeFileSync(marker_path, `${JSON.stringify({
1080:       pid: process.pid,
1081:       databasePath: target_path,
1082:       startedAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1083:     })}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
1084:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1085:     // Best-effort: marker failure must not block DB startup.
1086:   }
1087: }
1088: 
1089: function remove_unclean_shutdown_marker(target_path: string): void {
1090:   const marker_path = get_unclean_shutdown_marker_path(target_path);
1091:   if (!marker_path) {
1092:     return;
1093:   }
1094: 
1095:   try {
1096:     fs.rmSync(marker_path, { force: true });
1097:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1098:     // Best-effort: marker cleanup must not block DB close.
1099:   }
1100: }
1101: 
1102: function get_crash_probe_receipt_path(target_path: string): string | null {
1103:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
1104:     return null;
1105:   }
1106:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), CRASH_PROBE_RECEIPT_FILE);
1107: }
1108: 
1109: // Fingerprint of the on-disk DB state (main + WAL file size/mtime). Any write,
1110: // checkpoint, or WAL change alters it, so a stale receipt can never mask a
1111: // post-write corruption: on-disk corruption requires a change that invalidates
1112: // this fingerprint, which forces a fresh probe.
1113: function compute_db_state_fingerprint(target_path: string): string | null {
1114:   try {
1115:     const main = fs.statSync(target_path);
1116:     let walPart = 'nowal';
1117:     try {
1118:       const wal = fs.statSync(`${target_path}-wal`);
1119:       walPart = `${wal.size}:${Math.floor(wal.mtimeMs)}`;
1120:     } catch (_walError: unknown) { /* WAL absent — fingerprint reflects that */ }
1121:     return `${main.size}:${Math.floor(main.mtimeMs)}|${walPart}`;
1122:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1123:     return null;
1124:   }
1125: }
1126: 
1127: function read_crash_probe_receipt(target_path: string): { fingerprint: string; verdict: string } | null {
1128:   const receipt_path = get_crash_probe_receipt_path(target_path);
1129:   if (!receipt_path) {
1130:     return null;
1131:   }
1132:   try {
1133:     const raw = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(receipt_path, 'utf8')) as { fingerprint?: unknown; verdict?: unknown };
1134:     if (typeof raw.fingerprint === 'string' && typeof raw.verdict === 'string') {
1135:       return { fingerprint: raw.fingerprint, verdict: raw.verdict };
1136:     }
1137:   } catch (_error: unknown) { /* missing/corrupt receipt — treat as absent (probe runs) */ }
1138:   return null;
1139: }
1140: 
1141: function write_crash_probe_receipt(target_path: string, fingerprint: string): void {
1142:   const receipt_path = get_crash_probe_receipt_path(target_path);
1143:   if (!receipt_path) {
1144:     return;
1145:   }
1146:   try {
1147:     fs.writeFileSync(
1148:       receipt_path,
1149:       `${JSON.stringify({ fingerprint, verdict: 'ok', probedAt: new Date().toISOString() })}\n`,
1150:       { mode: 0o600 },
1151:     );
1152:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1153:     // Best-effort: receipt failure must not block DB startup (probe just reruns).
1154:   }
1155: }
1156: 
1157: function remove_sqlite_sidecars(database_path: string): void {
1158:   for (const sidecar_path of [`${database_path}-wal`, `${database_path}-shm`]) {
1159:     fs.rmSync(sidecar_path, { force: true });
1160:   }
1161: }
1162: 
1163: function fsync_file_if_possible(file_path: string): void {
1164:   let fd: number | null = null;
1165:   try {
1166:     fd = fs.openSync(file_path, 'r');
1167:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
1168:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1169:     // Recovery remains journal-driven when the platform cannot flush explicitly.
1170:   } finally {
1171:     if (fd !== null) {
1172:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1173:     }
1174:   }
1175: }
1176: 
1177: function fsync_directory_if_possible(dir_path: string): void {
1178:   let fd: number | null = null;
1179:   try {
1180:     fd = fs.openSync(dir_path, 'r');
1181:     fs.fsyncSync(fd);
1182:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
1183:     // Directory fsync is unavailable on some platforms/filesystems.
1184:   } finally {
1185:     if (fd !== null) {
1186:       try { fs.closeSync(fd); } catch (_error: unknown) { /* best-effort */ }
1187:     }
1188:   }
1189: }
1190: 
1191: function get_restore_journal_path(target_path: string): string | null {
1192:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
1193:     return null;
1194:   }
1195:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), 'checkpoints', RESTORE_JOURNAL_NAME);
1196: }
1197: 
1198: function get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path: string): string | null {
1199:   if (target_path === ':memory:') {
1200:     return null;
1201:   }
1202:   return path.join(path.dirname(target_path), 'checkpoints', NEEDS_REBUILD_SENTINEL_NAME);
1203: }
1204: 
1205: function get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path(shardPath: string): string | null {
1206:   if (shardPath === ':memory:') {
1207:     return null;
1208:   }
1209:   const shardDir = path.dirname(shardPath);
1210:   const baseDir = path.basename(shardDir) === 'vectors' ? path.dirname(shardDir) : shardDir;
1211:   return path.join(
1212:     baseDir,
1213:     'checkpoints',
1214:     `.${path.basename(shardPath)}${VECTOR_SHARD_REPAIR_PENDING_SENTINEL_SUFFIX}`,
1215:   );
1216: }
1217: 
1218: function write_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(
1219:   shardPath: string,
1220:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
1221:   reason: string,
1222:   quarantinePath: string,
1223: ): boolean {
1224:   const sentinelPath = get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path(shardPath);
1225:   if (!sentinelPath) {
1226:     return true;
1227:   }
1228:   try {
1229:     const sentinelDir = path.dirname(sentinelPath);
1230:     fs.mkdirSync(sentinelDir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
1231:     const tempSentinelPath = `${sentinelPath}.tmp.${process.pid}`;
1232:     fs.writeFileSync(tempSentinelPath, `${JSON.stringify({
1233:       formatVersion: 1,
1234:       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1235:       source: 'vector_shard_quarantine',
1236:       reason,
1237:       shardPath,
1238:       quarantinePath,
1239:       profile: {
1240:         provider: profile.provider,
1241:         model: profile.model,
1242:         dim: profile.dim,
1243:         dtype: profile.dtype,
1244:       },
1245:     } satisfies VectorShardRepairPendingSentinelFile, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
1246:     fsync_file_if_possible(tempSentinelPath);
1247:     fs.renameSync(tempSentinelPath, sentinelPath);
1248:     fsync_directory_if_possible(sentinelDir);
1249:     return true;
1250:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1251:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to write vector shard repair sentinel: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1252:     return false;
1253:   }
1254: }
1255: 
1256: function read_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(
1257:   shardPath: string,
1258: ): VectorShardRepairPendingSentinel | null {
1259:   const sentinelPath = get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path(shardPath);
1260:   if (!sentinelPath || !fs.existsSync(sentinelPath)) {
1261:     return null;
1262:   }
1263:   try {
1264:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(sentinelPath, 'utf-8')) as Partial<VectorShardRepairPendingSentinelFile>;
1265:     return {
1266:       reason: typeof parsed.reason === 'string' && parsed.reason.length > 0
1267:         ? parsed.reason
1268:         : 'pending vector shard repair sentinel',
1269:       quarantinePath: typeof parsed.quarantinePath === 'string' && parsed.quarantinePath.length > 0
1270:         ? parsed.quarantinePath
1271:         : null,
1272:     };
1273:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1274:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Ignoring unreadable vector shard repair sentinel: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1275:     return { reason: 'unreadable vector shard repair sentinel', quarantinePath: null };
1276:   }
1277: }
1278: 
1279: export function clear_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel(shardPath: string): void {
1280:   const sentinelPath = get_vector_shard_repair_pending_sentinel_path(shardPath);
1281:   if (!sentinelPath || !fs.existsSync(sentinelPath)) {
1282:     return;
1283:   }
1284:   try {
1285:     fs.unlinkSync(sentinelPath);
1286:     fsync_directory_if_possible(path.dirname(sentinelPath));
1287:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1288:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to clear vector shard repair sentinel: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1289:   }
1290: }
1291: 
1292: function resume_vector_shard_repair_from_sentinel(
1293:   database: Database.Database,
1294:   profile: EmbeddingProfile,
1295:   shardPath: string,
1296:   sentinel: { reason: string; quarantinePath: string | null },
1297: ): void {
1298:   const reason = `${sentinel.reason}; pending vector shard repair sentinel`;
1299:   recordVectorShardQuarantined({
1300:     shardPath,
1301:     quarantinePath: sentinel.quarantinePath ?? shardPath,
1302:     reason,
1303:     sentinelPersisted: true,
1304:   });
1305:   schedule_vector_shard_rebuild(database, profile, shardPath, reason);
1306: }
1307: 
1308: function write_needs_rebuild_sentinel_for_recovered_restore(target_path: string, checkpoint_name: string): void {
1309:   const sentinel_path = get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path);
1310:   if (!sentinel_path || fs.existsSync(sentinel_path)) {
1311:     return;
1312:   }
1313: 
1314:   try {
1315:     const sentinel_dir = path.dirname(sentinel_path);
1316:     const temp_sentinel_path = `${sentinel_path}.tmp`;
1317:     fs.mkdirSync(sentinel_dir, { recursive: true, mode: 0o700 });
1318:     fs.writeFileSync(temp_sentinel_path, `${JSON.stringify({
1319:       formatVersion: 1,
1320:       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1321:       source: 'swap_done_recovery',
1322:       reason: 'completed restore journal recovered without derived rebuild evidence',
1323:       checkpointName: checkpoint_name,
1324:     }, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
1325:     fsync_file_if_possible(temp_sentinel_path);
1326:     fs.renameSync(temp_sentinel_path, sentinel_path);
1327:     fsync_directory_if_possible(sentinel_dir);
1328:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1329:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to write checkpoint needs-rebuild sentinel during recovery: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1330:   }
1331: }
1332: 
1333: function write_needs_rebuild_sentinel_for_corruption(target_path: string, detail: string): void {
1334:   // Same atomic temp+rename discipline as the restore-recovery writer: a crash
1335:   // mid-write must never leave a torn sentinel that half-triggers a rebuild.
1336:   const sentinel_path = get_needs_rebuild_sentinel_path(target_path);
1337:   if (!sentinel_path) {
1338:     return;
1339:   }
1340:   try {
1341:     const sentinel_dir = path.dirname(sentinel_path);
1342:     fs.mkdirSync(sentinel_dir, { recursive: true });
1343:     const temp_sentinel_path = `${sentinel_path}.tmp.${process.pid}`;
1344:     fs.writeFileSync(temp_sentinel_path, `${JSON.stringify({
1345:       formatVersion: 1,
1346:       createdAt: new Date().toISOString(),
1347:       source: 'post_crash_integrity_probe',
1348:       reason: detail,
1349:     }, null, 2)}\n`, { mode: 0o600 });
1350:     fsync_file_if_possible(temp_sentinel_path);
1351:     fs.renameSync(temp_sentinel_path, sentinel_path);
1352:     fsync_directory_if_possible(sentinel_dir);
1353:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1354:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to write needs-rebuild sentinel after integrity probe: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1355:   }
1356: }
1357: 
1358: function read_restore_journal(journal_path: string): RestoreJournalFile | null {
1359:   try {
1360:     const parsed = JSON.parse(fs.readFileSync(journal_path, 'utf-8')) as Partial<RestoreJournalFile>;
1361:     const phase = parsed.phase === 'swap-done' ? 'swap-done' : 'swap-pending';
1362:     if (
1363:       parsed.formatVersion !== 1
1364:       || typeof parsed.createdAt !== 'string'
1365:       || typeof parsed.checkpointName !== 'string'
1366:       || typeof parsed.liveMainPath !== 'string'
1367:       || typeof parsed.backupMainPath !== 'string'
1368:       || typeof parsed.snapshotMainPath !== 'string'
1369:       || (parsed.liveShardPath !== null && typeof parsed.liveShardPath !== 'string')
1370:       || (parsed.backupShardPath !== null && typeof parsed.backupShardPath !== 'string')
1371:       || (parsed.snapshotVecPath !== null && typeof parsed.snapshotVecPath !== 'string')
1372:       || typeof parsed.shouldRestoreVec !== 'boolean'
1373:       || (
1374:         parsed.liveShardPreexisted !== undefined
1375:         && typeof parsed.liveShardPreexisted !== 'boolean'
1376:       )
1377:     ) {
1378:       throw new Error('restore journal shape is invalid');
1379:     }
1380:     return {
1381:       ...parsed,
1382:       phase,
1383:       liveShardPreexisted: parsed.liveShardPreexisted
1384:         ?? (parsed.shouldRestoreVec && typeof parsed.backupShardPath === 'string' && fs.existsSync(parsed.backupShardPath)),
1385:     } as RestoreJournalFile;
1386:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1387:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Ignoring unreadable checkpoint restore journal: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1388:     return null;
1389:   }
1390: }
1391: 
1392: function merge_checkpoint_catalog_from_backup(live_main_path: string, backup_main_path: string): void {
1393:   // A swap-done crash happens after the snapshot DB is in place but before the live

1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Orchestrator
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // 4-stage pipeline execution with per-stage error handling and timeouts.
5: //
6: // Each stage is wrapped in try/catch with withTimeout().
7: // Stage 1 is mandatory (throws on failure — no candidates = no results).
8: // Stages 2-4 fall back to previous stage output with degraded metadata.
9: // Timing is recorded for latency observability.
10: //
11: // I/O CONTRACT:
12: // Input:  PipelineConfig (query, embedding, limits, flags, intent, session)
13: // Output: PipelineResult { results: Stage4ReadonlyRow[], metadata, annotations, trace }
14: // Key invariants:
15: //     - results are scored by Stage 2, reranked by Stage 3, filtered by Stage 4
16: //     - Score fields are frozen after Stage 3; Stage 4 output scores == Stage 3 output scores
17: //     - Stage metadata for all four stages is included for observability
18: //     - When a stage degrades, metadata.degraded = true and timing tracks each stage
19: // Side effects:
20: //     - Delegates to each stage; see individual stage modules for their side effects
21: 
22: import type {
23:   PipelineConfig,
24:   PipelineResult,
25:   Stage1Output,
26:   Stage2Output,
27:   Stage3Output,
28:   Stage4Output,
29:   Stage4ReadonlyRow,
30:   SignalStatus,
31: } from './types.js';
32: 
33: import { executeStage1 } from './stage1-candidate-gen.js';
34: import { executeStage2 } from './stage2-fusion.js';
35: import { executeStage3 } from './stage3-rerank.js';
36: import { executeStage4 } from './stage4-filter.js';
37: import { MemoryError, withTimeout } from '../../errors/core.js';
38: 
39: // Feature catalog: 4-stage pipeline architecture
40: // Feature catalog: 4-stage pipeline refactor
41: 
42: 
43: /** Per-stage timeout in milliseconds. */
44: const STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS = 10_000;
45: 
46: /**
47:  * Execute the 4-stage retrieval pipeline with per-stage error handling.
48:  *
49:  * Stage 1: Candidate Generation — MANDATORY (throws on failure)
50:  * Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration — falls back to unsorted candidates
51:  * Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate — falls back to unranked scored results
52:  * Stage 4: Filter + Annotate — falls back to unfiltered results
53:  *
54:  * @param config Pipeline configuration derived from search args
55:  * @returns Pipeline result with stage metadata and timing
56:  */
57: export async function executePipeline(config: PipelineConfig): Promise<PipelineResult> {
58:   const timing: Record<string, number> = {};
59:   const pipelineStart = Date.now();
60:   let degraded = false;
61: 
62:   // -- Stage 1: Candidate Generation (MANDATORY — no fallback possible) --
63:   let stage1Result: Stage1Output;
64:   try {
65:     const t0 = Date.now();
66:     stage1Result = await withTimeout(
67:       executeStage1({ config }),
68:       STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
69:       'Stage 1: Candidate Generation',
70:     );
71:     timing.stage1 = Date.now() - t0;
72:   } catch (err) {
73:     throw new MemoryError(
74:       'PIPELINE_STAGE1_FAILED',
75:       `Candidate generation failed: ${err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)}`,
76:       { cause: err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err) },
77:     );
78:   }
79: 
80:   // -- Stage 2: Fusion + Signal Integration (falls back to unsorted candidates) --
81:   let stage2Result: Stage2Output;
82:   try {
83:     const t0 = Date.now();
84:     stage2Result = await withTimeout(
85:       executeStage2({
86:         candidates: stage1Result.candidates,
87:         config,
88:         stage1Metadata: stage1Result.metadata,
89:       }),
90:       STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
91:       'Stage 2: Fusion',
92:     );
93:     timing.stage2 = Date.now() - t0;
94:   } catch (err) {
95:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
96:     console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 2 failed, returning unscored candidates: ${msg}`);
97:     degraded = true;
98:     stage2Result = {
99:       scored: stage1Result.candidates,
100:       metadata: {
101:         sessionBoostApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
102:         causalBoostApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
103:         intentWeightsApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
104:         artifactRoutingApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
105:         feedbackSignalsApplied: 'failed' as SignalStatus,
106:         qualityFiltered: 0,
107:         durationMs: 0,
108:       },
109:     };
110:     timing.stage2 = 0;
111:   }
112: 
113:   // -- Stage 3: Rerank + Aggregate (falls back to unranked scored results) --
114:   let stage3Result: Stage3Output;
115:   try {
116:     const t0 = Date.now();
117:     stage3Result = await withTimeout(
118:       executeStage3({
119:         scored: stage2Result.scored,
120:         config,
121:       }),
122:       STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
123:       'Stage 3: Rerank',
124:     );
125:     timing.stage3 = Date.now() - t0;
126:   } catch (err) {
127:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
128:     console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 3 failed, returning unranked results: ${msg}`);
129:     degraded = true;
130:     stage3Result = {
131:       reranked: stage2Result.scored,
132:       metadata: {
133:         rerankApplied: false,
134:         chunkReassemblyStats: {
135:           collapsedChunkHits: 0,
136:           chunkParents: 0,
137:           reassembled: 0,
138:           fallback: 0,
139:         },
140:         durationMs: 0,
141:       },
142:     };
143:     timing.stage3 = 0;
144:   }
145: 
146:   // -- Stage 4: Filter + Annotate (falls back to unfiltered results) --
147:   let stage4Result: Stage4Output;
148:   try {
149:     const t0 = Date.now();
150:     stage4Result = await withTimeout(
151:       executeStage4({
152:         results: stage3Result.reranked as Stage4ReadonlyRow[],
153:         config,
154:         stage1Metadata: { constitutionalInjected: stage1Result.metadata.constitutionalInjected },
155:       }),
156:       STAGE_TIMEOUT_MS,
157:       'Stage 4: Filter',
158:     );
159:     timing.stage4 = Date.now() - t0;
160:   } catch (err) {
161:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
162:     console.warn(`[pipeline] Stage 4 failed, returning unfiltered results: ${msg}`);
163:     degraded = true;
164:     stage4Result = {
165:       final: stage3Result.reranked as Stage4ReadonlyRow[],
166:       metadata: {
167:         stateFiltered: 0,
168:         constitutionalInjected: stage1Result.metadata.constitutionalInjected,
169:         evidenceGapDetected: false,
170:         durationMs: 0,
171:       },
172:       annotations: {
173:         stateStats: {},
174:         featureFlags: {},
175:       },
176:     };
177:     timing.stage4 = 0;
178:   }
179: 
180:   timing.total = Date.now() - pipelineStart;
181: 
182:   return {
183:     results: stage4Result.final,
184:     metadata: {
185:       stage1: stage1Result.metadata,
186:       stage2: stage2Result.metadata,
187:       stage3: stage3Result.metadata,
188:       stage4: stage4Result.metadata,
189:       timing,
190:       degraded: degraded || undefined,
191:     },
192:     annotations: stage4Result.annotations,
193:     trace: config.trace,
194:   };
195: }

255: function hasIndex(database: Database.Database, indexName: string): boolean {
256:   const row = database.prepare(
257:     "SELECT 1 AS present FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='index' AND name = ?"
258:   ).get(indexName) as { present?: number } | undefined;
259: 
260:   return row?.present === 1;
261: }
262: 
263: function getTableColumns(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string[] {
264:   return (database.prepare(`PRAGMA table_info(${tableName})`).all() as Array<{ name: string }>)
265:     .map((column) => column.name)
266:     .filter((columnName) => typeof columnName === 'string' && columnName.length > 0);
267: }
268: 
269: function getTableSql(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): string | null {
270:   const row = database.prepare(
271:     "SELECT sql FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name = ?"
272:   ).get(tableName) as { sql?: string } | undefined;
273: 
274:   return typeof row?.sql === 'string' ? row.sql : null;
275: }
276: 
277: function hasEmbeddingCacheDimensionsPrimaryKey(database: Database.Database): boolean {
278:   const tableSql = getTableSql(database, 'embedding_cache');
279:   if (typeof tableSql !== 'string') {
280:     return false;
281:   }
282: 
283:   return /PRIMARY\s+KEY\s*\(\s*content_hash\s*,\s*model_id\s*,\s*dimensions\s*\)/i.test(tableSql)
284:     || /PRIMARY\s+KEY\s*\(\s*content_hash\s*,\s*profile_key\s*,\s*input_kind\s*,\s*model_id\s*,\s*dimensions\s*\)/i.test(tableSql);
285: }

328: function createRequiredIndex(
329:   database: Database.Database,
330:   indexName: string,
331:   sql: string,
332:   context: string,
333: ): void {
334:   try {
335:     database.exec(sql);
336:   } catch (error: unknown) {
337:     if (hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
338:       logger.warn(`${context}: reusing existing index after DDL warning`, {
339:         indexName,
340:         error: get_error_message(error),
341:       });
342:       return;
343:     }
344:     throw error;
345:   }
346: 
347:   if (!hasIndex(database, indexName)) {
348:     throw new Error(`${context}: expected index "${indexName}" to exist after migration`);
349:   }
350: }

358:       new_memory_hash TEXT,

360:       existing_memory_id INTEGER,
361:       similarity REAL,
362:       reason TEXT,
363:       new_content_preview TEXT,
364:       existing_content_preview TEXT,

366:       contradiction_type TEXT,
367:       spec_folder TEXT,
368:       created_at TEXT DEFAULT CURRENT_TIMESTAMP,

392: function getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(
393:   availableColumns: Set<string>,
394:   candidates: string[],
395:   fallback: string,
396: ): string {
397:   for (const candidate of candidates) {
398:     if (availableColumns.has(candidate)) {
399:       return candidate;
400:     }
401:   }
402:   return fallback;
403: }

405: function hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): boolean {
406:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
407:     return false;
408:   }
409: 
410:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts'));
411:   return REQUIRED_MEMORY_CONFLICT_COLUMNS.every((columnName) => columns.has(columnName));
412: }

414: function migrateMemoryConflictsTable(database: Database.Database): void {
415:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_conflicts')) {
416:     createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
417:     return;
418:   }
419: 
420:   if (hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
421:     return;
422:   }
423: 
424:   database.exec('ALTER TABLE memory_conflicts RENAME TO memory_conflicts_legacy');
425:   createMemoryConflictsTable(database);
426: 
427:   const legacyColumns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_conflicts_legacy'));
428:   database.exec(`
429:     INSERT INTO memory_conflicts (
430:       id,
431:       timestamp,
432:       action,
433:       new_memory_hash,
434:       new_memory_id,
435:       existing_memory_id,
436:       similarity,
437:       reason,
438:       new_content_preview,
439:       existing_content_preview,
440:       contradiction_detected,
441:       contradiction_type,
442:       spec_folder,
443:       created_at
444:     )
445:     SELECT
446:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['id'], 'NULL')},
447:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['timestamp', 'created_at'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')},
448:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['action'], 'NULL')},
449:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_hash'], 'NULL')},
450:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
451:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_memory_id'], 'NULL')},
452:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['similarity', 'similarity_score'], 'NULL')},
453:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['reason', 'notes'], 'NULL')},
454:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['new_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
455:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['existing_content_preview'], 'NULL')},
456:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_detected'], '0')},
457:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['contradiction_type'], 'NULL')},
458:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['spec_folder'], 'NULL')},
459:       ${getFirstAvailableColumnExpression(legacyColumns, ['created_at', 'timestamp'], 'CURRENT_TIMESTAMP')}
460:     FROM memory_conflicts_legacy
461:   `);
462:   database.exec('DROP TABLE memory_conflicts_legacy');
463: 
464:   if (!hasUnifiedMemoryConflictsTable(database)) {
465:     throw new Error('Migration v12: memory_conflicts table is still missing unified columns after rebuild');
466:   }
467: }

469: function summarizeCompatibilityFailures(report: SchemaCompatibilityReport): string {
470:   const details: string[] = [];
471: 
472:   if (report.missingTables.length > 0) {
473:     details.push(`missing tables: ${report.missingTables.join(', ')}`);
474:   }
475:   if (Object.keys(report.missingColumns).length > 0) {
476:     const missingColumns = Object.entries(report.missingColumns)
477:       .map(([tableName, columns]) => `${tableName}[${columns.join(', ')}]`)
478:       .join('; ');
479:     details.push(`missing columns: ${missingColumns}`);
480:   }
481:   if (report.missingIndexes.length > 0) {
482:     details.push(`missing indexes: ${report.missingIndexes.join(', ')}`);
483:   }
484:   if (report.constraintMismatches.length > 0) {
485:     details.push(`constraint mismatches: ${report.constraintMismatches.join(', ')}`);
486:   }
487: 
488:   return details.join(' | ');
489: }

495: }

546: function ensureMemoryChunkMetadataColumns(database: Database.Database, context: string): void {
547:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
548:     logger.warn(`${context}: memory_index missing; skipping chunk metadata columns`);
549:     return;
550:   }
551: 
552:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
553:   const chunkColumns: Array<{ name: string; sql: string }> = [
554:     { name: 'chunk_id', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN chunk_id TEXT' },
555:     { name: 'chunk_fingerprint', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN chunk_fingerprint TEXT' },
556:     { name: 'chunk_kind', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN chunk_kind TEXT' },
557:     { name: 'chunk_start_line', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN chunk_start_line INTEGER' },
558:     { name: 'chunk_end_line', sql: 'ALTER TABLE memory_index ADD COLUMN chunk_end_line INTEGER' },
559:   ];
560: 
561:   for (const column of chunkColumns) {
562:     if (columns.has(column.name)) {
563:       continue;
564:     }
565:     try {
566:       database.exec(column.sql);
567:       columns.add(column.name);
568:       logger.info(`${context}: Added memory_index.${column.name}`);
569:     } catch (error: unknown) {
570:       if (!get_error_message(error).includes('duplicate column')) {
571:         throw error;
572:       }
573:       logDuplicateColumnMigrationSkip(column.name, error);
574:     }
575:   }
576: 
577:   createRequiredIndex(database, 'idx_memory_chunk_identity', MEMORY_CHUNK_IDENTITY_INDEX_SQL, context);
578:   createRequiredIndex(database, 'idx_memory_chunk_fingerprint', MEMORY_CHUNK_FINGERPRINT_INDEX_SQL, context);

725: function sourceKindFromProvenanceSource(value: unknown): 'human' | 'agent' | 'system' | 'import' | 'feedback' {
726:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
727:     return 'system';
728:   }
729: 
730:   const normalized = value.trim().toLowerCase();
731:   if (/\b(human|manual|user|operator|author)\b/.test(normalized)) {
732:     return 'human';
733:   }
734:   if (normalized.includes('feedback') || normalized.includes('validate') || normalized.includes('validation')) {
735:     return 'feedback';
736:   }
737:   if (normalized.includes('import') || normalized.includes('ingest') || normalized.includes('scan') || normalized.includes('index')) {
738:     return 'import';
739:   }
740:   if (/\b(agent|assistant|claude|codex|opencode|automation|bot)\b/.test(normalized)) {
741:     return 'agent';
742:   }
743:   return 'system';
744: }

746: function ensureMemoryIndexSourceKindColumn(database: Database.Database, context: string): void {
747:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
748:     logger.warn(`${context}: memory_index missing; skipping source_kind column`);
749:     return;
750:   }
751: 
752:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
753:   if (!columns.has('source_kind')) {
754:     try {
755:       database.exec(SOURCE_KIND_COLUMN_SQL);
756:       columns.add('source_kind');
757:       logger.info(`${context}: Added memory_index.source_kind`);
758:     } catch (error: unknown) {
759:       if (!get_error_message(error).includes('duplicate column')) {
760:         throw error;
761:       }
762:       logDuplicateColumnMigrationSkip('source_kind', error);
763:     }
764:   }
765: 
766:   if (columns.has('provenance_source')) {
767:     const rows = database.prepare(`
768:       SELECT id, provenance_source
769:       FROM memory_index
770:       WHERE source_kind IS NULL OR TRIM(source_kind) = ''
771:         OR source_kind NOT IN ('human', 'agent', 'system', 'import', 'feedback')
772:         OR (source_kind = 'system' AND provenance_source IS NOT NULL AND TRIM(provenance_source) != '')
773:     `).all() as Array<{ id: number; provenance_source: string | null }>;
774:     const update = database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET source_kind = ? WHERE id = ?');
775:     for (const row of rows) {
776:       update.run(sourceKindFromProvenanceSource(row.provenance_source), row.id);
777:     }
778:   } else {
779:     database.prepare(`
780:       UPDATE memory_index
781:       SET source_kind = 'system'
782:       WHERE source_kind IS NULL OR TRIM(source_kind) = ''
783:         OR source_kind NOT IN ('human', 'agent', 'system', 'import', 'feedback')
784:     `).run();
785:   }
786: }

838: /**
839:  * Deprecate-before-create pre-pass for the active-row logical-key unique index.
840:  *
841:  * A pre-existing database can already hold duplicate active rows for one logical key
842:  * (e.g. created before the supersede-on-reindex guard existed). Creating the UNIQUE
843:  * index over those rows throws, and since all migrations share one transaction, the
844:  * whole upgrade batch rolls back and the schema wedges. To advance safely we retire the
845:  * duplicates first — deprecate, never delete — by setting the losers' importance_tier to
846:  * 'deprecated' (the same retirement the runtime reindex guard uses), which removes them
847:  * from the partial index predicate so the UNIQUE index can be built. Within each duplicate
848:  * group the winner is the highest importance tier, then the most recently updated row.
849:  *
850:  * Grouping mirrors the index exactly. Tolerant when a scope/anchor column is absent on an
851:  * older schema (the group key falls back to the empty string for the missing column, the
852:  * same as the index's COALESCE), and a no-op when the table or needed columns are missing.
853:  */
854: function deprecateDuplicateActiveLogicalKeysBeforeUniqueIndex(database: Database.Database): void {
855:   if (!hasTable(database, 'memory_index')) {
856:     return;
857:   }
858: 
859:   const columns = new Set(getTableColumns(database, 'memory_index'));
860:   // canonical_file_path / file_path and importance_tier are required to form the key and
861:   // pick a winner; without them this DB predates the guarded schema and there is nothing
862:   // to reconcile here.
863:   if (!columns.has('importance_tier') || (!columns.has('canonical_file_path') && !columns.has('file_path'))) {
864:     return;
865:   }
866: 
867:   // Build per-column key fragments that match the index's COALESCE expressions, degrading
868:   // to a literal '' for columns this older schema does not have (so the key shape is stable).
869:   const pathExpr = columns.has('canonical_file_path')
870:     ? "COALESCE(NULLIF(canonical_file_path, ''), file_path)"
871:     : 'file_path';
872:   const anchorExpr = columns.has('anchor_id')
873:     ? "COALESCE(NULLIF(TRIM(anchor_id), ''), '_')"
874:     : "'_'";
875:   const scopeExpr = (col: string): string => (columns.has(col) ? `COALESCE(${col}, '')` : "''");
876: 
877:   const keyExpr = [
878:     'spec_folder',
879:     pathExpr,
880:     anchorExpr,
881:     scopeExpr('tenant_id'),
882:     scopeExpr('user_id'),
883:     scopeExpr('agent_id'),
884:     scopeExpr('session_id'),
885:   ].join(" || '\\u0000' || ");
886: 
887:   // Active = the same predicate the unique index uses (excludes constitutional + deprecated).
888:   const activePredicate = "COALESCE(importance_tier, 'normal') NOT IN ('constitutional', 'deprecated')";
889: 
890:   // Tier rank mirrors IMPORTANCE_TIERS values so the highest-priority active row wins.
891:   const tierRankExpr = `CASE COALESCE(importance_tier, 'normal')
892:         WHEN 'critical' THEN 5
893:         WHEN 'important' THEN 4
894:         WHEN 'normal' THEN 3
895:         WHEN 'temporary' THEN 2
896:         ELSE 1
897:       END`;
898: 
899:   // Find groups with more than one active row, then deprecate every row except the winner
900:   // (highest tier rank, then most recently updated, then highest id as a stable tiebreak).
901:   const retireStmt = database.prepare(`
902:     UPDATE memory_index
903:     SET importance_tier = 'deprecated',
904:         updated_at = datetime('now')
905:     WHERE id IN (
906:       SELECT id FROM (
907:         SELECT id,
908:                (${keyExpr}) AS logical_key,
909:                ROW_NUMBER() OVER (
910:                  PARTITION BY (${keyExpr})
911:                  ORDER BY ${tierRankExpr} DESC, updated_at DESC, id DESC
912:                ) AS rn
913:         FROM memory_index
914:         WHERE ${activePredicate}
915:       )
916:       WHERE rn > 1
917:     )
918:   `);
919: 
920:   const result = retireStmt.run() as { changes: number };
921:   if (result.changes > 0) {
922:     logger.warn(
923:       `Migration v28: deprecated ${result.changes} duplicate active row(s) before building idx_memory_logical_key_active_unique`,
924:     );
925:   }
926: }
