1: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
2: // MODULE: Stage1 Candidate Gen
3: // ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
4: // 4-stage retrieval pipeline architecture
5: //
6: // Responsibility: Execute search channels and collect raw candidate results.
7: // This stage avoids downstream fusion/reranking, but may apply temporal
8: // Contiguity to raw vector-channel hits before later pipeline stages.
9: // Results are collected from the appropriate search channel based on search type.
10: //
11: // Search channels handled:
12: //   - multi-concept: Generate per-concept embeddings, run multiConceptSearch
13: //   - hybrid (deep mode): Query expansion + multi-variant hybrid search + dedup
14: // hybrid: Embedding-based query expansion (SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_EXPANSION)
15: // Suppressed when classifies query as "simple" (mutual exclusion)
16: //   - hybrid: collectRawCandidates → falls back to vector on failure
17: //   - vector: Direct vectorSearch
18: //
19: // Post-channel operations:
20: //   - Constitutional memory injection (if not already present)
21: //   - Quality score filtering
22: //   - Tier and contextType filtering
23: //
24: // I/O CONTRACT:
25: // Input:  Stage1Input { config: PipelineConfig }
26: // Output: Stage1Output { candidates: PipelineRow[], metadata }
27: // Key invariants:
28: //     - candidates contains raw channel scores; vector hits may include an
29: //       optional temporal-contiguity boost applied before downstream fusion
30: //     - Constitutional rows are always present when includeConstitutional=true and no tier filter
31: //     - All rows pass qualityThreshold (if set) and tier/contextType filters
32: // Side effects:
33: //     - Generates query embeddings via the embeddings provider (external call)
34: //     - Reads from the vector index and FTS5 / BM25 index (DB reads only)
35: //
36: import type { Stage1Input, Stage1Output, PipelineRow } from './types.js';
37: import { resolveEffectiveScore } from './types.js';
38: import * as vectorIndex from '../vector-index.js';
39: import * as hybridSearch from '../hybrid-search.js';
40: import { vectorSearchWithContiguity } from '../../cognitive/temporal-contiguity.js';
41: import { isMultiQueryEnabled, isEmbeddingExpansionEnabled, isMemorySummariesEnabled, isQueryDecompositionEnabled, isGraphConceptRoutingEnabled, isLlmReformulationEnabled, isHyDEEnabled, isQuerySurrogatesEnabled, isTemporalContiguityEnabled, isQueryConceptExpansionEnabled } from '../search-flags.js';
42: import { expandQuery } from '../query-expander.js';
43: import { expandQueryWithEmbeddings, isExpansionActive } from '../embedding-expansion.js';
44: import { querySummaryEmbeddings, checkScaleGate } from '../memory-summaries.js';
45: import { addTraceEntry } from '@spec-kit/shared/contracts/retrieval-trace';
46: import { requireDb } from '../../../utils/db-helpers.js';
47: import { filterRowsByScope } from '../../governance/scope-governance.js';
48: import { withTimeout } from '../../errors/core.js';
49: import { GRAPH_METADATA_MIGRATED_QUALITY_FLAG } from '../../graph/graph-metadata-schema.js';
50: import { computeBackfillQualityScore } from '../../validation/save-quality-gate.js';
51: import {
52:   isMultiFacet,
53:   decompose,
54:   mergeByFacetCoverage as mergeFacetCoveragePools,
55:   MAX_FACETS,
56: } from '../query-decomposer.js';
57: import { routeQueryConcepts, nounPhrases, getConceptExpansionTerms } from '../entity-linker.js';
58: import { cheapSeedRetrieve, llm, fanout } from '../llm-reformulation.js';
59: import { runHyDE } from '../hyde.js';
60: import { matchSurrogates } from '../query-surrogates.js';
61: import { loadSurrogatesBatch } from '../surrogate-storage.js';
62: 
63: // Feature catalog: 4-stage pipeline architecture
64: // Feature catalog: Hybrid search pipeline
65: 
66: 
67: // -- Constants --
68: 
69: /** Maximum number of deep-mode query variants to generate (original + expanded). */
70: const MAX_DEEP_QUERY_VARIANTS = 3;
71: 
72: /** F1: Deep-mode expansion timeout — fall back to base query if variants take too long. */
73: const DEEP_EXPANSION_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;
74: 
75: /** Minimum cosine similarity for multi-concept search. */
76: const MULTI_CONCEPT_MIN_SIMILARITY = 0.5;
77: 
78: /** Number of constitutional results to fetch when none appear in hybrid/vector results. */
79: const CONSTITUTIONAL_INJECT_LIMIT = 5;
80: 
81: /** Number of similar memories to mine for embedding-based query expansion terms. */
82: const DEFAULT_EXPANSION_CANDIDATE_LIMIT = 5;
83: 
84: /** D2: Timeout for facet decomposition parallel searches (ms). */
85: const DECOMPOSITION_TIMEOUT_MS = 5000;
86: const MAX_QUERY_DECOMPOSITION_FACETS = MAX_FACETS;
87: 
88: // -- Helper Functions --
89: 
90: /**
91:  * Filter results by a minimum quality score threshold.
92:  *
93:  * - If no threshold is provided or it is not a finite number, all results pass.
94:  * - Threshold is clamped to [0, 1].
95:  * - Rows with a missing or non-finite `quality_score` are treated as 0.
96:  *
97:  * @param results - Candidate rows to filter.
98:  * @param threshold - Minimum quality score in [0, 1] (inclusive).
99:  * @returns Filtered array; original array returned unchanged when no threshold applies.
100:  */
101: function filterByMinQualityScore(
102:   results: PipelineRow[],
103:   threshold?: number
104: ): PipelineRow[] {
105:   if (typeof threshold !== 'number' || !Number.isFinite(threshold)) {
106:     return results;
107:   }
108: 
109:   const clampedThreshold = Math.max(0, Math.min(1, threshold));
110: 
111:   return results.filter((row) => {
112:     const rawScore = row.quality_score as number | undefined;
113:     const score =
114:       typeof rawScore === 'number' && Number.isFinite(rawScore) ? rawScore : 0;
115:     return score >= clampedThreshold;
116:   });
117: }
118: 
119: function backfillMissingQualityScores(results: PipelineRow[]): PipelineRow[] {
120:   return results.map((row) => {
121:     if (row.quality_score !== 0 && row.quality_score != null) {
122:       return row;
123:     }
124: 
125:     return {
126:       ...row,
127:       quality_score: computeBackfillQualityScore(row),
128:     };
129:   });
130: }
131: 
132: function applyArchiveFilter(
133:   results: PipelineRow[],
134:   _includeArchived: boolean
135: ): PipelineRow[] {
136:   return results;
137: }
138: 
139: function applyFolderFilter(
140:   results: PipelineRow[],
141:   specFolder?: string
142: ): PipelineRow[] {
143:   if (!specFolder) return results;
144:   return results.filter((row) => {
145:     const rowSpecFolder = row.spec_folder ?? row.specFolder;
146:     return rowSpecFolder === specFolder;
147:   });
148: }
149: 
150: /**
151:  * Prefix-aware spec-folder filter for the summary lane: a parent-scope query
152:  * keeps descendant folders, matching the primary BM25/vector lanes rather than
153:  * the exact-match {@link applyFolderFilter}. Summary-lane use only.
154:  */
155: function applyFolderFilterPrefix(
156:   results: PipelineRow[],
157:   specFolder?: string
158: ): PipelineRow[] {
159:   if (!specFolder) return results;
160:   return results.filter((row) => {
161:     const folder = row.spec_folder ?? row.specFolder;
162:     if (typeof folder !== 'string' || folder.length === 0) return false;
163:     return folder === specFolder || folder.startsWith(specFolder + '/');
164:   });
165: }
166: 
167: function applyTierFilter(
168:   results: PipelineRow[],
169:   tier?: string
170: ): PipelineRow[] {
171:   if (!tier) return results;
172:   return results.filter((row) => row.importance_tier === tier);
173: }
174: 
175: /**
176:  * Resolve the effective context type from a pipeline row.
177:  *
178:  * Rows may carry context type under either `contextType` (camelCase) or
179:  * `context_type` (snake_case). This function normalises the lookup.
180:  *
181:  * @param row - The pipeline row to inspect.
182:  * @returns The context type string, or `undefined` if absent.
183:  */
184: function resolveRowContextType(row: PipelineRow): string | undefined {
185:   if (typeof row.contextType === 'string' && row.contextType.length > 0) {
186:     return row.contextType;
187:   }
188:   if (typeof row.context_type === 'string' && row.context_type.length > 0) {
189:     return row.context_type;
190:   }
191:   return undefined;
192: }
193: 
194: function mergeStringLists(...values: unknown[]): string[] {
195:   const merged = new Set<string>();
196:   for (const value of values) {
197:     if (!Array.isArray(value)) {
198:       continue;
199:     }
200:     for (const entry of value) {
201:       if (typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0) {
202:         merged.add(entry);
203:       }
204:     }
205:   }
206:   return Array.from(merged);
207: }
208: 
209: function readFiniteScoreMap(value: unknown): Record<string, number> {
210:   if (!value || typeof value !== 'object' || Array.isArray(value)) {
211:     return {};
212:   }
213: 
214:   const normalized: Record<string, number> = {};
215:   for (const [key, score] of Object.entries(value as Record<string, unknown>)) {
216:     if (typeof score === 'number' && Number.isFinite(score)) {
217:       normalized[key] = score;
218:     }
219:   }
220:   return normalized;
221: }
222: 
223: function readQualityFlags(value: unknown): string[] {
224:   if (Array.isArray(value)) {
225:     return value.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0);
226:   }
227:   if (typeof value !== 'string' || value.trim().length === 0) {
228:     return [];
229:   }
230:   try {
231:     const parsed = JSON.parse(value) as unknown;
232:     if (Array.isArray(parsed)) {
233:       return parsed.filter((entry): entry is string => typeof entry === 'string' && entry.length > 0);
234:     }
235:   } catch {
236:     // Fall back to comma-delimited parsing for legacy callers/tests.
237:   }
238:   return value
239:     .split(',')
240:     .map((entry) => entry.trim())
241:     .filter((entry) => entry.length > 0);
242: }
243: 
244: function isMigratedGraphMetadataCandidate(row: PipelineRow): boolean {
245:   const migratedValue = row.migrated ?? row.is_migrated ?? row.metadata_migrated;
246:   if (migratedValue === true || migratedValue === 1 || migratedValue === '1' || migratedValue === 'true') {
247:     return true;
248:   }
249:   const qualityFlags = readQualityFlags(row.quality_flags ?? row.qualityFlags);
250:   return qualityFlags.includes(GRAPH_METADATA_MIGRATED_QUALITY_FLAG);
251: }
252: 
253: function mergeScoreMaps(...maps: Array<Record<string, number>>): Record<string, number> {
254:   const merged: Record<string, number> = {};
255:   for (const map of maps) {
256:     for (const [key, score] of Object.entries(map)) {
257:       if (!(key in merged) || score > merged[key]!) {
258:         merged[key] = score;
259:       }
260:     }
261:   }
262:   return merged;
263: }
264: 
265: function getCandidateSources(row: PipelineRow): string[] {
266:   const explicitSources = mergeStringLists(row.sources);
267:   if (explicitSources.length > 0) {
268:     return explicitSources;
269:   }
270:   return typeof row.source === 'string' && row.source.length > 0 ? [row.source] : [];
271: }
272: 
273: function getCandidateSourceScores(row: PipelineRow): Record<string, number> {
274:   const sourceScores = readFiniteScoreMap(row.sourceScores);
275:   if (Object.keys(sourceScores).length > 0) {
276:     return sourceScores;
277:   }
278: 
279:   const resolvedScore = resolveEffectiveScore(row);
280:   if (resolvedScore <= 0) {
281:     return {};
282:   }
283: 
284:   const fallbackScores: Record<string, number> = {};
285:   for (const source of getCandidateSources(row)) {
286:     fallbackScores[source] = resolvedScore;
287:   }
288:   return fallbackScores;
289: }
290: 
291: function isPacketOrientedQuery(query: string): boolean {
292:   const normalized = query.toLowerCase();
293:   return [
294:     'packet',
295:     'spec folder',
296:     'graph metadata',
297:     'dependency',
298:     'depends on',
299:     'related to',
300:     'supersedes',
301:     'resume',
302:     'key files',
303:     'parent',
304:     'children',
305:   ].some((term) => normalized.includes(term));
306: }
307: 
308: function boostGraphMetadataCandidates(
309:   results: PipelineRow[],
310:   query: string,
311: ): PipelineRow[] {
312:   if (!isPacketOrientedQuery(query)) {
313:     return results;
314:   }
315: 
316:   return results.map((row) => {
317:     if (row.document_type !== 'graph_metadata' && row.documentType !== 'graph_metadata') {
318:       return row;
319:     }
320: 
321:     const currentScore = resolveEffectiveScore(row);
322:     const adjustedScore = isMigratedGraphMetadataCandidate(row)
323:       ? Math.max(0, currentScore - 0.12)
324:       : Math.min(1, currentScore + 0.12);
325:     const sourceScores = getCandidateSourceScores(row);
326:     return {
327:       ...row,
328:       score: adjustedScore,
329:       rrfScore: adjustedScore,
330:       sourceScores: {
331:         ...sourceScores,
332:         graph_metadata: adjustedScore,
333:       },
334:     };
335:   });
336: }
337: 
338: function annotateBranchScore(row: PipelineRow, branchLabel: string): Record<string, number> {
339:   const existingBranchScores = readFiniteScoreMap(row.stage1BranchScores);
340:   const effectiveScore = resolveEffectiveScore(row);
341:   if (branchLabel.length === 0 || !Number.isFinite(effectiveScore)) {
342:     return existingBranchScores;
343:   }
344:   return mergeScoreMaps(existingBranchScores, { [branchLabel]: effectiveScore });
345: }
346: 
347: function mergeCandidateRows(
348:   existing: PipelineRow | undefined,
349:   incoming: PipelineRow,
350:   branchLabel: string,
351: ): PipelineRow {
352:   const incomingBranchScores = annotateBranchScore(incoming, branchLabel);
353:   if (!existing) {
354:     const sources = getCandidateSources(incoming);
355:     const channelAttribution = mergeStringLists(incoming.channelAttribution, sources);
356:     return {
357:       ...incoming,
358:       sources: sources.length > 0 ? sources : incoming.sources,
359:       channelAttribution: channelAttribution.length > 0 ? channelAttribution : incoming.channelAttribution,
360:       sourceScores: Object.keys(getCandidateSourceScores(incoming)).length > 0
361:         ? getCandidateSourceScores(incoming)
362:         : incoming.sourceScores,
363:       stage1BranchScores: Object.keys(incomingBranchScores).length > 0 ? incomingBranchScores : undefined,
364:       stage1BranchCount: Object.keys(incomingBranchScores).length || incoming.stage1BranchCount,
365:       channelCount: sources.length > 0 ? sources.length : incoming.channelCount,
366:     };
367:   }
368: 
369:   const existingScore = resolveEffectiveScore(existing);
370:   const incomingScore = resolveEffectiveScore(incoming);
371:   const primary = incomingScore > existingScore ? incoming : existing;
372:   const secondary = primary === incoming ? existing : incoming;
373: 
374:   const mergedSources = mergeStringLists(getCandidateSources(existing), getCandidateSources(incoming));
375:   const mergedChannelAttribution = mergeStringLists(
376:     existing.channelAttribution,
377:     incoming.channelAttribution,
378:     mergedSources,
379:   );
380:   const mergedSourceScores = mergeScoreMaps(
381:     getCandidateSourceScores(existing),
382:     getCandidateSourceScores(incoming),
383:   );
384:   const mergedBranchScores = mergeScoreMaps(
385:     readFiniteScoreMap(existing.stage1BranchScores),
386:     incomingBranchScores,
387:   );
388: 
389:   return {
390:     ...secondary,
391:     ...primary,
392:     sources: mergedSources.length > 0 ? mergedSources : primary.sources,
393:     source: typeof primary.source === 'string' && primary.source.length > 0
394:       ? primary.source
395:       : (mergedSources[0] ?? primary.source),
396:     channelAttribution: mergedChannelAttribution.length > 0
397:       ? mergedChannelAttribution
398:       : primary.channelAttribution,
399:     sourceScores: Object.keys(mergedSourceScores).length > 0
400:       ? mergedSourceScores
401:       : primary.sourceScores,
402:     stage1BranchScores: Object.keys(mergedBranchScores).length > 0
403:       ? mergedBranchScores
404:       : primary.stage1BranchScores,
405:     stage1BranchCount: Object.keys(mergedBranchScores).length || primary.stage1BranchCount,
406:     channelCount: mergedSources.length > 0 ? mergedSources.length : primary.channelCount,
407:   };
408: }
409: 
410: function mergeCandidateBatches(
411:   batches: Array<{ label: string; rows: PipelineRow[] }>,
412:   options: { seedCandidates?: PipelineRow[]; seedLabel?: string } = {},
413: ): PipelineRow[] {
414:   const merged = new Map<string, PipelineRow>();
415:   const seedLabel = options.seedLabel ?? '';
416: 
417:   for (const row of options.seedCandidates ?? []) {
418:     const key = String(row.id);
419:     merged.set(key, mergeCandidateRows(merged.get(key), row, seedLabel));
420:   }
421: 
422:   for (const batch of batches) {
423:     for (const row of batch.rows) {
424:       const key = String(row.id);
425:       merged.set(key, mergeCandidateRows(merged.get(key), row, batch.label));
426:     }
427:   }
428: 
429:   return Array.from(merged.values());
430: }
431: 
432: /**
433:  * Build deep-mode query variants using rule-based synonym expansion.
434:  *
435:  * The original query is always the first variant. Up to `MAX_DEEP_QUERY_VARIANTS - 1`
436:  * additional variants are produced by `expandQuery`. If expansion fails or produces
437:  * no new terms, the array contains only the original query.
438:  *
439:  * Simple-query bypass (038): When classifies the query as "simple",
440:  * rule-based expansion is skipped — consistent with the embedding-expansion
441:  * path's `isExpansionActive()` gate. Simple queries do not benefit from synonym
442:  * expansion and the additional search channels add latency without recall gain.
443:  *
444:  * Duplicates are eliminated via `Set` deduplication before slicing.
445:  *
446:  * @param query - The original search query string.
447:  * @returns Array of distinct query variants, original first, capped at MAX_DEEP_QUERY_VARIANTS.
448:  */
449: async function buildDeepQueryVariants(query: string): Promise<string[]> {
450:   try {
451:     const expanded = expandQuery(query);
452:     const variants = new Set<string>(expanded);
453:     // ExpandQuery already includes the original as the first entry,
454:     // But be explicit in case the implementation changes.
455:     variants.add(query);
456: 
457:     // Deep mode: ensure at least 2 variants so the multi-search branch fires.
458:     // When synonym expansion produces no new variants (simple/domain-specific queries),
459:     // generate a basic reformulation to trigger the parallel search path.
460:     if (variants.size < 2) {
461:       const words = query.trim().split(/\s+/);
462:       if (words.length >= 2) {
463:         variants.add(words.slice().reverse().join(' '));
464:       } else {
465:         variants.add(`what is ${query}`);
466:       }
467:     }
468: 
469:     return Array.from(variants).slice(0, MAX_DEEP_QUERY_VARIANTS);
470:   } catch (err: unknown) {
471:     const msg = err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err);
472:     console.warn(
473:       `[stage1-candidate-gen] buildDeepQueryVariants failed, using original query: ${msg}`
474:     );
475:     return [query];
476:   }
477: }
478: 
479: function normalizeFacetText(fragment: string): string {
480:   return fragment
481:     .replace(/^[\s,:;]+|[\s,.:;?!]+$/g, '')
482:     .replace(/\s+/g, ' ')
483:     .trim();
484: }
485: 
486: function decomposeQueryFacets(query: string): string[] {
487:   const trimmed = normalizeFacetText(query);
488:   if (trimmed.length === 0) return [];
489: 
490:   const focusMatch = trimmed.match(/\babout\s+(.+?)[?.!]*$/i);
491:   const focusSegment = focusMatch?.[1]?.trim() ?? trimmed;
492: 
493:   const listLikeFacets = focusSegment
494:     .split(/\s*(?:,|\band\b|\bor\b|\balso\b|\bplus\b|\bas well as\b|\balong with\b)\s*/i)
495:     .map(normalizeFacetText)
496:     .filter((facet) => facet.length > 0);
497: 
498:   if (listLikeFacets.length >= 2) {
499:     return [...new Set(listLikeFacets)].slice(0, MAX_QUERY_DECOMPOSITION_FACETS);
500:   }
501: 
502:   if (!isMultiFacet(trimmed)) {
503:     return [trimmed];
504:   }
505: 
506:   const decomposedFacets = decompose(trimmed)
507:     .map(normalizeFacetText)
508:     .filter((facet) => facet.length > 0);
509: 
510:   if (decomposedFacets.length === 0) {
511:     return [trimmed];
512:   }
513: 
514:   return [...new Set(decomposedFacets)].slice(0, MAX_QUERY_DECOMPOSITION_FACETS);
515: }
516: 
517: function buildQueryDecompositionPool(query: string, mode?: string | null): string[] {
518:   const normalizedQuery = normalizeFacetText(query);
519:   if (mode !== 'deep' || !isQueryDecompositionEnabled()) {
520:     return [normalizedQuery];
521:   }
522: 
523:   const facets = decomposeQueryFacets(query).filter((facet) => facet !== normalizedQuery);
524:   return [normalizedQuery, ...facets];
525: }
526: 
527: function mergeQueryFacetCoverage(resultSets: PipelineRow[][]): PipelineRow[] {
528:   const firstSeenById = new Map<number, PipelineRow>();
529:   for (const resultSet of resultSets) {
530:     for (const row of resultSet) {
531:       if (!firstSeenById.has(row.id)) {
532:         firstSeenById.set(row.id, row);
533:       }
534:     }
535:   }
536:   return Array.from(firstSeenById.values());
537: }
538: 
539: // -- Stage 1 --
540: 
541: /**
542:  * Execute Stage 1: Candidate Generation.
543:  *
544:  * Selects and runs the appropriate search channel(s) based on `config.searchType`
545:  * and `config.mode`, then applies vector-channel temporal contiguity when
546:  * enabled, followed by constitutional injection, quality filtering, and
547:  * tier/contextType filtering.
548:  *
549:  * This stage does not apply Stage 2 fusion/reranking signals. Vector-channel
550:  * results may receive a temporal proximity boost before moving downstream.
551:  *
552:  * @param input - Stage 1 input containing the resolved pipeline configuration.
553:  * @returns Stage 1 output with raw candidate rows and channel metadata.
554:  */
555: export async function executeStage1(input: Stage1Input): Promise<Stage1Output> {
556:   const startTime = Date.now();
557:   try {
558:     return await executeStage1Core(input, startTime);
559:   } catch (error: unknown) {
560:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
561:     console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] Stage 1 failed, returning empty candidates: ${message}`);
562:     return {
563:       candidates: [],
564:       metadata: {
565:         searchType: input.config.searchType,
566:         channelCount: 0,
567:         activeChannels: 0,
568:         candidateCount: 0,
569:         constitutionalInjected: 0,
570:         durationMs: Date.now() - startTime,
571:       },
572:     };
573:   }
574: }
575: 
576: async function executeStage1Core(input: Stage1Input, startTime: number): Promise<Stage1Output> {
577:   const { config } = input;
578:   // Cache embedding at function scope for reuse in constitutional injection
579:   let cachedEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null = null;
580:   let constitutionalInjectedCount = 0;
581: 
582:   const {
583:     query,
584:     queryEmbedding,
585:     concepts,
586:     searchType,
587:     mode,
588:     limit,
589:     specFolder,
590:     tenantId,
591:     userId,
592:     agentId,
593:     tier,
594:     contextType,
595:     includeArchived,
596:     includeConstitutional,
597:     qualityThreshold,
598:     trace,
599:   } = config;
600: 
601:   let candidates: PipelineRow[] = [];
602:   let channelCount = 0;
603:   const hybridSearchOptions = {
604:     limit,
605:     specFolder,
606:     includeArchived,
607:     intent: config.adaptiveFusionIntent ?? undefined,
608:   };
609: 
610:   // -- Graph Concept Routing -----------------------------------
611:   //
612:   // When SPECKIT_GRAPH_CONCEPT_ROUTING is enabled, extract noun phrases from
613:   // the query and match them against the concept alias table. If concepts are
614:   // matched, log them to the trace for downstream use (graph channel activation
615:   // is surfaced via trace metadata; actual graph channel is handled in Stage 2).
616:   //
617:   // When SPECKIT_QUERY_CONCEPT_EXPANSION is also enabled,
618:   // matched concepts are reverse-mapped to their alias terms and appended to
619:   // the query for the hybrid search channel, improving recall for alias-rich
620:   // queries (e.g. "semantic search" → also searches "retrieval", "query", etc.).
621:   //
622:   // Fail-open: any error leaves candidates unchanged.
623: 
624:   /** Effective query for hybrid search — may be expanded by concept routing. */
625:   let effectiveQuery = query;
626: 
627:   if (isGraphConceptRoutingEnabled() && searchType === 'hybrid') {
628:     try {
629:       let routingDb: Parameters<typeof routeQueryConcepts>[1];
630:       try {
631:         routingDb = requireDb();
632:       } catch (_dbErr: unknown) {
633:         routingDb = undefined;
634:       }
635:       const routing = routeQueryConcepts(query, routingDb);
636:       if (routing.graphActivated && routing.concepts.length > 0) {
637:         // Expand query with concept alias terms
638:         if (isQueryConceptExpansionEnabled()) {
639:           try {
640:             const originalTokens = nounPhrases(query);
641:             const expansionTerms = getConceptExpansionTerms(
642:               routing.concepts,
643:               originalTokens,
644:               5,
645:             );
646:             if (expansionTerms.length > 0) {
647:               effectiveQuery = `${query} ${expansionTerms.join(' ')}`;
648:               if (trace) {
649:                 addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', 0, 0, 0, {
650:                   channel: 'd2-concept-expansion',
651:                   originalQuery: query,
652:                   expandedQuery: effectiveQuery,
653:                   expansionTerms,
654:                   matchedConcepts: routing.concepts,
655:                 });
656:               }
657:             }
658:           } catch (expansionErr: unknown) {
659:             const expansionMsg = expansionErr instanceof Error ? expansionErr.message : String(expansionErr);
660:             console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 concept expansion failed (fail-open): ${expansionMsg}`);
661:           }
662:         }
663: 
664:         if (trace) {
665:           addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', 0, 0, 0, {
666:             channel: 'd2-concept-routing',
667:             matchedConcepts: routing.concepts,
668:             graphActivated: true,
669:           });
670:         }
671:       }
672:     } catch (routingErr: unknown) {
673:       const routingMsg = routingErr instanceof Error ? routingErr.message : String(routingErr);
674:       console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 concept routing failed: ${routingMsg}`);
675:     }
676:   }
677: 
678:   // -- Channel: Multi-Concept --------------------------------------------------
679: 
680:   if (searchType === 'multi-concept' && Array.isArray(concepts) && concepts.length >= 2) {
681:     // Validate concept list: 2-5 non-empty strings
682:     if (concepts.length > 5) {
683:       throw new Error('[stage1-candidate-gen] Maximum 5 concepts allowed for multi-concept search');
684:     }
685: 
686:     for (const concept of concepts) {
687:       if (typeof concept !== 'string' || concept.trim().length === 0) {
688:         throw new Error('[stage1-candidate-gen] Each concept must be a non-empty string');
689:       }
690:     }
691: 
692:     // Generate one embedding per concept
693:     const conceptEmbeddings: Float32Array[] = [];
694:     for (const concept of concepts) {
695:       const emb = await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(concept);
696:       if (!emb) {
697:         throw new Error(
698:           `[stage1-candidate-gen] Failed to generate embedding for concept: "${concept}"`
699:         );
700:       }
701:       conceptEmbeddings.push(emb);
702:     }
703: 
704:     channelCount = 1;
705:     candidates = vectorIndex.multiConceptSearch(conceptEmbeddings, {
706:       minSimilarity: MULTI_CONCEPT_MIN_SIMILARITY,
707:       limit,
708:       specFolder,
709:     }) as PipelineRow[];
710:   }
711: 
712:   // -- Channel: Hybrid (with optional deep-mode query expansion) ---------------
713:   //
714:   else if (searchType === 'hybrid') {
715:     // Resolve the query embedding — either pre-computed in config or generate now
716:     // Cache this embedding for reuse in constitutional injection path
717:     // To avoid a duplicate generateQueryEmbedding() call.
718:     const effectiveEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null =
719:       queryEmbedding ?? (await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(query));
720:     cachedEmbedding = effectiveEmbedding;
721: 
722:     if (!effectiveEmbedding) {
723:       throw new Error('[stage1-candidate-gen] Failed to generate embedding for hybrid search query');
724:     }
725: 
726:     // Deep mode: expand query into variants and run hybrid for each, then dedup
727:     if (mode === 'deep' && isMultiQueryEnabled()) {
728:       // -- Query Decomposition (SPECKIT_QUERY_DECOMPOSITION) ---
729:       //
730:       // When enabled and the query is multi-faceted, decompose into up to 3
731:       // sub-query facets and run hybrid search per facet. Results are merged
732:       // by facet coverage (items appearing in multiple facets rank higher).
733:       //
734:       // This block takes the place of the synonym-expansion path below when
735:       // decomposition fires. If decomposition is disabled, not applicable, or
736:       // fails, execution falls through to the existing expansion logic.
737: 
738:       if (isQueryDecompositionEnabled() && isMultiFacet(query)) {
739:         try {
740:           const normalizedQuery = normalizeFacetText(query);
741:           let facets: string[] = [];
742: 
743:           try {
744:             facets = buildQueryDecompositionPool(query, mode)
745:               .filter((facet) => facet !== normalizedQuery)
746:               .map(normalizeFacetText)
747:               .filter((facet) => facet.length > 0);
748:           } catch (facetErr: unknown) {
749:             const facetMsg = facetErr instanceof Error ? facetErr.message : String(facetErr);
750:             console.warn(
751:               `[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 faceted query decomposition failed, falling back to basic decomposition: ${facetMsg}`
752:             );
753:           }
754: 
755:           if (facets.length === 0) {
756:             facets = decompose(query)
757:               .map(normalizeFacetText)
758:               .filter((facet) => facet.length > 0 && facet !== normalizedQuery);
759:           }
760: 
761:           facets = [...new Set(facets)].slice(0, MAX_QUERY_DECOMPOSITION_FACETS);
762: 
763:           if (facets.length > 0) {
764:             // Run hybrid for the original query plus each facet, in parallel
765:             const allQueries = [query, ...facets];
766:             // FIX #7: Use Promise.allSettled so one failing facet branch
767:             // does not discard results from all other branches.
768:             const facetSettledResults = await withTimeout(
769:               Promise.allSettled(
770:                 allQueries.map(async (q): Promise<PipelineRow[]> => {
771:                   const facetEmbedding = await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(q);
772:                   if (!facetEmbedding) {
773:                     console.warn('[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 facet embedding generation returned null');
774:                     return [];
775:                   }
776:                   return hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
777:                     q,
778:                     facetEmbedding,
779:                     hybridSearchOptions
780:                   ) as Promise<PipelineRow[]>;
781:                 })
782:               ),
783:               DECOMPOSITION_TIMEOUT_MS,
784:               'D2 facet decomposition',
785:             );
786:             const facetResultSets = facetSettledResults.map((result, idx) => {
787:               if (result.status === 'fulfilled') return result.value;
788:               console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 facet branch ${idx} rejected: ${result.reason}`);
789:               return [] as PipelineRow[];
790:             });
791: 
792:             channelCount = allQueries.length;
793:             const pools = allQueries.map((q, i) => ({
794:               query: q,
795:               results: facetResultSets[i] ?? [],
796:             }));
797:             candidates = mergeFacetCoveragePools(pools);
798: 
799:             if (trace) {
800:               addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', channelCount, candidates.length, 0, {
801:                 channel: 'd2-query-decomposition',
802:                 originalQuery: query,
803:                 facets,
804:                 facetCount: facets.length,
805:               });
806:             }
807: 
808:             // Skip the standard deep-mode expansion path below
809:             // (jump to post-channel processing via the else-if chain)
810:           }
811:         } catch (decompErr: unknown) {
812:           const decompMsg = decompErr instanceof Error ? decompErr.message : String(decompErr);
813:           console.warn(
814:             `[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 query decomposition failed, falling through to expansion: ${decompMsg}`
815:           );
816:           // Fall through to standard deep expansion path below (candidates is empty)
817:         }
818:       }
819: 
820:       // Only run existing expansion logic when decomposition did not produce results
821:       if (candidates.length === 0) {
822: 
823:       const queryVariants = await buildDeepQueryVariants(query);
824: 
825:       if (queryVariants.length > 1) {
826:         try {
827:           // F1: Wrap parallel variant searches with latency budget.
828:           // If variants exceed DEEP_EXPANSION_TIMEOUT_MS, fall back to base query.
829:           // FIX #7: Use Promise.allSettled so one failing variant does not
830:           // discard results from all other variant branches.
831:           const variantSettledResults = await withTimeout(
832:             Promise.allSettled(
833:               queryVariants.map(async (variant): Promise<PipelineRow[]> => {
834:                 const variantEmbedding = await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(variant);
835:                 if (!variantEmbedding) {
836:                   console.warn('[stage1-candidate-gen] Deep variant embedding generation returned null');
837:                   return [];
838:                 }
839:                 const variantResults = await hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
840:                   variant,
841:                   variantEmbedding,
842:                   hybridSearchOptions
843:                 );
844:                 return variantResults as PipelineRow[];
845:               })
846:             ),
847:             DEEP_EXPANSION_TIMEOUT_MS,
848:             'Deep-mode query expansion',
849:           );
850:           const variantResultSets: PipelineRow[][] = variantSettledResults.map((result, idx) => {
851:             if (result.status === 'fulfilled') return result.value;
852:             console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] Deep variant branch ${idx} rejected: ${result.reason}`);
853:             return [] as PipelineRow[];
854:           });
855: 
856:           channelCount = queryVariants.length;
857: 
858:           // Merge variant results, deduplicate by id, preserve first-occurrence order
859:           candidates = mergeCandidateBatches(
860:             variantResultSets.map((rows, index) => ({
861:               label: queryVariants[index] ?? `deep-variant-${index}`,
862:               rows,
863:             })),
864:           );
865:         } catch (expandErr: unknown) {
866:           const expandMsg =
867:             expandErr instanceof Error ? expandErr.message : String(expandErr);
868:           console.warn(
869:             `[stage1-candidate-gen] Deep query expansion failed, falling back to single hybrid: ${expandMsg}`
870:           );
871:           // Fall through to single hybrid search below
872:           channelCount = 1;
873:           candidates = (await hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
874:             query,
875:             effectiveEmbedding,
876:             hybridSearchOptions
877:           )) as PipelineRow[];
878:         }
879:       } else {
880:         // ExpandQuery returned only the original; treat as standard hybrid
881:         channelCount = 1;
882:         candidates = (await hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
883:           query,
884:           effectiveEmbedding,
885:           hybridSearchOptions
886:         )) as PipelineRow[];
887:       }
888: 
889:       } // end: if (candidates.length === 0)
890:     } else {
891:       // Embedding-based query expansion (SPECKIT_EMBEDDING_EXPANSION)
892:       //
893:       // When is enabled and does not classify the query as "simple",
894:       // We expand the query using embedding similarity to find related terms
895:       // From the memory index. The expanded query is used as an additional
896:       // Hybrid search channel whose results are merged with the baseline.
897:       //
898:       // Mutual exclusion: isExpansionActive returns false when classifies
899:       // The query as "simple", suppressing expansion with zero added latency.
900: 
901:       let r12ExpansionApplied = false;
902: 
903:       if (isEmbeddingExpansionEnabled() && isExpansionActive(query)) {
904:         try {
905:           // ExpandQueryWithEmbeddings requires a Float32Array; the effective
906:           // Embedding may be a number[] when generated by some providers, so
907:           // Convert if necessary before passing it in.
908:           const expansionEmbedding: Float32Array =
909:             effectiveEmbedding instanceof Float32Array
910:               ? effectiveEmbedding
911:               : Float32Array.from(effectiveEmbedding as number[]);
912: 
913:           const expanded = await expandQueryWithEmbeddings(query, expansionEmbedding, {
914:             limit: DEFAULT_EXPANSION_CANDIDATE_LIMIT,
915:           });
916: 
917:           // Only run an extra channel if the expansion actually produced new terms
918:           if (expanded.expanded.length > 0 && expanded.combinedQuery !== query) {
919:             // Run the baseline and the expanded query in parallel
920:             const [baselineResults, expansionResults] = await Promise.all([
921:               hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
922:                 query,
923:                 effectiveEmbedding,
924:                 hybridSearchOptions
925:               ).catch((err: unknown): PipelineRow[] => {
926:                 console.warn(
927:                   '[stage1-candidate-gen] Baseline candidate collection failed:',
928:                   err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
929:                 );
930:                 return [];
931:               }),
932:               vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(expanded.combinedQuery).then(
933:                 async (expandedEmb): Promise<PipelineRow[]> => {
934:                   if (!expandedEmb) {
935:                     console.warn('[stage1-candidate-gen] Expanded query embedding generation returned null');
936:                     return [];
937:                   }
938:                   return hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
939:                     expanded.combinedQuery,
940:                     expandedEmb,
941:                     hybridSearchOptions
942:                   ) as Promise<PipelineRow[]>;
943:                 }
944:               ).catch((err: unknown): PipelineRow[] => {
945:                 console.warn(
946:                   '[stage1-candidate-gen] Expansion candidate collection failed:',
947:                   err instanceof Error ? err.message : String(err)
948:                 );
949:                 return [];
950:               }),
951:             ]);
952: 
953:             channelCount = 2;
954:             r12ExpansionApplied = true;
955: 
956:             // Merge both result sets, deduplicate by id, baseline-first ordering
957:             // So baseline scores dominate when the same memory appears in both.
958:             candidates = mergeCandidateBatches([
959:               { label: query, rows: baselineResults as PipelineRow[] },
960:               { label: expanded.combinedQuery, rows: expansionResults as PipelineRow[] },
961:             ]);
962: 
963:             if (trace) {
964:               addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', channelCount, candidates.length, 0, {
965:                 channel: 'r12-embedding-expansion',
966:                 expandedTerms: expanded.expanded,
967:                 combinedQuery: expanded.combinedQuery,
968:               });
969:             }
970:           }
971:         } catch (r12Err: unknown) {
972:           const r12Msg = r12Err instanceof Error ? r12Err.message : String(r12Err);
973:           console.warn(
974:             `[stage1-candidate-gen] R12 embedding expansion failed, using standard hybrid: ${r12Msg}`
975:           );
976:         }
977:       }
978: 
979:       // Standard hybrid search — runs when is off, suppressed by,
980:       // Or produced no results (candidates still empty from the try block above).
981:       // Uses effectiveQuery (concept-expanded) for BM25 recall.
982:       if (!r12ExpansionApplied) {
983:         try {
984:           channelCount = 1;
985:           const hybridResults = (await hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
986:             effectiveQuery,
987:             effectiveEmbedding,
988:             hybridSearchOptions
989:           )) as PipelineRow[];
990:           candidates = hybridResults;
991:         } catch (hybridErr: unknown) {
992:           const hybridMsg =
993:             hybridErr instanceof Error ? hybridErr.message : String(hybridErr);
994:           console.warn(
995:             `[stage1-candidate-gen] Hybrid search failed, falling back to vector: ${hybridMsg}`
996:           );
997: 
998:           // Fallback: pure vector search
999:           channelCount = 1;
1000:           let vectorResults = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(effectiveEmbedding, {
1001:             limit,
1002:             specFolder,
1003:             tier,
1004:             contextType,
1005:             includeConstitutional: false, // Constitutional managed separately below
1006:             includeArchived,
1007:           }) as PipelineRow[];
1008:           if (isTemporalContiguityEnabled()) {
1009:             vectorResults = (
1010:               vectorSearchWithContiguity(
1011:                 vectorResults as Array<PipelineRow & { similarity: number; created_at: string }>,
1012:                 3600,
1013:               ) as PipelineRow[] | null
1014:             ) ?? vectorResults;
1015:           }
1016:           candidates = vectorResults;
1017: 
1018:           if (trace) {
1019:             addTraceEntry(trace, 'fallback', 0, candidates.length, 0, {
1020:               reason: hybridMsg,
1021:               channel: 'vector',

1024:         }
1025:       }
1026:     }
1027:   }
1028: 
1029:   // -- Channel: Vector ---------------------------------------------------------
1030: 
1031:   else if (searchType === 'vector') {
1032:     const effectiveEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null =
1033:       queryEmbedding ?? (await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(query));
1034: 
1035:     if (!effectiveEmbedding) {
1036:       throw new Error('[stage1-candidate-gen] Failed to generate embedding for vector search query');
1037:     }
1038: 
1039:     channelCount = 1;
1040:     let vectorResults = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(effectiveEmbedding, {
1041:       limit,
1042:       specFolder,
1043:       tier,
1044:       contextType,
1045:       includeConstitutional: false, // Constitutional managed separately below
1046:       includeArchived,

1067:   // -- Tier and contextType filtering -----------------------------------------
1068:   //
1069:   // Applied after candidate collection but before constitutional injection so
1070:   // Injected constitutional rows are evaluated by the same filters.

1091:     || userId
1092:     || agentId
1093:   );
1094:   const shouldApplyScopeFiltering = hasGovernanceScope;
1095:   const scopeFilter = {
1096:     tenantId,
1097:     userId,
1098:     agentId,

1100: 
1101:   if (shouldApplyScopeFiltering) {
1102:     try {
1103:       const db = requireDb();
1104:       void db;
1105:       candidates = filterRowsByScope(candidates, scopeFilter);
1106:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
1107:       candidates = filterRowsByScope(candidates, scopeFilter);
1108:     }
1109:   }
1110: 
1111:   // -- Constitutional Memory Injection ----------------------------------------
1112:   //
1113:   // If includeConstitutional is requested and no constitutional results exist
1114:   // In the current candidate set, fetch them separately via vector search.
1115:   // They enter the pipeline here so all subsequent stages (scoring, reranking)
1116:   // Treat them uniformly. Constitutional tier boost is applied in Stage 2.
1117:   //
1118:   // Injection is skipped when:
1119:   //   - includeConstitutional is false
1120:   //   - A tier filter is active (caller explicitly requested a specific tier)
1121:   //   - Constitutional results already exist in the candidate set
1122: 
1123:   if (includeConstitutional && !tier) {
1124:     const existingConstitutional = candidates.filter(
1125:       (r) => r.importance_tier === 'constitutional'
1126:     );
1127: 
1128:     if (existingConstitutional.length === 0) {
1129:       // Reuse cached embedding instead of generating a new one
1130:       const constitutionalEmbedding: Float32Array | number[] | null =
1131:         cachedEmbedding ?? queryEmbedding ?? (await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(query));
1132: 
1133:       if (constitutionalEmbedding) {
1134:         const constitutionalResults = vectorIndex.vectorSearch(
1135:           constitutionalEmbedding,
1136:           {
1137:             limit: CONSTITUTIONAL_INJECT_LIMIT,
1138:             specFolder,
1139:             tier: 'constitutional',
1140:             useDecay: false,
1141:           }
1142:         ) as PipelineRow[];
1143: 
1144:         // Only inject rows not already present
1145:         const existingIds = new Set(candidates.map((r) => r.id));
1146:         const uniqueConstitutional = constitutionalResults.filter(
1147:           (r) => !existingIds.has(r.id)
1148:         );
1149: 
1150:         // Re-apply filters after injection because constitutional rows fetched
1151:         // via vector search bypass the earlier governance/context gate.
1152:         const contextFilteredConstitutional = contextType
1153:           ? uniqueConstitutional.filter((r) => resolveRowContextType(r) === contextType)
1154:           : uniqueConstitutional;

1156:         const filteredConstitutional = shouldApplyScopeFiltering

1160:         constitutionalInjectedCount = filteredConstitutional.length;

1170:   // -- Quality Score Filtering ------------------------------------------------

1172:   candidates = backfillMissingQualityScores(candidates);

1176:   // -- Corpus-Grounded LLM Reformulation ----------------------
1177:   //
1178:   // When SPECKIT_LLM_REFORMULATION is enabled and mode === 'deep':
1179:   //   1. Retrieve top-3 seed results via fast BM25/FTS5 (no embedding call).
1180:   //   2. Ask the LLM to produce a step-back abstraction + corpus-grounded variants.
1181:   //   3. Fan-out [original, abstract, ...variants] as additional hybrid search channels.
1182:   //   4. Deduplicate and merge into candidates.
1183:   //
1184:   // Budget: 1 LLM call per cache miss (0 on cache hit).

1187:   if (mode === 'deep' && isLlmReformulationEnabled() && searchType === 'hybrid') {
1188:     try {
1189:       const seeds = cheapSeedRetrieve(query, { limit: 3 });

1200:           const reformSettledResults = await Promise.allSettled(

1203:               const emb = idx === 0 ? reformEmbedding : await vectorIndex.generateQueryEmbedding(q);
1204:               if (!emb) {
1205:                 console.warn('[stage1-candidate-gen] LLM reform embedding generation returned null');
1206:                 return [];
1207:               }
1208:               return hybridSearch.collectRawCandidates(
1209:                 q,
1210:                 emb,
1211:                 hybridSearchOptions
1212:               ) as Promise<PipelineRow[]>;
1213:             })
1214:           );
1215:           const reformResultSets = reformSettledResults.map((result, idx) => {
1216:             if (result.status === 'fulfilled') return result.value;
1217:             console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 LLM reform branch ${idx} rejected: ${result.reason}`);
1218:             return [] as PipelineRow[];
1219:           });
1220: 
1221:           if (reformResultSets.length > 0) {
1222:             const filteredReformSets = reformResultSets.map((resultSet, index) => {
1223:               let rows = shouldApplyScopeFiltering
1224:                 ? filterRowsByScope(resultSet, scopeFilter)
1225:                 : resultSet;

1227:                 rows = rows.filter((r) => resolveRowContextType(r) === contextType);
1228:               }
1229:               if (tier) {
1230:                 rows = applyTierFilter(rows, tier);
1231:               }
1232:               rows = backfillMissingQualityScores(rows);
1233:               rows = filterByMinQualityScore(rows, qualityThreshold);
1234:               return {
1235:                 label: allQueries[index] ?? `d2-reform-${index}`,
1236:                 rows,
1237:               };
1238:             });
1239:             const reformMergedCount = filteredReformSets.reduce((sum, batch) => sum + batch.rows.length, 0);
1240:             candidates = mergeCandidateBatches(filteredReformSets, {
1241:               seedCandidates: candidates,
1242:               seedLabel: query,
1243:             });
1244:             channelCount += allQueries.length - 1; // discount original (already counted)
1245: 
1246:             if (trace) {
1247:               addTraceEntry(trace, 'candidate', allQueries.length - 1, reformMergedCount, 0, {
1248:                 channel: 'd2-llm-reformulation',
1249:                 abstract: reform.abstract,
1250:                 variantCount: reform.variants.length,
1251:                 fanoutCount: allQueries.length,
1252:               });
1253:             }
1254:           }
1255:         }
1256:       }
1257:     } catch (reformErr: unknown) {
1258:       const reformMsg = reformErr instanceof Error ? reformErr.message : String(reformErr);
1259:       console.warn(`[stage1-candidate-gen] D2 LLM reformulation failed: ${reformMsg}`);
1260:     }
1261:   }
1262: 
1263:   // -- HyDE Shadow Mode ----------------------------------------
1264:   //
1265:   // When SPECKIT_HYDE is enabled and mode === 'deep':
1266:   //   - Check if the current baseline has low confidence.
1267:   //   - If so, generate a HyDE pseudo-document and embed it.
1268:   //   - Run a vector-only search with the pseudo-document embedding.
1269:   //   - Shadow mode (SPECKIT_HYDE_ACTIVE=false): log results, do NOT merge.
1270:   //   - Active mode (SPECKIT_HYDE_ACTIVE=true): merge into candidates.
1271:   //
1272:   // Budget: 1 LLM call per cache miss (shared cache with reformulation → ≤2 total).
1273:   // Fail-open: any error leaves candidates unchanged.

1275:   if (mode === 'deep' && isHyDEEnabled() && searchType === 'hybrid') {
1276:     try {
1277:       const rawHydeCandidates = await runHyDE(query, candidates, limit, specFolder);
1278:       const hydeCandidates = shouldApplyScopeFiltering

54: function isExpectedMissingVecMemoriesTable(error: unknown): boolean {
55:   const message = get_error_message(error).toLowerCase();
56:   return message.includes('no such table') && message.includes('vec_memories');

59: function activeVectorTableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
60:   try {
61:     const row = database.prepare(`
62:       SELECT 1 AS found
63:       FROM ${activeVectorSource('sqlite_master')}
64:       WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ?
65:       LIMIT 1
66:     `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
67:     return row?.found === 1;
68:   } catch (_error: unknown) {
69:     return false;
70:   }
71: }

73: function activeDimVectorSource(database: Database.Database): string | null {
74:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
75:   if (active.name === DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name) {
76:     return null;
77:   }
78:   const tableName = vecTableNameForDim(active.dim);
79:   return activeVectorTableExists(database, tableName) ? activeVectorSource(tableName) : null;
80: }

82: function writeActiveVectorPayload(database: Database.Database, id: bigint, embeddingBuffer: Buffer): void {

86:   `).run(id, embeddingBuffer);

91:   }

102: function invalidateGraphCaches(database: Database.Database): void {
103:   // Bump the generation FIRST so every cache key that includes it (e.g.
104:   // memory_search with causal boost) goes stale before the per-db caches clear;
105:   // without this, memory-delete sweeps served stale causal-boosted search results.

159: function deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database: Database.Database, id: number): void {
160:   const ancillaryTables = [
161:     'DELETE FROM degree_snapshots WHERE memory_id = ?',
162:     'DELETE FROM community_assignments WHERE memory_id = ?',
163:     'DELETE FROM memory_summaries WHERE memory_id = ?',
164:     'DELETE FROM memory_entities WHERE memory_id = ?',
165:     'DELETE FROM memory_lineage WHERE memory_id = ?',
166:   ];
167: 
168:   for (const sql of ancillaryTables) {
169:     try {
170:       const paramCount = (sql.match(/\?/g) || []).length;
171:       if (paramCount === 2) {
172:         database.prepare(sql).run(id, id);
173:       } else {
174:         database.prepare(sql).run(id);
175:       }
176:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
177:       // Best-effort for legacy databases that may not have these tables yet.
178:     }
179:   }

181:   // Clean active_memory_projection rows referencing this memory.
182:   try {
183:     database.prepare('DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ?').run(id);
184:   } catch (_error: unknown) {

186:   }

190:       memoryIds: [id, String(id)],
191:       reason: 'memory row mutation cleanup',
192:       command: 'vector-index-mutations.deleteAncillaryMemoryRows',
193:       restoreContext: { memoryId: id },

201:     // the match to those table names so an unrelated missing-table error still
202:     // propagates and rolls back the surrounding delete instead of committing a
203:     // hard delete without its tombstone audit row.
204:     const message = error instanceof Error ? error.message : String(error);
205:     if (!/no such table:\s*causal_edge/i.test(message)) {
206:       throw error;

211: function upsert_active_projection(
212:   database: ReturnType<typeof initialize_db>,
213:   specFolder: string,

221:     specFolder,

229:   // when the logical_key changes (e.g. anchor or path normalization drift).
230:   database.prepare(
231:     'DELETE FROM active_memory_projection WHERE active_memory_id = ? AND logical_key != ?',

233:   database.prepare(`
234:     INSERT INTO active_memory_projection (logical_key, root_memory_id, active_memory_id, updated_at)
235:     VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)

237:       root_memory_id = excluded.root_memory_id,
238:       active_memory_id = excluded.active_memory_id,
239:       updated_at = excluded.updated_at
240:   `).run(logicalKey, memoryId, memoryId, updatedAt);
