263: };
264: 
265: function normalizeMemoryScope(scope?: MemoryScopeParams): NormalizedMemoryScope {
266:   return {
267:     tenant_id: normalizeScopeValue(scope?.tenantId),
268:     user_id: normalizeScopeValue(scope?.userId),
269:     agent_id: normalizeScopeValue(scope?.agentId),
270:     session_id: normalizeScopeValue(scope?.sessionId),
271:   };
272: }
273: 
274: /**
275:  * Indexes a memory with an embedding payload.
276:  * @param params - The memory values to index.
277:  * @returns The indexed memory identifier.
278:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} When embedding validation fails or the mutation cannot be applied.
279:  * @example
280:  * ```ts
281:  * const id = index_memory({
282:  *   specFolder: 'specs/001-demo',
283:  *   filePath: 'spec.md',
284:  *   embedding,
285:  * });
286:  * ```
287:  */
288: export function index_memory(
289:   params: IndexMemoryParams,
290:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
291: ): number {
292:   const {
293:     specFolder,
294:     filePath,
295:     parentId = null,
296:     anchorId = null,
297:     title = null,
298:     triggerPhrases = [],
299:     importanceWeight = 0.5,
300:     embedding,
301:     encodingIntent,
302:     documentType = 'memory',
303:     specLevel = null,
304:     contentText = null,
305:     qualityScore = 0,
306:     qualityFlags = [],
307:     appendOnly = false,
308:   } = params;
309:   const scope = normalizeMemoryScope(params.scope);
310: 
311:   if (!embedding) {
312:     throw new VectorIndexError('Embedding is required', VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION);
313:   }
314: 
315:   const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
316:   if (embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
317:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Embedding dimension mismatch: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding.length}`);
318:     throw new VectorIndexError(
319:       `Embedding must be ${expected_dim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`,
320:       VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
321:     );
322:   }
323: 
324:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
325:   const triggers_json = JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases);
326:   const embedding_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(embedding);
327:   const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
328: 
329:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
330:   const existing = appendOnly
331:     ? null
332:     : stmts.get_by_folder_and_path.get(
333:         specFolder,
334:         canonicalFilePath,
335:         filePath,
336:         canonicalFilePath,
337:         filePath,
338:         anchorId,
339:         scope.tenant_id,
340:         scope.user_id,
341:         scope.agent_id,
342:         scope.session_id,
343:       );
344: 
345:   if (existing && !appendOnly) {
346:     return update_memory({
347:       id: existing.id,
348:       title: title ?? undefined,
349:       triggerPhrases,
350:       importanceWeight,
351:       embedding,
352:       encodingIntent,
353:       documentType,
354:       specLevel,
355:       contentText,
356:       qualityScore,
357:       qualityFlags,
358:       canonicalFilePath,
359:       filePath,
360:     }, database);
361:   }
362: 
363:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
364: 
365:   const index_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
366:     const embedding_status = sqlite_vec ? 'success' : 'pending';
367: 
368:     const result = database.prepare(`
369:       INSERT INTO memory_index (
370:         spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id, title, trigger_phrases,
371:         importance_weight, created_at, updated_at, embedding_model,
372:         embedding_generated_at, embedding_status, encoding_intent, document_type, spec_level,
373:         content_text, quality_score, quality_flags, parent_id,
374:         tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id
375:       ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
376:     `).run(
377:       specFolder, filePath, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, title, triggers_json,
378:       importanceWeight, now, now, embeddingsProvider.getModelName(), now, embedding_status,
379:       encodingIntent ?? 'document', documentType, specLevel, contentText, qualityScore, JSON.stringify(qualityFlags), parentId,
380:       scope.tenant_id, scope.user_id, scope.agent_id, scope.session_id
381:     );
382: 
383:     const row_id = BigInt(result.lastInsertRowid);
384:     const metadata_id = Number(row_id);
385: 
386:     upsert_active_projection(database, specFolder, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, metadata_id, now, scope);
387: 
388:     if (sqlite_vec) {
389:       writeActiveVectorPayload(database, row_id, embedding_buffer);
390:     }
391: 
392:     refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, specFolder);
393:     clear_search_cache();
394: 
395:     return metadata_id;
396:   });
397: 
398:   return index_memory_tx();
399: }
400: 
401: // Deferred indexing - entry searchable via BM25/FTS5 only
402: /**
403:  * Indexes a memory record without storing an embedding yet.
404:  * @param params - The deferred memory values to index.
405:  * @returns The indexed memory identifier.
406:  */
407: export function index_memory_deferred(
408:   params: IndexMemoryDeferredParams,
409:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
410: ): number {
411:   const {
412:     specFolder,
413:     filePath,
414:     parentId = null,
415:     anchorId = null,
416:     title = null,
417:     triggerPhrases = [],
418:     importanceWeight = 0.5,
419:     failureReason = null,
420:     encodingIntent,
421:     documentType = 'memory',
422:     specLevel = null,
423:     contentText = null,
424:     qualityScore = 0,
425:     qualityFlags = [],
426:     appendOnly = false,
427:   } = params;
428:   const scope = normalizeMemoryScope(params.scope);
429: 
430:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
431:   const triggers_json = JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases);
432:   const canonicalFilePath = getCanonicalPathKey(filePath);
433: 
434:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
435:   const existing = appendOnly
436:     ? null
437:     : stmts.get_by_folder_and_path.get(
438:         specFolder,
439:         canonicalFilePath,
440:         filePath,
441:         canonicalFilePath,
442:         filePath,
443:         anchorId,
444:         scope.tenant_id,
445:         scope.user_id,
446:         scope.agent_id,
447:         scope.session_id,
448:       );
449: 
450:   const index_memory_deferred_tx = database.transaction(() => {
451:     if (existing && !appendOnly) {
452:       database.prepare(`
453:         UPDATE memory_index
454:         SET title = ?,
455:             trigger_phrases = ?,
456:             importance_weight = ?,
457:             file_path = ?,
458:             canonical_file_path = ?,
459:             embedding_status = 'pending',
460:             failure_reason = ?,
461:             updated_at = ?,
462:             encoding_intent = COALESCE(?, encoding_intent),
463:             document_type = ?,
464:             spec_level = ?,
465:             content_text = ?,
466:             quality_score = ?,
467:             quality_flags = ?,
468:             retry_count = 0,
469:             last_retry_at = NULL
470:         WHERE id = ?
471:       `).run(title, triggers_json, importanceWeight, filePath, canonicalFilePath, failureReason, now, encodingIntent, documentType, specLevel, contentText, qualityScore, JSON.stringify(qualityFlags), existing.id);
472:       upsert_active_projection(database, specFolder, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, existing.id, now, scope);
473:       refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, specFolder);
474:       return existing.id;
475:     }
476: 
477:     const result = database.prepare(`
478:       INSERT INTO memory_index (
479:         spec_folder, file_path, canonical_file_path, anchor_id, title, trigger_phrases,
480:         importance_weight, created_at, updated_at, embedding_status,
481:         failure_reason, retry_count, encoding_intent, document_type, spec_level,
482:         content_text, quality_score, quality_flags, parent_id,
483:         tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id
484:       ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, 'pending', ?, 0, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
485:     `).run(
486:       specFolder, filePath, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, title, triggers_json,
487:       importanceWeight, now, now, failureReason, encodingIntent ?? 'document', documentType, specLevel, contentText, qualityScore, JSON.stringify(qualityFlags), parentId,
488:       scope.tenant_id, scope.user_id, scope.agent_id, scope.session_id
489:     );
490: 
491:     const row_id = BigInt(result.lastInsertRowid);
492:     upsert_active_projection(database, specFolder, canonicalFilePath, anchorId, Number(row_id), now, scope);
493:     refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, specFolder);
494:     logger.info(`Deferred indexing: Memory ${Number(row_id)} saved without embedding (BM25/FTS5 searchable)`);
495: 
496:     return Number(row_id);
497:   });
498: 
499:   return index_memory_deferred_tx();
500: }
501: 
502: /**
503:  * Updates stored memory metadata and embeddings.
504:  * @param params - The memory values to update.
505:  * @returns The updated memory identifier.
506:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} When embedding validation fails or the mutation transaction cannot complete.
507:  * @example
508:  * ```ts
509:  * const id = update_memory({ id: 42, title: 'Updated title', embedding });
510:  * ```
511:  */
512: export function update_memory(
513:   params: UpdateMemoryParams,
514:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
515: ): number {
516:   const {
517:     id,
518:     title,
519:     triggerPhrases,
520:     importanceWeight,
521:     importanceTier,
522:     embedding,
523:     canonicalFilePath,
524:     filePath,
525:     encodingIntent,
526:     documentType,
527:     specLevel,
528:     contentText,
529:     qualityScore,
530:     qualityFlags,
531:   } = params;
532: 
533:   const now = new Date().toISOString();
534: 
535:   const update_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
536:     const existingRow = database.prepare(`
537:       SELECT spec_folder, anchor_id, canonical_file_path, file_path, importance_tier,
538:              tenant_id, user_id, agent_id, session_id
539:       FROM memory_index
540:       WHERE id = ?
541:     `).get(id) as {
542:       spec_folder: string | null;
543:       anchor_id: string | null;
544:       canonical_file_path: string | null;
545:       file_path: string | null;
546:       importance_tier: string | null;
547:       tenant_id: string | null;
548:       user_id: string | null;
549:       agent_id: string | null;
550:       session_id: string | null;
551:     } | undefined;
552:     const updates = ['updated_at = ?'];
553:     const values: unknown[] = [now];
554: 
555:     if (title !== undefined) {
556:       updates.push('title = ?');
557:       values.push(title);
558:     }
559:     if (triggerPhrases !== undefined) {
560:       updates.push('trigger_phrases = ?');
561:       values.push(JSON.stringify(triggerPhrases));
562:     }
563:     if (importanceWeight !== undefined) {
564:       updates.push('importance_weight = ?');
565:       values.push(importanceWeight);
566:     }
567:     if (importanceTier !== undefined) {
568:       let nextImportanceTier = importanceTier;
569:       const previousTier = existingRow?.importance_tier ?? null;
570:       if (
571:         existingRow
572:       ) {
573:         const guardPath = existingRow.canonical_file_path || existingRow.file_path;
574:         const hasNonConstitutionalPath = guardPath && !isIndexableConstitutionalMemoryPath(guardPath);
575:         if (hasNonConstitutionalPath && importanceTier === 'constitutional') {
576:           // Non-constitutional paths cannot be promoted to constitutional tier.
577:           nextImportanceTier = 'important';
578:         }
579:         if (
580:           hasNonConstitutionalPath
581:           && (
582:             importanceTier === 'constitutional'
583:             || (previousTier === 'constitutional' && nextImportanceTier !== 'constitutional')
584:           )
585:         ) {
586:           tryRecordTierDowngradeAudit(database, {
587:             memoryId: id,
588:             specFolder: existingRow.spec_folder,
589:             anchorId: existingRow.anchor_id,
590:             filePath: existingRow.file_path,
591:             canonicalFilePath: existingRow.canonical_file_path,
592:             requestedTier: importanceTier,
593:             previousTier,
594:             nextTier: nextImportanceTier,
595:             source: 'update_memory',
596:           });
597:         }
598:       }
599:       updates.push('importance_tier = ?');
600:       values.push(nextImportanceTier);
601:       clear_constitutional_cache();
602:     }
603:     if (canonicalFilePath !== undefined) {
604:       updates.push('canonical_file_path = ?');
605:       values.push(canonicalFilePath);
606:     }
607:     if (filePath !== undefined) {
608:       updates.push('file_path = ?');
609:       values.push(filePath);
610:     }
611:     if (encodingIntent !== undefined) {
612:       updates.push('encoding_intent = ?');
613:       values.push(encodingIntent);
614:     }
615:     if (documentType !== undefined) {
616:       updates.push('document_type = ?');
617:       values.push(documentType);
618:     }
619:     if (specLevel !== undefined) {
620:       updates.push('spec_level = ?');
621:       values.push(specLevel);
622:     }
623:     if (contentText !== undefined) {
624:       updates.push('content_text = ?');
625:       values.push(contentText);
626:     }
627:     if (qualityScore !== undefined) {
628:       updates.push('quality_score = ?');
629:       values.push(qualityScore);
630:     }
631:     if (qualityFlags !== undefined) {
632:       updates.push('quality_flags = ?');
633:       values.push(JSON.stringify(qualityFlags));
634:     }
635:     if (embedding) {
636:       updates.push('embedding_model = ?');
637:       updates.push('embedding_generated_at = ?');
638:       updates.push('embedding_status = ?');
639:       values.push(embeddingsProvider.getModelName(), now, 'pending');
640:     }
641: 
642:     values.push(id);
643: 
644:     const updateResult = database.prepare(`
645:       UPDATE memory_index SET ${updates.join(', ')} WHERE id = ?
646:     `).run(...values);
647: 
648:     // Return early if the target row no longer exists.
649:     if (updateResult.changes === 0) {
650:       return id;
651:     }
652: 
653:     const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
654:     if (embedding && sqlite_vec) {
655:       const expected_dim = get_embedding_dim();
656:       if (embedding.length !== expected_dim) {
657:         console.warn(`[vector-index] Embedding dimension mismatch in update: expected ${expected_dim}, got ${embedding.length}`);
658:         throw new VectorIndexError(
659:           `Embedding must be ${expected_dim} dimensions, got ${embedding.length}`,
660:           VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
661:         );
662:       }
663: 
664:       const embedding_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(embedding);
665:       writeActiveVectorPayload(database, BigInt(id), embedding_buffer);
666:       database.prepare('UPDATE memory_index SET embedding_status = ? WHERE id = ?').run('success', id);
667:     }
668: 
669:     if (existingRow?.spec_folder) {
670:       const projectionPath = canonicalFilePath
671:         ?? existingRow.canonical_file_path
672:         ?? getCanonicalPathKey(existingRow.file_path ?? '');
673:       upsert_active_projection(database, existingRow.spec_folder, projectionPath, existingRow.anchor_id ?? null, id, now, {
674:         tenant_id: existingRow.tenant_id,
675:         user_id: existingRow.user_id,
676:         agent_id: existingRow.agent_id,
677:         session_id: existingRow.session_id,
678:       });
679:     }
680: 
681:     if (existingRow?.spec_folder) {
682:       refresh_interference_scores_for_folder(database, existingRow.spec_folder);
683:     }
684:     clear_search_cache();
685: 
686:     return id;
687:   });
688: 
689:   return update_memory_tx();
690: }
691: 
692: /**
693:  * Deletes a memory and its related index records.
694:  * @param id - The memory identifier.
695:  * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
696:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation or store initialization failures from the delete pipeline.
697:  * @example
698:  * ```ts
699:  * const deleted = delete_memory(42);
700:  * ```
701:  */
702: export function delete_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): boolean {
703:   return delete_memory_from_database(database, id);
704: }
705: 
706: /**
707:  * Deletes a memory and related index records using the provided database handle.
708:  * @param database - The database containing the target memory.
709:  * @param id - The memory identifier.
710:  * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
711:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates mutation failures encountered while deleting the memory.
712:  * @example
713:  * ```ts
714:  * const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(database, 42);
715:  * ```
716:  */
717: export function delete_memory_from_database(database: Database.Database, id: number): boolean {
718:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
719: 
720:   const delete_memory_tx = database.transaction(() => {
721:     // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
722:     // So DELETE audit events recorded by handlers persist as audit trail.
723:     const row = database.prepare('SELECT content_hash FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(id) as
724:       | { content_hash: string | null }
725:       | undefined;
726: 
727:     if (sqlite_vec) {
728:       try {
729:         deleteActiveVectorPayload(database, BigInt(id));
730:       } catch (e: unknown) {
731:         if (!isExpectedMissingVecMemoriesTable(e)) {
732:           throw new VectorIndexError(
733:             `Vector deletion failed for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`,
734:             VectorIndexErrorCode.MUTATION_FAILED,
735:           );
736:         }
737:       }
738:     }
739: 
740:     // Clean ancillary records so deletes do not leave graph residue behind.
741:     deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
742: 
743:     if (row?.content_hash) {
744:       // Governed retention delete semantics include derived embedding-cache rows.
745:       try {
746:         deleteByContentHash(database, row.content_hash);
747:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
748:         // Legacy or test databases may not have embedding_cache yet.
749:       }
750:     }
751: 
752:     const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
753: 
754:     clear_search_cache();
755:     clear_constitutional_cache();
756: 
757:     return result.changes > 0;
758:   });
759: 
760:   // Remove the BM25 document only after the source row is deleted.
761:   const deleted = delete_memory_tx();
762:   if (deleted) {
763:     // Invalidate entity-density cache so deleted memory's tokens are not routed
764:     // to the graph channel beyond the 60s TTL window. Best-effort: non-fatal.
765:     try {
766:       invalidateEntityDensityCache();
767:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
768:       // Cache invalidation is best-effort; do not mask a successful delete.
769:     }
770:   }
771:   if (deleted) {
772:     try {
773:       if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
774:         bm25Index.getIndex().removeDocument(String(id));
775:       }
776:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
777:       // BEST-EFFORT BM25 CLEANUP MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL PRIMARY DELETE.
778:     }
779:   }
780:   return deleted;
781: }
782: 
783: /**
784:  * Deletes the latest memory for a file path and optional anchor.
785:  * @param spec_folder - The owning spec folder.
786:  * @param file_path - The file path to delete.
787:  * @param anchor_id - The optional anchor identifier.
788:  * @returns True when a memory was deleted.
789:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates delete failures from the underlying mutation helpers.
790:  * @example
791:  * ```ts
792:  * const deleted = delete_memory_by_path('specs/001-demo', 'spec.md');
793:  * ```
794:  */
795: export function delete_memory_by_path(
796:   spec_folder: string,
797:   file_path: string,
798:   anchor_id: string | null = null,
799:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
800: ): boolean {
801:   const canonicalPath = getCanonicalPathKey(file_path);
802: 
803:   const row = database.prepare(`
804:     SELECT id, spec_folder FROM memory_index
805:     WHERE spec_folder = ?
806:       AND (canonical_file_path = ? OR file_path = ?)
807:       AND (anchor_id = ? OR (anchor_id IS NULL AND ? IS NULL))
808:     ORDER BY id DESC
809:     LIMIT 1
810:   `).get(spec_folder, canonicalPath, file_path, anchor_id, anchor_id) as { id: number; spec_folder?: string | null } | undefined;
811: 
812:   if (row) {
813:     const deleted = delete_memory_from_database(database, row.id);
814:     if (deleted) {
815:       // Self-record DELETE history only after the delete succeeded.
816:       try {
817:         recordHistory(row.id, 'DELETE', file_path ?? null, null, 'mcp:delete_by_path', row.spec_folder ?? spec_folder);
818:       } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
819:         // BEST-EFFORT HISTORY WRITES MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL DELETE.
820:       }
821:     }
822:     return deleted;
823:   }
824:   return false;
825: }
826: 
827: /**
828:  * Deletes multiple memories in a single transaction.
829:  * @param memory_ids - The memory identifiers to delete.
830:  * @returns Counts for deleted and failed items.
831:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} When one or more deletes fail and the transaction is rolled back.
832:  * @example
833:  * ```ts
834:  * const outcome = delete_memories([1, 2, 3]);
835:  * ```
836:  */
837: export function delete_memories(
838:   memory_ids: number[],
839:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
840: ): { deleted: number; failed: number } {
841:   if (!memory_ids || memory_ids.length === 0) {
842:     return { deleted: 0, failed: 0 };
843:   }
844:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
845:   let deleted = 0;
846:   let failed = 0;
847:   const deletedIds: number[] = [];
848: 
849:   const specFolderById = new Map<number, string | null>();
850:   for (const id of memory_ids) {
851:     if (!Number.isInteger(id) || id <= 0) {
852:       continue;
853:     }
854:     try {
855:       const row = database.prepare('SELECT spec_folder FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').get(id) as { spec_folder?: string | null } | undefined;
856:       specFolderById.set(id, row?.spec_folder ?? null);
857:     } catch (_error: unknown) {
858:       specFolderById.set(id, null);
859:     }
860:   }
861: 
862:   const delete_transaction = database.transaction(() => {
863:     let transactionDeleted = 0;
864:     let transactionFailed = 0;
865:     const failed_ids: number[] = [];
866: 
867:     for (const id of memory_ids) {
868:       try {
869:         if (sqlite_vec) {
870:           try {
871:             deleteActiveVectorPayload(database, BigInt(id));
872:           } catch (vec_error: unknown) {
873:             console.warn(`[VectorIndex] Failed to delete vector for memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(vec_error)}`);
874:           }
875:         }
876: 
877:         deleteAncillaryMemoryRows(database, id);
878:         const result = database.prepare('DELETE FROM memory_index WHERE id = ?').run(id);
879:         if (result.changes > 0) {
880:           // Memory_history rows are intentionally preserved after deletion
881:           // So DELETE audit events recorded here persist as audit trail.
882:           try {
883:             recordHistory(id, 'DELETE', null, null, 'mcp:delete_memories', specFolderById.get(id) ?? null);
884:           } catch (_histErr: unknown) {
885:             // BEST-EFFORT HISTORY WRITES MUST NOT MASK A SUCCESSFUL DELETE.
886:           }
887:           transactionDeleted++;
888:           deletedIds.push(id);
889:         } else {
890:           transactionFailed++;
891:           failed_ids.push(id);
892:         }
893:       } catch (e: unknown) {
894:         console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to delete memory ${id}: ${get_error_message(e)}`);
895:         transactionFailed++;
896:         failed_ids.push(id);
897:       }
898:     }
899: 
900:     if (failed_ids.length > 0) {
901:       throw new VectorIndexError(
902:         `Failed to delete memories: ${failed_ids.join(', ')}. Transaction rolled back.`,
903:         VectorIndexErrorCode.MUTATION_FAILED,
904:       );
905:     }
906: 
907:     return {
908:       deleted: transactionDeleted,
909:       failed: transactionFailed,
910:     };
911:   });
912: 
913:   try {
914:     const outcome = delete_transaction();
915:     deleted = outcome.deleted;
916:     failed = outcome.failed;
917:     if (deleted > 0) {
918:       clear_constitutional_cache();
919:       clear_search_cache();
920:       // Invalidate entity-density cache so deleted memories' tokens are evicted
921:       // immediately rather than persisting for up to the 60s TTL. Best-effort.
922:       try {
923:         invalidateEntityDensityCache();
924:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
925:         // Cache invalidation is best-effort; do not mask a successful delete.
926:       }
927:       try {
928:         if (bm25Index.isBm25Enabled()) {
929:           const bm25 = bm25Index.getIndex();
930:           for (const id of deletedIds) {
931:             bm25.removeDocument(String(id));
932:           }
933:         }
934:       } catch (_error: unknown) {
935:         // BM25 cleanup is best-effort for bulk deletes as well.
936:       }
937:     }
938:   } catch (e: unknown) {
939:     console.warn(`[vector-index] delete_memories transaction error: ${get_error_message(e)}`);
940:     deleted = 0;
941:     failed = memory_ids.length;
942:   }
943: 
944:   return { deleted, failed };
945: }
946: 
947: // Valid statuses: 'pending', 'success', 'failed', 'retry', 'partial'
948: /**
949:  * Updates the embedding status for a memory.
950:  * @param id - The memory identifier.
951:  * @param status - The new embedding status.
952:  * @returns True when the status was updated.
953:  */
954: export function update_embedding_status(
955:   id: number,
956:   status: string,
957:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
958: ): boolean {
959:   const valid_statuses = ['pending', 'success', 'failed', 'retry', 'partial'];
960:   if (!valid_statuses.includes(status)) {
961:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Invalid embedding status: ${status}`);
962:     return false;
963:   }
964: 
965:   try {
966:     const result = database.prepare(`
967:       UPDATE memory_index
968:       SET embedding_status = ?, updated_at = datetime('now')
969:       WHERE id = ?
970:     `).run(status, id);
971: 
972:     return result.changes > 0;
973:   } catch (error: unknown) {
974:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to update embedding status for ${id}: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
975:     return false;
976:   }
977: }
978: 
979: /**
980:  * Updates the confidence value for a memory.
981:  * @param memory_id - The memory identifier.
982:  * @param confidence - The confidence value to store.
983:  * @returns True when the confidence was updated.
984:  */
985: export function update_confidence(
986:   memory_id: number,
987:   confidence: number,
988:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
989: ): boolean {
990:   if (typeof confidence !== 'number' || confidence < 0 || confidence > 1) {
991:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Invalid confidence value: ${confidence}`);
992:     return false;
993:   }
994: 
995:   try {
996:     const result = database.prepare(`
997:       UPDATE memory_index
998:       SET confidence = ?
999:       WHERE id = ?
1000:     `).run(confidence, memory_id);
1001: 
1002:     return result.changes > 0;
1003:   } catch (error: unknown) {
1004:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to update confidence for ${memory_id}: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
1005:     return false;
1006:   }
1007: }
1008: 
1009: // Exported so the store-port clear() can resolve and purge the active per-dim
1010: // vector shard, mirroring the per-record delete path.
1011: export { activeDimVectorSource };

54: const logger = createLogger('VectorIndex');

56: type RelatedMemoryLink = { id: number; similarity: number };
57: type UsageStatsOptions = { sortBy?: string; order?: string; limit?: number };
58: type CleanupOptions = {

68:   embeddingColumn: string;

71: export interface KnnQueryShapeBenchmarkResult {
72:   readonly corpusSize: number;
73:   readonly iterations: number;
74:   readonly scalarJoinAverageMs: number;
75:   readonly matchAverageMs: number | null;
76:   readonly matchSupported: boolean;
77:   readonly matchImprovementPct: number | null;
78:   readonly decision: 'adopt_match' | 'keep_scalar_join';
79:   readonly reason: string;
80: }
81: 
82: const KNN_MATCH_ADOPTION_THRESHOLD_PCT = 20;
83: 
84: function appendSpecFolderScope(
85:   clauses: string[],
86:   params: unknown[],
87:   specFolder: string | null | undefined,
88:   column = 'm.spec_folder',
89: ): void {
90:   if (!specFolder) return;
91:   clauses.push(`(${column} = ? OR ${column} LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')`);
92:   params.push(specFolder, specFolderLikePattern(specFolder));
93: }

95: function tableExists(database: Database.Database, tableName: string): boolean {
96:   const row = database.prepare(`
97:     SELECT 1 AS found
98:     FROM active_vec.sqlite_master
99:     WHERE type = 'table' AND name = ?
100:     LIMIT 1
101:   `).get(tableName) as { found?: number } | undefined;
102:   return row?.found === 1;
103: }
104: 
105: function getActiveVectorSourceForQuery(database: Database.Database): VectorSource {
106:   const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
107:   const dimTableName = `vec_${active.dim}`;
108:   if (active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name && tableExists(database, dimTableName)) {
109:     return {
110:       dim: active.dim,
111:       tableName: activeVectorSchema(dimTableName),
112:       idColumn: 'id',
113:       embeddingColumn: 'vec',
114:     };
115:   }
116: 
117:   return {
118:     dim: get_embedding_dim(),
119:     tableName: activeVectorSchema('vec_memories'),
120:     idColumn: 'rowid',
121:     embeddingColumn: 'embedding',
122:   };
123: }
124: 
125: function elapsedMs(start: bigint): number {
126:   return Number(process.hrtime.bigint() - start) / 1_000_000;
127: }
128: 
129: function average(values: number[]): number {
130:   if (values.length === 0) return 0;
131:   return values.reduce((sum, value) => sum + value, 0) / values.length;
132: }
133: 
134: export function shouldAdoptMatchQueryShape(result: Pick<KnnQueryShapeBenchmarkResult, 'matchSupported' | 'matchImprovementPct'>): boolean {
135:   return result.matchSupported === true
136:     && typeof result.matchImprovementPct === 'number'
137:     && result.matchImprovementPct > KNN_MATCH_ADOPTION_THRESHOLD_PCT;
138: }
139: 
140: export function benchmarkKnnQueryShapes(
141:   query_embedding: EmbeddingInput,
142:   options: { iterations?: number; limit?: number } = {},
143:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
144: ): KnnQueryShapeBenchmarkResult {
145:   const vectorSource = getActiveVectorSourceForQuery(database);
146:   if (!query_embedding || query_embedding.length !== vectorSource.dim) {
147:     throw new VectorIndexError(
148:       `Invalid embedding dimension: expected ${vectorSource.dim}, got ${query_embedding?.length}`,
149:       VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
150:     );
151:   }
152: 
153:   const iterations = Math.max(1, Math.floor(options.iterations ?? 5));
154:   const limit = Math.max(1, Math.floor(options.limit ?? 20));
155:   const queryBuffer = to_embedding_buffer(query_embedding);
156:   const corpusSize = (database.prepare(`
157:     SELECT COUNT(*) AS count
158:     FROM memory_index m
159:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
160:     WHERE m.embedding_status = 'success'
161:   `).get() as { count: number }).count;
162: 
163:   const scalarSql = `
164:     SELECT m.id, vec_distance_cosine(v.${vectorSource.embeddingColumn}, ?) AS distance
165:     FROM memory_index m
166:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
167:     JOIN ${vectorSource.tableName} v ON m.id = v.${vectorSource.idColumn}
168:     WHERE m.embedding_status = 'success'
169:     ORDER BY distance ASC
170:     LIMIT ?
171:   `;
172:   const scalarStmt = database.prepare(scalarSql);
173:   scalarStmt.all(queryBuffer, limit);
174:   const scalarRuns: number[] = [];
175:   for (let i = 0; i < iterations; i += 1) {
176:     const start = process.hrtime.bigint();
177:     scalarStmt.all(queryBuffer, limit);
178:     scalarRuns.push(elapsedMs(start));
179:   }
180: 
181:   const matchTable = activeVectorSchema('vec_memories');
182:   const matchAvailable = tableExists(database, 'vec_memories');
183:   let matchAverageMs: number | null = null;
184:   let matchError: string | null = null;
185: 
186:   if (matchAvailable) {
187:     try {
188:       const matchSql = `
189:         WITH knn AS (
190:           SELECT rowid, distance
191:           FROM ${matchTable}
192:           WHERE embedding MATCH ? AND k = ?
193:         )
194:         SELECT m.id, knn.distance
195:         FROM knn
196:         JOIN memory_index m ON m.id = knn.rowid
197:         JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
198:         WHERE m.embedding_status = 'success'
199:         ORDER BY knn.distance ASC
200:         LIMIT ?
201:       `;
202:       const matchStmt = database.prepare(matchSql);
203:       matchStmt.all(queryBuffer, limit, limit);
204:       const matchRuns: number[] = [];
205:       for (let i = 0; i < iterations; i += 1) {
206:         const start = process.hrtime.bigint();
207:         matchStmt.all(queryBuffer, limit, limit);
208:         matchRuns.push(elapsedMs(start));
209:       }
210:       matchAverageMs = average(matchRuns);
211:     } catch (error: unknown) {
212:       matchError = get_error_message(error);
213:     }
214:   }
215: 
216:   const scalarJoinAverageMs = average(scalarRuns);
217:   const matchImprovementPct = matchAverageMs !== null && scalarJoinAverageMs > 0
218:     ? ((scalarJoinAverageMs - matchAverageMs) / scalarJoinAverageMs) * 100
219:     : null;
220:   const preliminary: Pick<KnnQueryShapeBenchmarkResult, 'matchSupported' | 'matchImprovementPct'> = {
221:     matchSupported: matchAverageMs !== null,
222:     matchImprovementPct,
223:   };
224:   const decision = shouldAdoptMatchQueryShape(preliminary) ? 'adopt_match' : 'keep_scalar_join';
225: 
226:   return {
227:     corpusSize,
228:     iterations,
229:     scalarJoinAverageMs,
230:     matchAverageMs,
231:     matchSupported: matchAverageMs !== null,
232:     matchImprovementPct,
233:     decision,
234:     reason: decision === 'adopt_match'
235:       ? `MATCH exceeded ${KNN_MATCH_ADOPTION_THRESHOLD_PCT}% threshold`
236:       : matchError
237:         ? `MATCH unavailable: ${matchError}`
238:         : `MATCH did not exceed ${KNN_MATCH_ADOPTION_THRESHOLD_PCT}% threshold`,
239:   };
240: }

243:    SIMPLE QUERIES
244: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/

246: /**
247:  * Gets one indexed memory by identifier.
248:  * @param id - The memory identifier.
249:  * @returns The matching memory row, if found.
250:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates store initialization failures from the vector index.
251:  * @example
252:  * ```ts
253:  * const memory = get_memory(42);
254:  * ```
255:  */
256: export function get_memory(id: number, database: Database.Database = initialize_db()): MemoryRow | null {
257:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
258:   const row = stmts.get_by_id.get(id);

260:   if (row) {
261:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
262:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';

268: /**
269:  * Gets indexed memories for a spec folder.

271:  * @returns The memory rows for the folder.

274:   spec_folder: string,
275:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
276: ): MemoryRow[] {
277:   const rows = database.prepare(`
278:     SELECT m.*
279:     FROM memory_index m
280:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
281:     WHERE (m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')
282:     ORDER BY m.created_at DESC
283:   `).all(spec_folder, specFolderLikePattern(spec_folder)) as MemoryRow[];
284: 
285:   return rows.map((row: MemoryRow) => {
286:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
287:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';

292: /**
293:  * Gets the total number of indexed memories.
294:  * @returns The total memory count.
295:  */

297:   const stmts = init_prepared_statements(database);
298:   const result = stmts.count_all.get();

301: 
302: /**
303:  * Gets memory counts grouped by embedding status.
304:  * @returns The counts for each embedding status.
305:  */
306: export function get_status_counts(
307:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
308: ): { pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
309:   const rows = database.prepare(`
310:     SELECT m.embedding_status, COUNT(*) as count
311:     FROM memory_index m
312:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
313:     GROUP BY m.embedding_status
314:   `).all();
315: 
316:   // Include 'partial' status in counts.
317:   const counts = { pending: 0, success: 0, failed: 0, retry: 0, partial: 0 };
318:   for (const row of rows as Array<{ embedding_status: keyof typeof counts; count: number }>) {
319:     if (row.embedding_status in counts) {
320:       counts[row.embedding_status] = row.count;
321:     }
322:   }
323: 
324:   return counts;
325: }
326: 
327: /**
328:  * Gets summary counts for indexed memories.
329:  * @returns The aggregate memory statistics.
330:  */
331: export function get_stats(
332:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
333: ): { total: number; pending: number; success: number; failed: number; retry: number; partial: number } {
334:   const counts = get_status_counts(database);
335:   const total = counts.pending + counts.success + counts.failed + counts.retry + counts.partial;
336: 
337:   return {
338:     total,
339:     ...counts
340:   };

342: 
343: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
344:    VECTOR SEARCH
345: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
346: 
347: /**
348:  * Searches indexed memories by vector similarity.
349:  * @param query_embedding - The query embedding to search with.
350:  * @param options - Search options.
351:  * @returns The matching memory rows.
352:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates store initialization failures from the vector index.
353:  * @example
354:  * ```ts
355:  * const rows = vector_search(queryEmbedding, { limit: 5, specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
356:  * ```
357:  */
358: export function vector_search(
359:   query_embedding: EmbeddingInput,
360:   options: VectorSearchOptions = {},
361:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
362: ): MemoryRow[] {
363:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
364:   if (!sqlite_vec) {
365:     console.warn('[vector-index] Vector search unavailable - sqlite-vec not loaded');
366:     return [];
367:   }
368: 
369:   const {
370:     limit = 10,
371:     specFolder = null,
372:     minSimilarity = 0,
373:     useDecay = true,
374:     tier = null,
375:     contextType = null,
376:     includeConstitutional = true,
377:     includeArchived = false
378:   } = options;
379: 
380:   const vectorSource = getActiveVectorSourceForQuery(database);
381:   if (!query_embedding || query_embedding.length !== vectorSource.dim) {
382:     throw new VectorIndexError(
383:       `Invalid embedding dimension: expected ${vectorSource.dim}, got ${query_embedding?.length}`,
384:       VectorIndexErrorCode.EMBEDDING_VALIDATION,
385:     );
386:   }
387: 
388:   const query_buffer = to_embedding_buffer(query_embedding);
389:   const max_distance = 2 * (1 - minSimilarity / 100);
390: 
391:   // FSRS-preferred decay with half-life fallback
392:   const decay_expr = useDecay
393:     ? `CASE
394:          WHEN m.is_pinned = 1 THEN m.importance_weight
395:          WHEN m.last_review IS NOT NULL AND m.review_count > 0 THEN
396:            m.importance_weight * POWER(
397:              1.0 + (19.0/81.0) * (julianday('now') - julianday(m.last_review)) / COALESCE(NULLIF(m.stability, 0), 1.0),
398:              -0.5
399:            )
400:          ELSE m.importance_weight * POWER(0.5, (julianday('now') - julianday(m.updated_at)) / COALESCE(NULLIF(m.decay_half_life_days, 0), 90.0))
401:        END`
402:     : 'm.importance_weight';
403: 
404:   let constitutional_results: MemoryRow[] = [];
405: 
406:   if (includeConstitutional && tier !== 'constitutional') {
407:     constitutional_results = get_constitutional_memories(database, specFolder, includeArchived);
408:   }
409: 
410:   const where_clauses = ['m.embedding_status = \'success\''];
411:   const params: unknown[] = [query_buffer];
412: 
413:   where_clauses.push('(m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime(\'now\'))');
414: 
415:   if (tier === 'deprecated') {
416:     where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
417:     params.push('deprecated');
418:   } else if (tier === 'constitutional') {
419:     where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
420:     params.push('constitutional');
421:   } else if (tier) {
422:     where_clauses.push('m.importance_tier = ?');
423:     params.push(tier);
424:   } else if (isArchivedVectorInclusionEnabled()) {
425:     // Cold/archived rows admitted to the projection (option A) must not be re-excluded
426:     // here; still keep constitutional on its own injected path.
427:     where_clauses.push('(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier != \'constitutional\')');
428:   } else {
429:     where_clauses.push('(m.importance_tier IS NULL OR m.importance_tier NOT IN (\'deprecated\', \'constitutional\'))');
430:   }
431: 
432:   appendSpecFolderScope(where_clauses, params, specFolder);
433: 
434:   if (contextType) {
435:     where_clauses.push('m.context_type = ?');
436:     params.push(contextType);
437:   }
438: 
439:   // If constitutional results already satisfy limit, return them directly.
440:   if (constitutional_results.length >= limit) {
441:     return constitutional_results.slice(0, limit);
442:   }
443:   const adjusted_limit = Math.max(1, limit - constitutional_results.length);
444:   params.push(max_distance, adjusted_limit);
445: 
446:   const sql = `
447:     SELECT sub.*,
448:            ROUND((1 - sub.distance / 2) * 100, 2) as similarity
449:     FROM (
450:       SELECT m.*, vec_distance_cosine(v.${vectorSource.embeddingColumn}, ?) as distance,
451:              ${decay_expr} as effective_importance
452:       FROM memory_index m
453:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
454:       JOIN ${vectorSource.tableName} v ON m.id = v.${vectorSource.idColumn}
455:       WHERE ${where_clauses.join(' AND ')}
456:     ) sub
457:     WHERE sub.distance <= ?
458:     ORDER BY (sub.distance - (sub.effective_importance * 0.1)) ASC
459:     LIMIT ?
460:   `;
461: 
462:   const rows = database.prepare(sql).all(...params);
463: 
464:   const regular_results = (rows as MemoryRow[]).map((row: MemoryRow) => {
465:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
466:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
467:     return row;
468:   });
469: 
470:   return [...constitutional_results, ...regular_results];
471: }
472: 
473: /**
474:  * Gets constitutional memories for prompt assembly.
475:  * @param options - Retrieval options.
476:  * @returns The constitutional memory rows.
477:  */
478: export function get_constitutional_memories_public(
479:   options: { specFolder?: string | null; maxTokens?: number; includeArchived?: boolean } = {},
480:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
481: ): MemoryRow[] {
482:   const { specFolder = null, maxTokens = 2000, includeArchived = false } = options;
483: 
484:   let results = get_constitutional_memories(database, specFolder, includeArchived);
485: 
486:   const TOKENS_PER_MEMORY = 100;
487:   const max_count = Math.floor(maxTokens / TOKENS_PER_MEMORY);
488:   if (results.length > max_count) {
489:     results = results.slice(0, max_count);
490:   }
491: 
492:   return results;
493: }
494: 
495: /**
496:  * Searches indexed memories with multiple concept embeddings.
497:  * @param concept_embeddings - The concept embeddings to search with.
498:  * @param options - Search options.
499:  * @returns The matching memory rows.
500:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} When concept count or embedding dimensions are invalid, or when store initialization fails.
501:  * @example
502:  * ```ts
503:  * const rows = multi_concept_search([embA, embB], { limit: 8, specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
504:  * ```
505:  */
506: export function multi_concept_search(
507:   concept_embeddings: EmbeddingInput[],
508:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string | null; minSimilarity?: number } = {}
509: ): MemoryRow[] {
510:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
511:   if (!sqlite_vec) {
512:     console.warn('[vector-index] Multi-concept search unavailable - sqlite-vec not loaded');
513:     return [];
514:   }
515: 
516:   const database = initialize_db();
517: 
518:   const concepts = concept_embeddings;
519:   if (!Array.isArray(concepts) || concepts.length < 2 || concepts.length > 5) {
520:     throw new VectorIndexError('Multi-concept search requires 2-5 concepts', VectorIndexErrorCode.QUERY_FAILED);
521:   }
522: 
523:   const vectorSource = getActiveVectorSourceForQuery(database);
524:   for (const emb of concepts) {
525:     if (!emb || emb.length !== vectorSource.dim) {
526:       throw new VectorIndexError(
527:         `Invalid embedding dimension: expected ${vectorSource.dim}, got ${emb?.length}`,
528:         VectorIndexErrorCode.QUERY_FAILED,
529:       );
530:     }
531:   }
532: 
533:   const { limit = 10, specFolder = null, minSimilarity = 50 } = options;
534: 
535:   const concept_buffers = concepts.map(c => to_embedding_buffer(c));
536:   const max_distance = 2 * (1 - minSimilarity / 100);
537: 
538:   const distance_expressions = concept_buffers.map((_, i) =>
539:     `vec_distance_cosine(v.${vectorSource.embeddingColumn}, ?) as dist_${i}`
540:   ).join(', ');
541: 
542:   const distance_filters = concept_buffers.map((_, _i) =>
543:     `vec_distance_cosine(v.${vectorSource.embeddingColumn}, ?) <= ?`
544:   ).join(' AND ');
545: 
546:   const folder_filter = specFolder ? "AND (m.spec_folder = ? OR m.spec_folder LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')" : '';
547:   const similarity_select = concept_buffers.map((_, i) =>
548:     `ROUND((1 - sub.dist_${i} / 2) * 100, 2) as similarity_${i}`
549:   ).join(', ');
550: 
551:   const avg_distance_expr = concept_buffers.map((_, i) => `sub.dist_${i}`).join(' + ');
552: 
553:   const sql = `
554:     SELECT
555:       sub.*,
556:       ${similarity_select},
557:       (${avg_distance_expr}) / ${concepts.length} as avg_distance
558:     FROM (
559:       SELECT
560:         m.*,
561:         ${distance_expressions}
562:       FROM memory_index m
563:       JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
564:       JOIN ${vectorSource.tableName} v ON m.id = v.${vectorSource.idColumn}
565:       WHERE m.embedding_status = 'success'
566:         AND (m.expires_at IS NULL OR m.expires_at > datetime('now'))
567:         ${folder_filter}
568:         AND ${distance_filters}
569:     ) sub
570:     ORDER BY avg_distance ASC
571:     LIMIT ?
572:   `;
573: 
574:   const params = [
575:     ...concept_buffers,
576:     ...(specFolder ? [specFolder, specFolderLikePattern(specFolder)] : []),
577:     ...concept_buffers.flatMap(b => [b, max_distance]),
578:     limit
579:   ];
580: 
581:   const rows = database.prepare(sql).all(...params);
582: 
583:   return (rows as MemoryRow[]).map((row: MemoryRow) => {
584:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
585:     row.concept_similarities = concept_buffers.map((_, i) => Number(row[`similarity_${i}`] ?? 0));
586:     row.avg_similarity = (row.concept_similarities as number[]).reduce((a, b) => a + b, 0) / concepts.length;
587:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
588:     return row;
589:   });
590: }
591: 
592: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
593:    CONTENT EXTRACTION HELPERS
594: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
595: 
596: /**
597:  * Extracts a display title from indexed content.
598:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
599:  * @param filename - The optional source filename.
600:  * @returns The extracted title.
601:  */
602: export function extract_title(content: unknown, filename?: string): string {
603:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
604:     return filename ? path.basename(filename, path.extname(filename)) : 'Untitled';
605:   }
606: 
607:   const h1_match = content.match(/^#\s+(.+)$/m);
608:   if (h1_match && h1_match[1]) {
609:     return h1_match[1].trim();
610:   }
611: 
612:   const h2_match = content.match(/^##\s+(.+)$/m);
613:   if (h2_match && h2_match[1]) {
614:     return h2_match[1].trim();
615:   }
616: 
617:   const yaml_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^title:\s*(.+)$/m);
618:   if (yaml_match && yaml_match[1]) {
619:     return yaml_match[1].trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
620:   }
621: 
622:   const lines = content.split('\n').filter(l => l.trim().length > 0);
623:   if (lines.length > 0) {
624:     const first_line = lines[0].trim();
625:     return first_line.replace(/^#+\s*/, '').substring(0, 100);
626:   }
627: 
628:   return filename ? path.basename(filename, path.extname(filename)) : 'Untitled';
629: }
630: 
631: /**
632:  * Extracts a preview snippet from indexed content.
633:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
634:  * @param max_length - The maximum snippet length.
635:  * @returns The extracted snippet.
636:  */
637: export function extract_snippet(content: unknown, max_length = 200): string {
638:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
639:     return '';
640:   }
641: 
642:   const text = content.replace(/^---[\s\S]*?---\n*/m, '');
643:   const lines = text.split('\n');
644:   const snippet_lines: string[] = [];
645: 
646:   for (const line of lines) {
647:     const trimmed = line.trim();
648: 
649:     if (!trimmed || /^#+\s/.test(trimmed)) {
650:       if (snippet_lines.length > 0) {
651:         break;
652:       }
653:       continue;
654:     }
655: 
656:     if (/^[a-z_-]+:\s/i.test(trimmed) && snippet_lines.length === 0) {
657:       continue;
658:     }
659: 
660:     snippet_lines.push(trimmed);
661: 
662:     const current_length = snippet_lines.join(' ').length;
663:     if (current_length >= max_length) {
664:       break;
665:     }
666:   }
667: 
668:   let snippet = snippet_lines.join(' ');
669: 
670:   if (snippet.length > max_length) {
671:     snippet = snippet.substring(0, max_length);
672:     const last_space = snippet.lastIndexOf(' ');
673:     if (last_space > max_length * 0.7) {
674:       snippet = snippet.substring(0, last_space);
675:     }
676:     snippet += '...';
677:   }
678: 
679:   return snippet;
680: }
681: 
682: /**
683:  * Extracts tags from indexed content.
684:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
685:  * @returns The extracted tags.
686:  */
687: export function extract_tags(content: unknown): string[] {
688:   if (!content || typeof content !== 'string') {
689:     console.warn('[vector-index] extract_tags: invalid content type, expected string');
690:     return [];
691:   }
692: 
693:   const tags = new Set<string>();
694: 
695:   const yaml_tags_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^tags:\s*\[([^\]]+)\]/m);
696:   if (yaml_tags_match && yaml_tags_match[1]) {
697:     yaml_tags_match[1].split(',').forEach(tag => {
698:       const cleaned = tag.trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
699:       if (cleaned) tags.add(cleaned.toLowerCase());
700:     });
701:   }
702: 
703:   const yaml_list_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^tags:\s*\n((?:\s*-\s*.+\n?)+)/m);
704:   if (yaml_list_match && yaml_list_match[1]) {
705:     yaml_list_match[1].match(/-\s*(.+)/g)?.forEach(match => {
706:       const tag = match.replace(/^-\s*/, '').trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
707:       if (tag) tags.add(tag.toLowerCase());
708:     });
709:   }
710: 
711:   const hashtag_matches = content.match(/(?:^|\s)#([a-zA-Z][a-zA-Z0-9_-]*)/g);
712:   if (hashtag_matches) {
713:     hashtag_matches.forEach(match => {
714:       const tag = match.trim().replace(/^#/, '');
715:       if (tag && !tag.match(/^[0-9]+$/)) {
716:         tags.add(tag.toLowerCase());
717:       }
718:     });
719:   }
720: 
721:   return Array.from(tags);
722: }
723: 
724: /**
725:  * Extracts a relevant date from indexed content or path.
726:  * @param content - The content to inspect.
727:  * @param file_path - The optional source file path.
728:  * @returns The extracted date, if available.
729:  */
730: export function extract_date(content: unknown, file_path?: string): string | null {
731:   if (content && typeof content === 'string') {
732:     const date_match = content.match(/^---[\s\S]*?^date:\s*(.+)$/m);
733:     if (date_match && date_match[1]) {
734:       const date_str = date_match[1].trim().replace(/^["']|["']$/g, '');
735:       try {
736:         const parsed = new Date(date_str);
737:         if (!isNaN(parsed.getTime())) {
738:           return parsed.toISOString().split('T')[0];
739:         }
740:       } catch (_e: unknown) {
741:         console.warn('[vector-index-queries] Date parsing failed', { error: _e instanceof Error ? _e.message : String(_e) });
742:       }
743:     }
744:   }
745: 
746:   if (file_path) {
747:     const filename = path.basename(file_path);
748: 
749:     const iso_match = filename.match(/(\d{4}-\d{2}-\d{2})/);
750:     if (iso_match) {
751:       return iso_match[1];
752:     }
753: 
754:     const ddmmyy_match = filename.match(/(\d{2})-(\d{2})-(\d{2})/);
755:     if (ddmmyy_match) {
756:       const [, day, month, year] = ddmmyy_match;
757:       const full_year = parseInt(year) > 50 ? `19${year}` : `20${year}`;
758:       return `${full_year}-${month}-${day}`;
759:     }
760:   }
761: 
762:   return null;
763: }
764: 
765: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
766:    EMBEDDING GENERATION WRAPPER
767: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
768: 
769: /**
770:  * Generates an embedding for a search query.
771:  * @param query - The search query.
772:  * @returns A promise that resolves to the embedding, if generated.
773:  */
774: export async function generate_query_embedding(query: string): Promise<Float32Array | null> {
775:   if (!query || typeof query !== 'string' || query.trim().length === 0) {
776:     console.warn('[vector-index] Empty query provided for embedding');
777:     return null;
778:   }
779: 
780:   try {
781:     const database = initialize_db();
782:     const active = getActiveEmbedder(database);
783:     const trimmedQuery = query.trim();
784:     const modelId = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
785:       ? active.name
786:       : embeddingsProvider.getModelName();
787:     const embeddingDim = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
788:       ? active.dim
789:       : embeddingsProvider.getEmbeddingDimension();
790:     const profileKey = getActiveEmbeddingProfileKey(database, modelId, embeddingDim);
791:     const contentHash = computeContentHash(trimmedQuery);
792:     const cached = lookupEmbedding(database, contentHash, modelId, embeddingDim, {
793:       profileKey,
794:       inputKind: 'query',
795:     });
796:     if (cached) {
797:       return new Float32Array(
798:         cached.buffer,
799:         cached.byteOffset,
800:         Math.floor(cached.byteLength / Float32Array.BYTES_PER_ELEMENT),
801:       );
802:     }
803: 
804:     const provider = active.name !== DEFAULT_ACTIVE_EMBEDDER.name
805:       ? active.provider ?? getManifest(active.name)?.backend ?? 'ollama'
806:       : getStartupEmbeddingProfile().provider;
807:     const adapter = getEmbedderAdapter(provider, modelId, embeddingDim);
808:     const activeAdapter = adapter as typeof adapter & {
809:       embed: (
810:         texts: ReadonlyArray<string>,
811:         options?: { inputType?: 'query' | 'document' },
812:       ) => Promise<Float32Array[]>;
813:     };
814:     const [activeEmbedding] = await activeAdapter.embed([trimmedQuery], { inputType: 'query' });
815:     const embedding: Float32Array | null = activeEmbedding ?? null;
816: 
817:     if (embedding) {
818:       storeEmbedding(
819:         database,
820:         contentHash,
821:         modelId,
822:         Buffer.from(embedding.buffer, embedding.byteOffset, embedding.byteLength),
823:         embedding.length,
824:         { profileKey, inputKind: 'query' },
825:       );
826:     }
827: 
828:     return embedding;
829:   } catch (error: unknown) {
830:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Query embedding failed: ${get_error_message(error)}`);
831:     return null;
832:   }
833: }
834: 
835: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
836:    KEYWORD SEARCH FALLBACK
837: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
838: 
839: /**
840:  * Searches indexed memories using keyword matching.
841:  * @param query - The search query.
842:  * @param options - Search options.
843:  * @returns The matching memory rows.
844:  */
845: export function keyword_search(
846:   query: string,
847:   options: { limit?: number; specFolder?: string | null } = {},
848:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
849: ): MemoryRow[] {
850:   const { limit = 20, specFolder = null } = options;
851: 
852:   if (!query || typeof query !== 'string') {
853:     console.warn('[vector-index] keyword_search: invalid query, expected non-empty string');
854:     return [];
855:   }
856: 
857:   const search_terms = query.toLowerCase().trim().split(/\s+/).filter(t => t.length >= 2);
858:   if (search_terms.length === 0) {
859:     console.warn('[vector-index] keyword_search: no valid search terms after tokenization');
860:     return [];
861:   }
862: 
863:   let where_clause = '1=1';
864:   const params: unknown[] = [];
865: 
866:   if (specFolder) {
867:     where_clause += " AND (spec_folder = ? OR spec_folder LIKE ? ESCAPE '\\')";
868:     params.push(specFolder, specFolderLikePattern(specFolder));
869:   }
870: 
871:   const sql = `
872:     SELECT m.* FROM memory_index m
873:     JOIN active_memory_projection p ON p.active_memory_id = m.id
874:     WHERE ${where_clause}
875:     ORDER BY m.importance_weight DESC, m.created_at DESC
876:   `;
877: 
878:   const rows = database.prepare(sql).all(...params);
879: 
880:   const scored = (rows as MemoryRow[]).map((row: MemoryRow) => {
881:     let score = 0;
882:     const searchable_text = [
883:       row.title || '',
884:       parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases).join(' '),
885:       row.spec_folder || '',
886:       row.file_path || ''
887:     ].join(' ').toLowerCase();
888: 
889:     for (const term of search_terms) {
890:       if (searchable_text.includes(term)) {
891:         score += 1;
892:         if ((row.title || '').toLowerCase().includes(term)) {
893:           score += 2;
894:         }
895:         if (parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases).join(' ').toLowerCase().includes(term)) {
896:           score += 1.5;
897:         }
898:       }
899:     }
900: 
901:     score *= (0.5 + (row.importance_weight ?? 0));
902:     return { ...row, keyword_score: score };
903:   });
904: 
905:   const filtered = scored
906:     .filter((row: MemoryRow) => Number(row.keyword_score ?? 0) > 0)
907:     .sort((a: MemoryRow, b: MemoryRow) => Number(b.keyword_score ?? 0) - Number(a.keyword_score ?? 0))
908:     .slice(0, limit);
909: 
910:   return filtered.map((row: MemoryRow) => {
911:     row.trigger_phrases = parse_trigger_phrases(row.trigger_phrases);
912:     row.isConstitutional = row.importance_tier === 'constitutional';
913:     return row;
914:   });
915: }
916: 
917: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
918:    ENRICHED VECTOR SEARCH
919: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
920: 
921: /**
922:  * Runs enriched vector search for a text query.
923:  * @param query - The search query.
924:  * @param limit - The maximum number of results to return.
925:  * @param options - Search options.
926:  * @returns A promise that resolves to enriched search results.
927:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} Propagates vector-store initialization failures from the underlying search pipeline.
928:  * @example
929:  * ```ts
930:  * const results = await vector_search_enriched('sqlite vec mismatch', 10, { specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
931:  * ```
932:  */
933: export async function vector_search_enriched(
934:   query: string,
935:   limit = 20,
936:   options: { specFolder?: string | null; minSimilarity?: number } = {},
937:   database: Database.Database = initialize_db(),
938: ): Promise<EnrichedSearchResult[]> {
939:   const start_time = Date.now();
940:   const { specFolder = null, minSimilarity = 30 } = options;
941: 
942:   const query_embedding = await generate_query_embedding(query);
943: 
944:   let raw_results;
945:   let search_method = 'vector';
946: 
947:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
948:   if (query_embedding && sqlite_vec) {
949:     raw_results = vector_search(query_embedding, {
950:       limit,
951:       specFolder,
952:       minSimilarity
953:     }, database);
954:   } else {
955:     console.warn('[vector-index] Falling back to keyword search');
956:     search_method = 'keyword';
957:     raw_results = keyword_search(query, { limit, specFolder }, database);
958:   }
959: 
960:   // HIGH-004 FIX: Read all files concurrently
961:   const file_contents = await Promise.all(
962:     raw_results.map((row: MemoryRow) => safe_read_file_async(row.file_path))
963:   );
964: 
965:   const enriched_results = raw_results.map((row: MemoryRow, i: number) => {
966:     const content = file_contents[i];
967:     const title = row.title || extract_title(content, row.file_path);
968:     const snippet = extract_snippet(content);
969:     const tags = extract_tags(content);
970:     const date = extract_date(content, row.file_path) || row.created_at?.split('T')[0] || null;
971: 
972:     const similarity = search_method === 'vector'
973:       ? (row.similarity || 0)
974:       : Math.min(100, (row.keyword_score || 0) * 20);
975: 
976:     return {
977:       rank: i + 1,
978:       similarity: Math.round(similarity * 100) / 100,
979:       title,
980:       specFolder: row.spec_folder,
981:       filePath: row.file_path,
982:       date,
983:       tags,
984:       snippet,
985:       id: row.id,
986:       importanceWeight: row.importance_weight ?? 0.5,
987:       searchMethod: search_method,
988:       isConstitutional: row.isConstitutional || row.importance_tier === 'constitutional'
989:     };
990:   });
991: 
992:   const elapsed = Date.now() - start_time;
993:   if (elapsed > 500) {
994:     console.warn(`[vector-index] Enriched search took ${elapsed}ms (target <500ms)`);
995:   }
996: 
997:   return enriched_results;
998: }
999: 
1000: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1001:    MULTI-CONCEPT ENRICHED SEARCH
1002: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
1003: 
1004: /**
1005:  * Runs enriched search across multiple concepts.
1006:  * @param concepts - The concept queries or embeddings.
1007:  * @param limit - The maximum number of results to return.
1008:  * @param options - Search options.
1009:  * @returns A promise that resolves to enriched search results.
1010:  * @throws {VectorIndexError} When concept validation fails or the vector search pipeline cannot execute.
1011:  * @example
1012:  * ```ts
1013:  * const results = await multi_concept_search_enriched(['sqlite', 'vector'], 10, { specFolder: 'specs/001-demo' });
1014:  * ```
1015:  */
1016: export async function multi_concept_search_enriched(
1017:   concepts: Array<string | EmbeddingInput>,
1018:   limit = 20,
1019:   options: { specFolder?: string | null; minSimilarity?: number } = {}
1020: ): Promise<EnrichedSearchResult[]> {
1021:   const start_time = Date.now();
1022: 
1023:   if (!Array.isArray(concepts) || concepts.length < 2 || concepts.length > 5) {
1024:     throw new VectorIndexError('Multi-concept search requires 2-5 concepts', VectorIndexErrorCode.QUERY_FAILED);
1025:   }
1026: 
1027:   const { specFolder = null, minSimilarity = 50 } = options;
1028: 
1029:   const concept_embeddings: EmbeddingInput[] = [];
1030:   for (const concept of concepts) {
1031:     if (typeof concept === 'string') {
1032:       const embedding = await generate_query_embedding(concept);
1033:       if (!embedding) {
1034:         console.warn(`[vector-index] Failed to embed concept: "${concept}"`);
1035:         return await multi_concept_keyword_search(concepts.filter(c => typeof c === 'string'), limit, options);
1036:       }
1037:       concept_embeddings.push(embedding);
1038:     } else {
1039:       concept_embeddings.push(concept);
1040:     }
1041:   }
1042: 
1043:   const sqlite_vec = get_sqlite_vec_available();
1044:   if (!sqlite_vec) {
1045:     console.warn('[vector-index] Falling back to keyword multi-concept search');
1046:     return await multi_concept_keyword_search(concepts.filter(c => typeof c === 'string'), limit, options);
1047:   }
1048: 
1049:   const raw_results = multi_concept_search(concept_embeddings, { limit, specFolder, minSimilarity });
1050: 
1051:   const file_contents = await Promise.all(
1052:     raw_results.map((row: MemoryRow) => safe_read_file_async(row.file_path))
1053:   );
1054: 
1055:   const enriched_results = raw_results.map((row: MemoryRow, i: number) => {
1056:     const content = file_contents[i];
1057:     const title = row.title || extract_title(content, row.file_path);
1058:     const snippet = extract_snippet(content);
1059:     const tags = extract_tags(content);
1060:     const date = extract_date(content, row.file_path) || row.created_at?.split('T')[0] || null;
1061: 
1062:     return {
1063:       rank: i + 1,
1064:       avgSimilarity: Math.round((row.avg_similarity || 0) * 100) / 100,
1065:       conceptSimilarities: (row.concept_similarities as number[] | undefined) || [],
1066:       title,

1076:   });

1086: /**

1093: export async function multi_concept_keyword_search(
1094:   concepts: string[],

1097: ): Promise<EnrichedSearchResult[]> {
1098:   const { specFolder = null } = options;
1099: 
1100:   if (!concepts.length) {
1101:     console.warn('[vector-index] multi_concept_keyword_search: empty concepts array');
1102:     return [];
1103:   }
1104: 
1105:   const concept_results = concepts.map((concept: string) =>
1106:     keyword_search(concept, { limit: 100, specFolder })
1107:   );
1108: 
1109:   const id_counts = new Map<number, number>();
1110:   const id_to_row = new Map<number, MemoryRow>();
1111: 
1112:   for (const results of concept_results) {
1113:     for (const row of results) {
1114:       const count = id_counts.get(row.id) || 0;
1115:       id_counts.set(row.id, count + 1);
1116:       if (!id_to_row.has(row.id)) {
1117:         id_to_row.set(row.id, row);
1118:       }
1119:     }
1120:   }
1121: 
1122:   const matching_ids: number[] = [];
1123:   for (const [id, count] of id_counts) {
1124:     if (count === concepts.length) {
1125:       matching_ids.push(id);
1126:     }
1127:   }
1128: 
1129:   const limited_ids = matching_ids.slice(0, limit);
1130:   const rows = limited_ids.map(id => id_to_row.get(id)).filter((row): row is MemoryRow => Boolean(row));
1131: 
1132:   const file_contents = await Promise.all(
1133:     rows.map(row => safe_read_file_async(row.file_path))
1134:   );
1135: 
1136:   const enriched_results = rows.map((row, i) => {
1137:     const content = file_contents[i];

1139:     const snippet = extract_snippet(content);

1142: 
1143:     return {
1144:       rank: i + 1,
1145:       avgSimilarity: Math.min(100, (row.keyword_score || 1) * 15),
1146:       conceptSimilarities: concepts.map(() => row.keyword_score || 1),
1147:       title,
1148:       specFolder: row.spec_folder,
1149:       filePath: row.file_path,
1150:       date,
1151:       tags,
1152:       snippet,
1153:       id: row.id,
1154:       importanceWeight: row.importance_weight ?? 0.5,
1155:       searchMethod: 'keyword',
1156:       isConstitutional: row.importance_tier === 'constitutional'
1157:     };
1158:   });
1159: 
1160:   return enriched_results;
1161: }

1163: /**

1168: export function parse_quoted_terms(query: string): string[] {

1171:     return [];

1173: 
1174:   const quoted: string[] = [];

1176:   let match;
1177:

1181:     }
1182:   }

1187: /* ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
1188:    SMART RANKING AND DIVERSITY
1189: ----------------------------------------------------------------*/
1190: 
1191: // Weights read from config instead of hardcoded
1192: /**
1193:  * Applies smart ranking weights to enriched results.
1194:  * @param results - The results to rank.
1195:  * @returns The ranked results.
1196:  */
1197: export function apply_smart_ranking(results: EnrichedSearchResult[]): EnrichedSearchResult[] {
1198:   if (!results || results.length === 0) return results;
1199: 
1200:   const recency_weight = search_weights.smartRanking?.recencyWeight || 0.3;
1201:   const access_weight = search_weights.smartRanking?.accessWeight || 0.2;
1202:   const relevance_weight = search_weights.smartRanking?.relevanceWeight || 0.5;
1203: 
1204:   const now = Date.now();
1205:   const week_ms = 7 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
1206:   const month_ms = 30 * 24 * 60 * 60 * 1000;
1207: 
1208:   return results.map((r: EnrichedSearchResult) => {
1209:     const created_at = r.created_at ? new Date(r.created_at).getTime() : now;
1210:     const age = now - created_at;
1211:     let recency_factor;
1212:     if (age < week_ms) {
1213:       recency_factor = 1.0;
1214:     } else if (age < month_ms) {
1215:       recency_factor = 0.8;
1216:     } else {
1217:       recency_factor = 0.5;
1218:     }
1219: 
1220:     const usage_factor = Math.min(1.0, (r.access_count || 0) / 10);
1221:     const similarity_factor = (r.similarity || 0) / 100;
1222: 
1223:     r.smartScore = (similarity_factor * relevance_weight) + (recency_factor * recency_weight) + (usage_factor * access_weight);
1224:     r.smartScore = Math.round(r.smartScore * 100) / 100;
1225: 
1226:     return r;
1227:   }).sort((a, b) => Number(b.smartScore ?? 0) - Number(a.smartScore ?? 0));
1228: }
1229: 
1230: // Apply diversity filtering using MMR (Maximal Marginal Relevance)
1231: /**
1232:  * Applies diversity reordering to enriched results.
1233:  * @param results - The results to diversify.
1234:  * @param diversity_factor - The diversity weight to apply.
1235:  * @returns The diversified results.
1236:  */
1237: export function apply_diversity(results: EnrichedSearchResult[], diversity_factor = 0.3): EnrichedSearchResult[] {
1238:   if (!results || results.length <= 3) return results;
1239: 
1240:   const selected = [results[0]];
1241:   const remaining = [...results.slice(1)];
1242: 
1243:   while (selected.length < results.length && remaining.length > 0) {
1244:     let best_idx = 0;
1245:     let best_score = -Infinity;
1246: 
1247:     for (let i = 0; i < remaining.length; i++) {
1248:       const candidate = remaining[i];
1249:       const relevance = candidate.smartScore || ((candidate.similarity ?? 0) / 100) || 0;
1250: 
1251:       let max_similarity_to_selected = 0;
1252:       for (const sel of selected) {
1253:         if (sel.specFolder === candidate.specFolder || sel.spec_folder === candidate.spec_folder) {
1254:           max_similarity_to_selected = Math.max(max_similarity_to_selected, 0.8);
1255:         }
1256:         if (sel.date === candidate.date) {
1257:           max_similarity_to_selected = Math.max(max_similarity_to_selected, 0.5);
1258:         }
1259:       }
1260: 
1261:       const mmr_score = relevance - (diversity_factor * max_similarity_to_selected);
1262: 
1263:       if (mmr_score > best_score) {
1264:         best_score = mmr_score;
1265:         best_idx = i;
1266:       }
1267:     }
1268: 
1269:     selected.push(remaining.splice(best_idx, 1)[0]);
1270:   }
1271: 
1272:   return selected;
1273: }

25:    * Higher = better lexical match. Not directly comparable to vector similarity

64: type Bm25Engine = typeof BM25_ENGINE_VALUES[number];

143: /**

146:  * @returns `true` when BM25 indexing/search is enabled for the current process.
147:  * @example
